name: comment_form
description: Outputs a complete commenting form for use within a template. Most strings and form fields may be controlled through the `$args` array passed into the function, while you may also choose to use the {@see 'comment_form_default_fields'} filter to modify the array of default fields if you'd just like to add a new one or remove a single field. All fields are also individually passed through a filter of the {@see 'comment_form_field_$name'} where `$name` is the key used in the array of fields.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Default arguments and form fields to override. @type array $fields { Default comment fields, filterable by default via the {@see 'comment_form_default_fields'} hook. @type string $author Comment author field HTML. @type string $email Comment author email field HTML. @type string $url Comment author URL field HTML. @type string $cookies Comment cookie opt-in field HTML. } @type string $comment_field The comment textarea field HTML. @type string $must_log_in HTML element for a 'must be logged in to comment' message. @type string $logged_in_as The HTML for the 'logged in as [user]' message, the Edit profile link, and the Log out link. @type string $comment_notes_before HTML element for a message displayed before the comment fields if the user is not logged in. Default 'Your email address will not be published.'. @type string $comment_notes_after HTML element for a message displayed after the textarea field. @type string $action The comment form element action attribute. Default '/wp-comments-post.php'. @type bool $novalidate Whether the novalidate attribute is added to the comment form. Default false. @type string $id_form The comment form element id attribute. Default 'commentform'. @type string $id_submit The comment submit element id attribute. Default 'submit'. @type string $class_container The comment form container class attribute. Default 'comment-respond'. @type string $class_form The comment form element class attribute. Default 'comment-form'. @type string $class_submit The comment submit element class attribute. Default 'submit'. @type string $name_submit The comment submit element name attribute. Default 'submit'. @type string $title_reply The translatable 'reply' button label. Default 'Leave a Reply'. @type string $title_reply_to The translatable 'reply-to' button label. Default 'Leave a Reply to %s', where %s is the author of the comment being replied to. @type string $title_reply_before HTML displayed before the comment form title. Default: ' '. @type string $title_reply_after HTML displayed after the comment form title. Default: ' '. @type string $cancel_reply_before HTML displayed before the cancel reply link. @type string $cancel_reply_after HTML displayed after the cancel reply link. @type string $cancel_reply_link The translatable 'cancel reply' button label. Default 'Cancel reply'. @type string $label_submit The translatable 'submit' button label. Default 'Post a comment'. @type string $submit_button HTML format for the Submit button. Default: ' '. @type string $submit_field HTML format for the markup surrounding the Submit button and comment hidden fields. Default: ' %1$s %2$s ', where %1$s is the submit button markup and %2$s is the comment hidden fields. @type string $format The comment form format. Default 'xhtml'. Accepts 'xhtml', 'html5'. }

name: comments_template
description: Loads the comment template specified in $file. Will not display the comments template if not on single post or page, or if the post does not have comments. Uses the WordPress database object to query for the comments. The comments are passed through the {@see 'comments_array'} filter hook with the list of comments and the post ID respectively. The `$file` path is passed through a filter hook called {@see 'comments_template'}, which includes the template directory and $file combined. Tries the $filtered path first and if it fails it will require the default comment template from the default theme. If either does not exist, then the WordPress process will be halted. It is advised for that reason, that the default theme is not deleted. Will not try to get the comments if the post has none.

params.name: file
params.description: Optional. The file to load. Default '/comments.php'.

params.name: separate_comments

name: count_users
description: Counts number of users who have each of the user roles. Assumes there are neither duplicated nor orphaned capabilities meta_values. Assumes role names are unique phrases. Same assumption made by WP_User_Query::prepare_query() Using $strategy = 'time' this is CPU-intensive and should handle around 10^7 users. Using $strategy = 'memory' this is memory-intensive and should handle around 10^5 users, but see WP Bug #12257.

params.name: strategy
params.description: Optional. The computational strategy to use when counting the users. Accepts either 'time' or 'memory'. Default 'time'.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Optional. The site ID to count users for. Defaults to the current site.

returnDescription: { User counts. @type int $total_users Total number of users on the site. @type int[] $avail_roles Array of user counts keyed by user role. }

name: current_time
description: Retrieves the current time based on specified type. - The 'mysql' type will return the time in the format for MySQL DATETIME field. - The 'timestamp' or 'U' types will return the current timestamp or a sum of timestamp and timezone offset, depending on `$gmt`. - Other strings will be interpreted as PHP date formats (e.g. 'Y-m-d'). If `$gmt` is a truthy value then both types will use GMT time, otherwise the output is adjusted with the GMT offset for the site.

params.name: type
params.description: Type of time to retrieve. Accepts 'mysql', 'timestamp', 'U', or PHP date format string (e.g. 'Y-m-d').

params.name: gmt
params.description: Optional. Whether to use GMT timezone. Default false.

returnDescription: Integer if `$type` is 'timestamp' or 'U', string otherwise.

name: current_user_can
description: Returns whether the current user has the specified capability. This function also accepts an ID of an object to check against if the capability is a meta capability. Meta capabilities such as `edit_post` and `edit_user` are capabilities used by the `map_meta_cap()` function to map to primitive capabilities that a user or role has, such as `edit_posts` and `edit_others_posts`. Example usage: current_user_can( 'edit_posts' ); current_user_can( 'edit_post', $post->ID ); current_user_can( 'edit_post_meta', $post->ID, $meta_key ); While checking against particular roles in place of a capability is supported in part, this practice is discouraged as it may produce unreliable results. Note: Will always return true if the current user is a super admin, unless specifically denied.

params.name: capability
params.description: Capability name.

returnDescription: Whether the current user has the given capability. If `$capability` is a meta cap and `$object_id` is passed, whether the current user has the given meta capability for the given object.

name: current_user_can_for_blog
description: Returns whether the current user has the specified capability for a given site.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Site ID.

params.name: capability
params.description: Capability name.

returnDescription: Whether the user has the given capability.

name: current_user_can_for_site
description: Returns whether the current user has the specified capability for a given site. This function also accepts an ID of an object to check against if the capability is a meta capability. Meta capabilities such as `edit_post` and `edit_user` are capabilities used by the `map_meta_cap()` function to map to primitive capabilities that a user or role has, such as `edit_posts` and `edit_others_posts`. This function replaces the current_user_can_for_blog() function. Example usage: current_user_can_for_site( $site_id, 'edit_posts' ); current_user_can_for_site( $site_id, 'edit_post', $post->ID ); current_user_can_for_site( $site_id, 'edit_post_meta', $post->ID, $meta_key );

params.name: site_id
params.description: Site ID.

params.name: capability
params.description: Capability name.

returnDescription: Whether the user has the given capability.

name: date_i18n
description: Retrieves the date in localized format, based on a sum of Unix timestamp and timezone offset in seconds. If the locale specifies the locale month and weekday, then the locale will take over the format for the date. If it isn't, then the date format string will be used instead. Note that due to the way WP typically generates a sum of timestamp and offset with `strtotime()`, it implies offset added at a _current_ time, not at the time the timestamp represents. Storing such timestamps or calculating them differently will lead to invalid output.

params.name: format
params.description: Format to display the date.

params.name: timestamp_with_offset
params.description: Optional. A sum of Unix timestamp and timezone offset in seconds. Default false.

params.name: gmt
params.description: Optional. Whether to use GMT timezone. Only applies if timestamp is not provided. Default false.

returnDescription: The date, translated if locale specifies it.

params.name: execute

returnDescription: Strings containing the results of the various update queries.

name: deactivated_plugins_notice

name: dead_db
description: Loads custom DB error or display WordPress DB error. If a file exists in the wp-content directory named db-error.php, then it will be loaded instead of displaying the WordPress DB error. If it is not found, then the WordPress DB error will be displayed instead. The WordPress DB error sets the HTTP status header to 500 to try to prevent search engines from caching the message. Custom DB messages should do the same. This function was backported to WordPress 2.3.2, but originally was added in WordPress 2.5.0.

name: do_action
description: Calls the callback functions that have been added to an action hook. This function invokes all functions attached to action hook `$hook_name`. It is possible to create new action hooks by simply calling this function, specifying the name of the new hook using the `$hook_name` parameter. You can pass extra arguments to the hooks, much like you can with `apply_filters()`. Example usage: // The action callback function. function example_callback( $arg1, $arg2 ) { // (maybe) do something with the args. } add_action( 'example_action', 'example_callback', 10, 2 ); /* * Trigger the actions by calling the 'example_callback()' function * that's hooked onto `example_action` above. * * - 'example_action' is the action hook. * - $arg1 and $arg2 are the additional arguments passed to the callback. do_action( 'example_action', $arg1, $arg2 );

params.name: hook_name
params.description: The name of the action to be executed.

name: do_action_deprecated

params.name: version

params.name: replacement

params.name: message

name: doing_action
description: Returns whether or not an action hook is currently being processed. The function current_action() only returns the most recent action being executed. did_action() returns the number of times an action has been fired during the current request. This function allows detection for any action currently being executed (regardless of whether it's the most recent action to fire, in the case of hooks called from hook callbacks) to be verified.

params.name: hook_name
params.description: Optional. Action hook to check. Defaults to null, which checks if any action is currently being run.

returnDescription: Whether the action is currently in the stack.

name: doing_filter
description: Returns whether or not a filter hook is currently being processed. The function current_filter() only returns the most recent filter being executed. did_filter() returns the number of times a filter has been applied during the current request. This function allows detection for any filter currently being executed (regardless of whether it's the most recent filter to fire, in the case of hooks called from hook callbacks) to be verified.

params.name: hook_name
params.description: Optional. Filter hook to check. Defaults to null, which checks if any filter is currently being run.

returnDescription: Whether the filter is currently in the stack.

params.description: Optional. Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings. Default 'default'.

params.name: domain
params.description: Optional. Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings. Default 'default'.

returnDescription: Translated text.

params.name: text
params.description: Text to translate.

params.name: domain
params.description: Optional. Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings. Default 'default'.

name: esc_js
description: Escapes single quotes, `"`, ` `, `&`, and fixes line endings. Escapes text strings for echoing in JS. It is intended to be used for inline JS (in a tag attribute, for example `onclick="..."`). Note that the strings have to be in single quotes. The {@see 'js_escape'} filter is also applied here.

params.name: text
params.description: The text to be escaped.

returnDescription: Escaped text.

name: esc_sql
description: Escapes data for use in a MySQL query. Usually you should prepare queries using wpdb::prepare(). Sometimes, spot-escaping is required or useful. One example is preparing an array for use in an IN clause. NOTE: Since 4.8.3, '%' characters will be replaced with a placeholder string, this prevents certain SQLi attacks from taking place. This change in behavior may cause issues for code that expects the return value of esc_sql() to be usable for other purposes.

params.name: data
params.description: Unescaped data.

returnDescription: Escaped data, in the same type as supplied.

name: esc_textarea
description: Escaping for textarea values.

name: esc_url
description: Checks and cleans a URL. A number of characters are removed from the URL. If the URL is for displaying (the default behavior) ampersands are also replaced. The {@see 'clean_url'} filter is applied to the returned cleaned URL.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL to be cleaned.

params.name: protocols
params.description: Optional. An array of acceptable protocols. Defaults to return value of wp_allowed_protocols().

params.name: _context
params.description: Private. Use sanitize_url() for database usage.

returnDescription: The cleaned URL after the {@see 'clean_url'} filter is applied. An empty string is returned if `$url` specifies a protocol other than those in `$protocols`, or if `$url` contains an empty string.

name: esc_url_raw
description: Sanitizes a URL for database or redirect usage. This function is an alias for sanitize_url().

params.name: url
params.description: The URL to be cleaned.

params.name: protocols

name: export_wp
description: Generates the WXR export file for download. Default behavior is to export all content, however, note that post content will only be exported for post types with the `can_export` argument enabled. Any posts with the 'auto-draft' status will be skipped.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Arguments for generating the WXR export file for download. Default empty array. @type string $content Type of content to export. If set, only the post content of this post type will be exported. Accepts 'all', 'post', 'page', 'attachment', or a defined custom post. If an invalid custom post type is supplied, every post type for which `can_export` is enabled will be exported instead. If a valid custom post type is supplied but `can_export` is disabled, then 'posts' will be exported instead. When 'all' is supplied, only post types with `can_export` enabled will be exported. Default 'all'. @type string $author Author to export content for. Only used when `$content` is 'post', 'page', or 'attachment'. Accepts false (all) or a specific author ID. Default false (all). @type string $category Category (slug) to export content for. Used only when `$content` is 'post'. If set, only post content assigned to `$category` will be exported. Accepts false or a specific category slug. Default is false (all categories). @type string $start_date Start date to export content from. Expected date format is 'Y-m-d'. Used only when `$content` is 'post', 'page' or 'attachment'. Default false (since the beginning of time). @type string $end_date End date to export content to. Expected date format is 'Y-m-d'. Used only when `$content` is 'post', 'page' or 'attachment'. Default false (latest publish date). @type string $status Post status to export posts for. Used only when `$content` is 'post' or 'page'. Accepts false (all statuses except 'auto-draft'), or a specific status, i.e. 'publish', 'pending', 'draft', 'auto-draft', 'future', 'private', 'inherit', or 'trash'. Default false (all statuses except 'auto-draft'). }

params.name: attr
params.description: { Attributes of the gallery shortcode. @type string $order Order of the images in the gallery. Default 'ASC'. Accepts 'ASC', 'DESC'. @type string $orderby The field to use when ordering the images. Default 'menu_order ID'. Accepts any valid SQL ORDERBY statement. @type int $id Post ID. @type string $itemtag HTML tag to use for each image in the gallery. Default 'dl', or 'figure' when the theme registers HTML5 gallery support. @type string $icontag HTML tag to use for each image's icon. Default 'dt', or 'div' when the theme registers HTML5 gallery support. @type string $captiontag HTML tag to use for each image's caption. Default 'dd', or 'figcaption' when the theme registers HTML5 gallery support. @type int $columns Number of columns of images to display. Default 3. @type string|int[] $size Size of the images to display. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'thumbnail'. @type string $ids A comma-separated list of IDs of attachments to display. Default empty. @type string $include A comma-separated list of IDs of attachments to include. Default empty. @type string $exclude A comma-separated list of IDs of attachments to exclude. Default empty. @type string $link What to link each image to. Default empty (links to the attachment page). Accepts 'file', 'none'. }

returnDescription: HTML content to display gallery.

name: get_archives

name: get_archives_link
description: Retrieves archive link content based on predefined or custom code. The format can be one of four styles. The 'link' for head element, 'option' for use in the select element, 'html' for use in list (either ol or ul HTML elements). Custom content is also supported using the before and after parameters. The 'link' format uses the ` ` HTML element with the **archives** relationship. The before and after parameters are not used. The text parameter is used to describe the link. The 'option' format uses the option HTML element for use in select element. The value is the url parameter and the before and after parameters are used between the text description. The 'html' format, which is the default, uses the li HTML element for use in the list HTML elements. The before parameter is before the link and the after parameter is after the closing link. The custom format uses the before parameter before the link ('a' HTML element) and the after parameter after the closing link tag. If the above three values for the format are not used, then custom format is assumed.

params.name: url
params.description: URL to archive.

params.name: text
params.description: Archive text description.

params.name: format
params.description: Optional. Can be 'link', 'option', 'html', or custom. Default 'html'.

params.name: before
params.description: Optional. Content to prepend to the description. Default empty.

params.name: after
params.description: Optional. Content to append to the description. Default empty.

params.name: selected
params.description: Optional. Set to true if the current page is the selected archive page. Default false.

returnDescription: HTML link content for archive.

By default the path will go through the {@see 'get_attached_file'} filter, but passing `true` to the `$unfiltered` argument will return the file path unfiltered. The function works by retrieving the `_wp_attached_file` post meta value. This is a convenience function to prevent looking up the meta name and provide a mechanism for sending the attached filename through a filter.

params.name: attachment_id

params.name: unfiltered
params.description: Optional. Whether to skip the {@see 'get_attached_file'} filter. Default false.

returnDescription: The file path to where the attached file should be, false otherwise.

name: get_avatar
description: Retrieves the avatar ` ` tag for a user, email address, MD5 hash, comment, or post.

params.name: id_or_email
params.description: The avatar to retrieve. Accepts a user ID, Gravatar MD5 hash, user email, WP_User object, WP_Post object, or WP_Comment object.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Height and width of the avatar in pixels. Default 96.

params.name: default_value
params.description: URL for the default image or a default type. Accepts: - '404' (return a 404 instead of a default image) - 'retro' (a 8-bit arcade-style pixelated face) - 'robohash' (a robot) - 'monsterid' (a monster) - 'wavatar' (a cartoon face) - 'identicon' (the "quilt", a geometric pattern) - 'initials' (initials based avatar with background color) - 'color' (generated background color) - 'mystery', 'mm', or 'mysteryman' (The Oyster Man) - 'blank' (transparent GIF) - 'gravatar_default' (the Gravatar logo) Default is the value of the 'avatar_default' option, with a fallback of 'mystery'.

params.name: alt
params.description: Optional. Alternative text to use in the avatar image tag. Default empty.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Extra arguments to retrieve the avatar. @type int $height Display height of the avatar in pixels. Defaults to $size. @type int $width Display width of the avatar in pixels. Defaults to $size. @type bool $force_default Whether to always show the default image, never the Gravatar. Default false. @type string $rating What rating to display avatars up to. Accepts: - 'G' (suitable for all audiences) - 'PG' (possibly offensive, usually for audiences 13 and above) - 'R' (intended for adult audiences above 17) - 'X' (even more mature than above) Default is the value of the 'avatar_rating' option. @type string $scheme URL scheme to use. See set_url_scheme() for accepted values. Default null. @type array|string $class Array or string of additional classes to add to the img element. Default null. @type bool $force_display Whether to always show the avatar - ignores the show_avatars option. Default false. @type string $loading Value for the `loading` attribute. Default null. @type string $fetchpriority Value for the `fetchpriority` attribute. Default null. @type string $decoding Value for the `decoding` attribute. Default null. @type string $extra_attr HTML attributes to insert in the IMG element. Is not sanitized. Default empty. }

returnDescription: ` ` tag for the user's avatar. False on failure.

name: get_avatar_data
description: Retrieves default data about the avatar.

params.name: id_or_email
params.description: The avatar to retrieve. Accepts a user ID, Gravatar SHA-256 or MD5 hash, user email, WP_User object, WP_Post object, or WP_Comment object.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Arguments to use instead of the default arguments. @type int $size Height and width of the avatar in pixels. Default 96. @type int $height Display height of the avatar in pixels. Defaults to $size. @type int $width Display width of the avatar in pixels. Defaults to $size. @type string $default URL for the default image or a default type. Accepts: - '404' (return a 404 instead of a default image) - 'retro' (a 8-bit arcade-style pixelated face) - 'robohash' (a robot) - 'monsterid' (a monster) - 'wavatar' (a cartoon face) - 'identicon' (the "quilt", a geometric pattern) - 'initials' (initials based avatar with background color) - 'color' (generated background color) - 'mystery', 'mm', or 'mysteryman' (The Oyster Man) - 'blank' (transparent GIF) - 'gravatar_default' (the Gravatar logo) Default is the value of the 'avatar_default' option, with a fallback of 'mystery'. @type bool $force_default Whether to always show the default image, never the Gravatar. Default false. @type string $rating What rating to display avatars up to. Accepts: - 'G' (suitable for all audiences) - 'PG' (possibly offensive, usually for audiences 13 and above) - 'R' (intended for adult audiences above 17) - 'X' (even more mature than above) Default is the value of the 'avatar_rating' option. @type string $scheme URL scheme to use. See set_url_scheme() for accepted values. For Gravatars this setting is ignored and HTTPS is used to avoid unnecessary redirects. The setting is retained for systems using the {@see 'pre_get_avatar_data'} filter to customize avatars. Default null. @type array $processed_args When the function returns, the value will be the processed/sanitized $args plus a "found_avatar" guess. Pass as a reference. Default null. @type string $extra_attr HTML attributes to insert in the IMG element. Is not sanitized. Default empty. }

returnDescription: { Along with the arguments passed in `$args`, this will contain a couple of extra arguments. @type bool $found_avatar True if an avatar was found for this user, false or not set if none was found. @type string|false $url The URL of the avatar that was found, or false. }

name: get_avatar_url
description: Retrieves the avatar URL.

params.name: id_or_email
params.description: The avatar to retrieve a URL for. Accepts a user ID, Gravatar SHA-256 or MD5 hash, user email, WP_User object, WP_Post object, or WP_Comment object.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Arguments to use instead of the default arguments. @type int $size Height and width of the avatar in pixels. Default 96. @type string $default URL for the default image or a default type. Accepts: - '404' (return a 404 instead of a default image) - 'retro' (a 8-bit arcade-style pixelated face) - 'robohash' (a robot) - 'monsterid' (a monster) - 'wavatar' (a cartoon face) - 'identicon' (the "quilt", a geometric pattern) - 'initials' (initials based avatar with background color) - 'color' (generated background color) - 'mystery', 'mm', or 'mysteryman' (The Oyster Man) - 'blank' (transparent GIF) - 'gravatar_default' (the Gravatar logo) Default is the value of the 'avatar_default' option, with a fallback of 'mystery'. @type bool $force_default Whether to always show the default image, never the Gravatar. Default false. @type string $rating What rating to display avatars up to. Accepts: - 'G' (suitable for all audiences) - 'PG' (possibly offensive, usually for audiences 13 and above) - 'R' (intended for adult audiences above 17) - 'X' (even more mature than above) Default is the value of the 'avatar_rating' option. @type string $scheme URL scheme to use. See set_url_scheme() for accepted values. Default null. @type array $processed_args When the function returns, the value will be the processed/sanitized $args plus a "found_avatar" guess. Pass as a reference. Default null. }

returnDescription: The URL of the avatar on success, false on failure.

name: get_block_wrapper_attributes
description: Generates a string of attributes by applying to the current block being rendered all of the features that the block supports.

This is useful to check whether you need to install an option and is commonly used during installation of plugin options and to test whether upgrading is required. If the option was serialized then it will be unserialized when it is returned.

name: get_bloginfo
description: Retrieves information about the current site. Possible values for `$show` include: - 'name' - Site title (set in Settings > General) - 'description' - Site tagline (set in Settings > General) - 'wpurl' - The WordPress address (URL) (set in Settings > General) - 'url' - The Site address (URL) (set in Settings > General) - 'admin_email' - Admin email (set in Settings > General) - 'charset' - The "Encoding for pages and feeds" (set in Settings > Reading) - 'version' - The current WordPress version - 'html_type' - The Content-Type (default: "text/html"). Themes and plugins can override the default value using the {@see 'pre_option_html_type'} filter - 'text_direction' - The text direction determined by the site's language. is_rtl() should be used instead - 'language' - Language code for the current site - 'stylesheet_url' - URL to the stylesheet for the active theme. An active child theme will take precedence over this value - 'stylesheet_directory' - Directory path for the active theme. An active child theme will take precedence over this value - 'template_url' / 'template_directory' - URL of the active theme's directory. An active child theme will NOT take precedence over this value - 'pingback_url' - The pingback XML-RPC file URL (xmlrpc.php) - 'atom_url' - The Atom feed URL (/feed/atom) - 'rdf_url' - The RDF/RSS 1.0 feed URL (/feed/rdf) - 'rss_url' - The RSS 0.92 feed URL (/feed/rss) - 'rss2_url' - The RSS 2.0 feed URL (/feed) - 'comments_atom_url' - The comments Atom feed URL (/comments/feed) - 'comments_rss2_url' - The comments RSS 2.0 feed URL (/comments/feed) Some `$show` values are deprecated and will be removed in future versions. These options will trigger the _deprecated_argument() function. Deprecated arguments include: - 'siteurl' - Use 'url' instead - 'home' - Use 'url' instead

params.name: show
params.description: Optional. Site info to retrieve. Default empty (site name).

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional. How to filter what is retrieved. Default 'raw'.

returnDescription: Mostly string values, might be empty.

name: get_bloginfo_rss
description: Retrieves RSS container for the bloginfo function. You can retrieve anything that you can using the get_bloginfo() function. Everything will be stripped of tags and characters converted, when the values are retrieved for use in the feeds.

params.name: show
params.description: See get_bloginfo() for possible values.

params.name: filter

params.name: context

name: get_bookmarks
description: Retrieves the list of bookmarks. Attempts to retrieve from the cache first based on MD5 hash of arguments. If that fails, then the query will be built from the arguments and executed. The results will be stored to the cache.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. String or array of arguments to retrieve bookmarks. @type string $orderby How to order the links by. Accepts 'id', 'link_id', 'name', 'link_name', 'url', 'link_url', 'visible', 'link_visible', 'rating', 'link_rating', 'owner', 'link_owner', 'updated', 'link_updated', 'notes', 'link_notes', 'description', 'link_description', 'length' and 'rand'. When `$orderby` is 'length', orders by the character length of 'link_name'. Default 'name'. @type string $order Whether to order bookmarks in ascending or descending order. Accepts 'ASC' (ascending) or 'DESC' (descending). Default 'ASC'. @type int $limit Amount of bookmarks to display. Accepts any positive number or -1 for all. Default -1. @type string $category Comma-separated list of category IDs to include links from. Default empty. @type string $category_name Category to retrieve links for by name. Default empty. @type int|bool $hide_invisible Whether to show or hide links marked as 'invisible'. Accepts 1|true or 0|false. Default 1|true. @type int|bool $show_updated Whether to display the time the bookmark was last updated. Accepts 1|true or 0|false. Default 0|false. @type string $include Comma-separated list of bookmark IDs to include. Default empty. @type string $exclude Comma-separated list of bookmark IDs to exclude. Default empty. @type string $search Search terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after and searched in 'link_url', 'link_name' and 'link_description'. Default empty. }

returnDescription: List of bookmark row objects.

name: get_category

It will cache the category data. If you pass $category an integer of the category ID, then that category will be retrieved from the database, if it isn't already cached, and pass it back. If you look at get_term(), then both types will be passed through several filters and finally sanitized based on the $filter parameter value.

params.name: output

If it doesn't find a match, then it will return the first category matching slug, if $full_match, is set to false. If it does not, then it will return null. It is also possible that it will return a WP_Error object on failure. Check for it when using this function.

params.name: category_path
params.description: URL containing category slugs.

params.name: full_match
params.description: Optional. Whether full path should be matched.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The required return type. One of OBJECT, ARRAY_A, or ARRAY_N, which correspond to a WP_Term object, an associative array, or a numeric array, respectively. Default OBJECT.

returnDescription: Type is based on $output value.

The hierarchy for this template looks like: 1. category-{slug}.php 2. category-{id}.php 3. category.php An example of this is: 1. category-news.php 2. category-2.php 3. category.php The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'category'.

params.description: Category ID

returnDescription: category name

name: get_children
description: Retrieves all children of the post parent ID. Normally, without any enhancements, the children would apply to pages. In the context of the inner workings of WordPress, pages, posts, and attachments share the same table, so therefore the functionality could apply to any one of them. It is then noted that while this function does not work on posts, it does not mean that it won't work on posts. It is recommended that you know what context you wish to retrieve the children of. Attachments may also be made the child of a post, so if that is an accurate statement (which needs to be verified), it would then be possible to get all of the attachments for a post. Attachments have since changed since version 2.5, so this is most likely inaccurate, but serves generally as an example of what is possible. The arguments listed as defaults are for this function and also of the get_posts() function. The arguments are combined with the get_children defaults and are then passed to the get_posts() function, which accepts additional arguments. You can replace the defaults in this function, listed below and the additional arguments listed in the get_posts() function. The 'post_parent' is the most important argument and important attention needs to be paid to the $args parameter. If you pass either an object or an integer (number), then just the 'post_parent' is grabbed and everything else is lost. If you don't specify any arguments, then it is assumed that you are in The Loop and the post parent will be grabbed for from the current post. The 'post_parent' argument is the ID to get the children. The 'numberposts' is the amount of posts to retrieve that has a default of '-1', which is used to get all of the posts. Giving a number higher than 0 will only retrieve that amount of posts. The 'post_type' and 'post_status' arguments can be used to choose what criteria of posts to retrieve. The 'post_type' can be anything, but WordPress post types are 'post', 'pages', and 'attachments'. The 'post_status' argument will accept any post status within the write administration panels.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. User defined arguments for replacing the defaults. Default empty.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The required return type. One of OBJECT, ARRAY_A, or ARRAY_N, which correspond to a WP_Post object, an associative array, or a numeric array, respectively. Default OBJECT.

returnDescription: Array of post objects, arrays, or IDs, depending on `$output`.

description: Returns the HTML email link to the author of the current comment. Care should be taken to protect the email address and assure that email harvesters do not capture your commenter's email address. Most assume that their email address will not appear in raw form on the site. Doing so will enable anyone, including those that people don't want to get the email address and use it for their own means good and bad.

params.name: link_text

params.name: after

returnDescription: HTML markup for the comment author email link. By default, the email address is obfuscated via the {@see 'comment_email'} filter with antispambot().

name: get_comment_author_rss

So it will appear in the order of $before, link, and finally $after.

params.name: after

An empty array if a valid but non-existing comment ID is passed and `$single` is false. An empty string if a valid but non-existing comment ID is passed and `$single` is true. description: Calculates the total number of comment pages.

params.description: Optional. Array of WP_Comment objects. Defaults to `$wp_query->comments`.

params.name: per_page
params.description: Optional. Comments per page. Defaults to the value of `comments_per_page` query var, option of the same name, or 1 (in that order).

params.name: threaded
params.description: Optional. Control over flat or threaded comments. Defaults to the value of `thread_comments` option.

returnDescription: Number of comment pages.

name: get_comment_reply_link
description: Retrieves HTML content for reply to comment link.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Override default arguments. @type string $add_below The first part of the selector used to identify the comment to respond below. The resulting value is passed as the first parameter to addComment.moveForm(), concatenated as $add_below-$comment->comment_ID. Default 'comment'. @type string $respond_id The selector identifying the responding comment. Passed as the third parameter to addComment.moveForm(), and appended to the link URL as a hash value. Default 'respond'. @type string $reply_text The visible text of the Reply link. Default 'Reply'. @type string $reply_to_text The accessible name of the Reply link, using `%s` as a placeholder for the comment author's name. Default 'Reply to %s'. Should start with the visible `reply_text` value. @type bool $show_reply_to_text Whether to use `reply_to_text` as visible link text. Default false. @type string $login_text The text of the link to reply if logged out. Default 'Log in to Reply'. @type int $max_depth The max depth of the comment tree. Default 0. @type int $depth The depth of the new comment. Must be greater than 0 and less than the value of the 'thread_comments_depth' option set in Settings > Discussion. Default 0. @type string $before The text or HTML to add before the reply link. Default empty. @type string $after The text or HTML to add after the reply link. Default empty. }

returnDescription: Link to show comment form on success. False if comments are closed. Null on failure.

name: get_comment_statuses
description: Retrieves all of the WordPress supported comment statuses. Comments have a limited set of valid status values, this provides the comment status values and descriptions.
returnDescription: List of comment status labels keyed by status.

name: get_comment_text
description: Retrieves the text of the current comment.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Optional. WP_Comment or ID of the comment for which to get the text. Default current comment.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. An array of arguments. Default empty array.

returnDescription: The comment content.

name: get_comment_time
description: Retrieves the comment time of the current comment.

params.name: format
params.description: Optional. PHP date format. Defaults to the 'time_format' option.

params.name: gmt
params.description: Optional. Whether to use the GMT date. Default false.

params.name: translate
params.description: Optional. Whether to translate the time (for use in feeds). Default true.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Optional. WP_Comment or ID of the comment for which to get the time. Default current comment.

returnDescription: The formatted time.

name: get_comment_to_edit
description: Returns a WP_Comment object based on comment ID.

params.name: id
params.description: ID of comment to retrieve.

returnDescription: Comment if found. False on failure.

name: get_comment_type
description: Retrieves the comment type of the current comment.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Optional. WP_Comment or ID of the comment for which to get the type. Default current comment.

returnDescription: The comment type.

name: get_commentdata
description: Retrieve an array of comment data about comment $comment_id.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: The ID of the comment

params.name: no_cache
params.description: Whether to use the cache (cast to bool)

params.name: include_unapproved
params.description: Whether to include unapproved comments

returnDescription: The comment data

name: get_comments
description: Retrieves a list of comments. The comment list can be for the blog as a whole or for an individual post.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Array or string of arguments. See WP_Comment_Query::__construct() for information on accepted arguments. Default empty string.

returnDescription: List of comments or number of found comments if `$count` argument is true.

name: get_comments_link
description: Retrieves the link to the current post comments.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

returnDescription: The link to the comments.

name: get_comments_number
description: Retrieves the amount of comments a post has.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is the global `$post`.

returnDescription: If the post exists, a numeric string representing the number of comments the post has, otherwise 0.

name: get_comments_number_text
description: Displays the language string for the number of comments the current post has.

params.name: zero
params.description: Optional. Text for no comments. Default false.

params.name: one
params.description: Optional. Text for one comment. Default false.

params.name: more
params.description: Optional. Text for more than one comment. Default false.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is the global `$post`.

returnDescription: Language string for the number of comments a post has.

name: get_comments_pagenum_link
description: Retrieves the comments page number link.

params.name: pagenum
params.description: Optional. Page number. Default 1.

params.name: max_page
params.description: Optional. The maximum number of comment pages. Default 0.

returnDescription: The comments page number link URL.

name: get_comments_pagination_arrow
description: Helper function that returns the proper pagination arrow HTML for `CommentsPaginationNext` and `CommentsPaginationPrevious` blocks based on the provided `paginationArrow` from `CommentsPagination` context. It's used in CommentsPaginationNext and CommentsPaginationPrevious blocks.

params.name: block
params.description: Block instance.

params.name: pagination_type
params.description: Optional. Type of the arrow we will be rendering. Accepts 'next' or 'previous'. Default 'next'.

returnDescription: The pagination arrow HTML or null if there is none.

name: get_comments_popup_template
description: Retrieve path of comment popup template in current or parent template.
returnDescription: Full path to comments popup template file.

name: get_compat_media_markup
description: WordPress function get_compat_media_markup

name: get_core_checksums
description: Gets and caches the checksums for the given version of WordPress.

params.name: version
params.description: Version string to query.

params.name: locale
params.description: Locale to query.

returnDescription: An array of checksums on success, false on failure.

name: get_core_updates
description: Gets available core updates.

params.name: options
params.description: Set $options['dismissed'] to true to show dismissed upgrades too, set $options['available'] to false to skip not-dismissed updates.

returnDescription: Array of the update objects on success, false on failure.

name: get_current_blog_id
description: Retrieves the current site ID.
returnDescription: Site ID.

name: get_current_network_id
description: Retrieves the current network ID.
returnDescription: The ID of the current network.

name: get_current_screen
description: Get the current screen object
returnDescription: Current screen object or null when screen not defined.

name: get_current_site
description: Gets the current network. Returns an object containing the 'id', 'domain', 'path', and 'site_name' properties of the network being viewed.
returnDescription: The current network.

name: get_current_site_name
description: This deprecated function formerly set the site_name property of the $current_site object. This function simply returns the object, as before. The bootstrap takes care of setting site_name.

name: get_current_theme
description: Retrieve current theme name.

name: get_current_user_id
description: Gets the current user's ID.
returnDescription: The current user's ID, or 0 if no user is logged in.

name: get_currentuserinfo
description: Populate global variables with information about the currently logged in user.
returnDescription: False on XMLRPC Request and invalid auth cookie, WP_User instance otherwise.

name: get_custom_header
description: Gets the header image data.

name: get_custom_header_markup
description: Retrieves the markup for a custom header. The container div will always be returned in the Customizer preview.
returnDescription: The markup for a custom header on success.

name: get_custom_logo
description: Returns a custom logo, linked to home unless the theme supports removing the link on the home page.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. ID of the blog in question. Default is the ID of the current blog.

returnDescription: Custom logo markup.

name: get_dashboard_blog
description: Get the "dashboard blog", the blog where users without a blog edit their profile data. Dashboard blog functionality was removed in WordPress 3.1, replaced by the user admin.
returnDescription: Current site object.

name: get_dashboard_url
description: Retrieves the URL to the user's dashboard. If a user does not belong to any site, the global user dashboard is used. If the user belongs to the current site, the dashboard for the current site is returned. If the user cannot edit the current site, the dashboard to the user's primary site is returned.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. User ID. Defaults to current user.

params.name: path
params.description: Optional path relative to the dashboard. Use only paths known to both site and user admins. Default empty.

params.name: scheme
params.description: The scheme to use. Default is 'admin', which obeys force_ssl_admin() and is_ssl(). 'http' or 'https' can be passed to force those schemes.

returnDescription: Dashboard URL link with optional path appended.

name: get_date_from_gmt
description: Given a date in UTC or GMT timezone, returns that date in the timezone of the site. Requires a date in the Y-m-d H:i:s format. Default return format of 'Y-m-d H:i:s' can be overridden using the `$format` parameter.

params.name: date_string
params.description: The date to be converted, in UTC or GMT timezone.

params.name: format
params.description: The format string for the returned date. Default 'Y-m-d H:i:s'.

returnDescription: Formatted version of the date, in the site's timezone.

name: get_date_template
description: Retrieves path of date template in current or parent template. The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'date'.
returnDescription: Full path to date template file.

name: get_day_link
description: Retrieves the permalink for the day archives with year and month.

params.name: year
params.description: Integer of year. False for current year.

params.name: month
params.description: Integer of month. False for current month.

params.name: day
params.description: Integer of day. False for current day.

returnDescription: The permalink for the specified day, month, and year archive.

name: get_default_block_categories
description: Returns the list of default categories for block types.
returnDescription: Array of categories for block types.

name: get_default_block_editor_settings
description: Returns the default block editor settings.
returnDescription: The default block editor settings.

name: get_default_block_template_types
description: Returns a filtered list of default template types, containing their localized titles and descriptions.
returnDescription: { The default template types. @type array ...$0 { Data for the template type. @type string $title Template type title. @type string $description Template type description. } }

name: get_default_comment_status
description: Gets the default comment status for a post type.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Optional. Post type. Default 'post'.

params.name: comment_type
params.description: Optional. Comment type. Default 'comment'.

returnDescription: Either 'open' or 'closed'.

name: get_default_feed
description: Retrieves the default feed. The default feed is 'rss2', unless a plugin changes it through the {@see 'default_feed'} filter.
returnDescription: Default feed, or for example 'rss2', 'atom', etc.

name: get_default_link_to_edit
description: Retrieves the default link for editing.
returnDescription: Default link object.

name: get_default_page_to_edit
description: Gets the default page information to use.
returnDescription: Post object containing all the default post data as attributes

name: get_default_post_to_edit
description: Returns default post information to use when populating the "Write Post" form.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Optional. A post type string. Default 'post'.

params.name: create_in_db
params.description: Optional. Whether to insert the post into database. Default false.

returnDescription: Post object containing all the default post data as attributes

name: get_delete_post_link
description: Retrieves the delete posts link for post. Can be used within the WordPress loop or outside of it, with any post type.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Default is the global `$post`.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Not used.

params.name: force_delete
params.description: Optional. Whether to bypass Trash and force deletion. Default false.

returnDescription: The delete post link URL for the given post.

name: get_dirsize
description: Gets the size of a directory. A helper function that is used primarily to check whether a blog has exceeded its allowed upload space.

params.name: directory
params.description: Full path of a directory.

params.name: max_execution_time
params.description: Maximum time to run before giving up. In seconds. The timeout is global and is measured from the moment WordPress started to load.

returnDescription: Size in bytes if a valid directory. False if not. Null if timeout.

name: get_dropins
description: Checks the wp-content directory and retrieve all drop-ins with any plugin data.
returnDescription: Array of arrays of dropin plugin data, keyed by plugin file name. See get_plugin_data().

name: get_dynamic_block_names
description: Returns an array of the names of all registered dynamic block types.
returnDescription: Array of dynamic block names.

name: get_edit_bookmark_link
description: Displays the edit bookmark link.

params.name: link
params.description: Optional. Bookmark ID. Default is the ID of the current bookmark.

returnDescription: The edit bookmark link URL.

name: get_edit_comment_link
description: Retrieves the edit comment link.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Optional. Comment ID or WP_Comment object.

params.name: context
params.description: Optional. Context in which the URL should be used. Either 'display', to include HTML entities, or 'url'. Default 'display'.

returnDescription: The edit comment link URL for the given comment, or void if the comment id does not exist or the current user is not allowed to edit it.

name: get_edit_post_link
description: Retrieves the edit post link for post. Can be used within the WordPress loop or outside of it. Can be used with pages, posts, attachments, revisions, global styles, templates, and template parts.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Default is the global `$post`.

params.name: context
params.description: Optional. How to output the '&' character. Default '&'.

returnDescription: The edit post link for the given post. Null if the post type does not exist or does not allow an editing UI.

name: get_edit_profile_url
description: Retrieves the URL to the user's profile editor.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. User ID. Defaults to current user.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Optional. The scheme to use. Default is 'admin', which obeys force_ssl_admin() and is_ssl(). 'http' or 'https' can be passed to force those schemes.

returnDescription: Dashboard URL link with optional path appended.

name: get_edit_tag_link
description: Retrieves the edit link for a tag.

params.name: tag
params.description: The ID or term object whose edit link will be retrieved.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy slug. Default 'post_tag'.

returnDescription: The edit tag link URL for the given tag.

name: get_edit_term_link
description: Retrieves the URL for editing a given term.

params.name: term
params.description: The ID or term object whose edit link will be retrieved.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy. Defaults to the taxonomy of the term identified by `$term`.

params.name: object_type
params.description: Optional. The object type. Used to highlight the proper post type menu on the linked page. Defaults to the first object_type associated with the taxonomy.

returnDescription: The edit term link URL for the given term, or null on failure.

name: get_edit_user_link
description: Retrieves the edit user link.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. User ID. Defaults to the current user.

returnDescription: URL to edit user page or empty string.

name: get_editable_authors
description: Gets author users who can edit posts.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

returnDescription: List of editable authors. False if no editable users.

name: get_editable_roles
description: Fetch a filtered list of user roles that the current user is allowed to edit. Simple function whose main purpose is to allow filtering of the list of roles in the $wp_roles object so that plugins can remove inappropriate ones depending on the situation or user making edits. Specifically because without filtering anyone with the edit_users capability can edit others to be administrators, even if they are only editors or authors. This filter allows admins to delegate user management.
returnDescription: Array of arrays containing role information.

name: get_editable_user_ids
description: Gets the IDs of any users who can edit posts.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

params.name: exclude_zeros
params.description: Optional. Whether to exclude zeroes. Default true.

returnDescription: Array of editable user IDs, empty array otherwise.

name: get_editor_stylesheets
description: Retrieves any registered editor stylesheet URLs.
returnDescription: If registered, a list of editor stylesheet URLs.

name: get_embed_template
description: Retrieves an embed template path in the current or parent template. The hierarchy for this template looks like: 1. embed-{post_type}-{post_format}.php 2. embed-{post_type}.php 3. embed.php An example of this is: 1. embed-post-audio.php 2. embed-post.php 3. embed.php The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'embed'.
returnDescription: Full path to embed template file.

name: get_enclosed
description: Retrieves enclosures already enclosed for a post.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Post ID.

returnDescription: Array of enclosures for the given post.

name: get_extended
description: Gets extended entry info ( ). There should not be any space after the second dash and before the word 'more'. There can be text or space(s) after the word 'more', but won't be referenced. The returned array has 'main', 'extended', and 'more_text' keys. Main has the text before the ` `. The 'extended' key has the content after the ` ` comment. The 'more_text' key has the custom "Read More" text.

params.name: post
params.description: Post content.

returnDescription: { Extended entry info. @type string $main Content before the more tag. @type string $extended Content after the more tag. @type string $more_text Custom read more text, or empty string. }

name: get_feed_build_date
description: Gets the UTC time of the most recently modified post from WP_Query. If viewing a comment feed, the time of the most recently modified comment will be returned.

params.name: format
params.description: Date format string to return the time in.

returnDescription: The time in requested format, or false on failure.

name: get_feed_link
description: Retrieves the permalink for the feed type.

params.name: feed
params.description: Optional. Feed type. Possible values include 'rss2', 'atom'. Default is the value of get_default_feed().

returnDescription: The feed permalink.

name: get_file
description: WordPress function get_file

name: get_file_data
description: Retrieves metadata from a file. Searches for metadata in the first 8 KB of a file, such as a plugin or theme. Each piece of metadata must be on its own line. Fields can not span multiple lines, the value will get cut at the end of the first line. If the file data is not within that first 8 KB, then the author should correct their plugin file and move the data headers to the top.

params.name: file
params.description: Absolute path to the file.

params.name: default_headers
params.description: List of headers, in the format `array( 'HeaderKey' => 'Header Name' )`.

params.name: context
params.description: Optional. If specified adds filter hook {@see 'extra_$context_headers'}. Default empty string.

returnDescription: Array of file header values keyed by header name.

name: get_file_description
description: Gets the description for standard WordPress theme files.

params.name: file
params.description: Filesystem path or filename.

returnDescription: Description of file from $wp_file_descriptions or basename of $file if description doesn't exist. Appends 'Page Template' to basename of $file if the file is a page template.

name: get_filesystem_method
description: Determines which method to use for reading, writing, modifying, or deleting files on the filesystem. The priority of the transports are: Direct, SSH2, FTP PHP Extension, FTP Sockets (Via Sockets class, or `fsockopen()`). Valid values for these are: 'direct', 'ssh2', 'ftpext' or 'ftpsockets'. The return value can be overridden by defining the `FS_METHOD` constant in `wp-config.php`, or filtering via {@see 'filesystem_method'}.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Connection details. Default empty array.

returnDescription: The transport to use, see description for valid return values.

name: get_footer
description: Loads footer template. Includes the footer template for a theme or if a name is specified then a specialized footer will be included. For the parameter, if the file is called "footer-special.php" then specify "special".

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the specialized footer. Default null.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments passed to the footer template. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Void on success, false if the template does not exist.

name: get_front_page_template
description: Retrieves path of front page template in current or parent template. The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'frontpage'.
returnDescription: Full path to front page template file.

name: get_gmt_from_date
description: Given a date in the timezone of the site, returns that date in UTC. Requires and returns a date in the Y-m-d H:i:s format. Return format can be overridden using the $format parameter.

params.name: date_string
params.description: The date to be converted, in the timezone of the site.

params.name: format
params.description: The format string for the returned date. Default 'Y-m-d H:i:s'.

returnDescription: Formatted version of the date, in UTC.

name: get_header
description: Loads header template. Includes the header template for a theme or if a name is specified then a specialized header will be included. For the parameter, if the file is called "header-special.php" then specify "special".

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the specialized header. Default null.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments passed to the header template. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Void on success, false if the template does not exist.

name: get_header_image
description: Retrieves header image for custom header.

name: get_header_image_tag
description: Creates image tag markup for a custom header image.

params.name: attr
params.description: Optional. Additional attributes for the image tag. Can be used to override the default attributes. Default empty.

returnDescription: HTML image element markup or empty string on failure.

name: get_header_textcolor
description: Retrieves the custom header text color in 3- or 6-digit hexadecimal form.
returnDescription: Header text color in 3- or 6-digit hexadecimal form (minus the hash symbol).

name: get_header_video_settings
description: Retrieves header video settings.

name: get_header_video_url
description: Retrieves header video URL for custom header. Uses a local video if present, or falls back to an external video.
returnDescription: Header video URL or false if there is no video.

name: get_hidden_columns
description: Get a list of hidden columns.

params.name: screen
params.description: The screen you want the hidden columns for

returnDescription: Array of IDs of hidden columns.

name: get_hidden_meta_boxes
description: Gets an array of IDs of hidden meta boxes.

params.name: screen
params.description: Screen identifier

returnDescription: IDs of hidden meta boxes.

name: get_home_path
description: Gets the absolute filesystem path to the root of the WordPress installation.
returnDescription: Full filesystem path to the root of the WordPress installation.

name: get_home_template
description: Retrieves path of home template in current or parent template. The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'home'.
returnDescription: Full path to home template file.

name: get_home_url
description: Retrieves the URL for a given site where the front end is accessible. Returns the 'home' option with the appropriate protocol. The protocol will be 'https' if is_ssl() evaluates to true; otherwise, it will be the same as the 'home' option. If `$scheme` is 'http' or 'https', is_ssl() is overridden.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. Site ID. Default null (current site).

params.name: path
params.description: Optional. Path relative to the home URL. Default empty.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Optional. Scheme to give the home URL context. Accepts 'http', 'https', 'relative', 'rest', or null. Default null.

returnDescription: Home URL link with optional path appended.

name: get_hooked_blocks
description: Retrieves block types hooked into the given block, grouped by anchor block type and the relative position.
returnDescription: Array of block types grouped by anchor block type and the relative position.

name: get_html_split_regex
description: Retrieves the regular expression for an HTML element.
returnDescription: The regular expression.

name: get_http_origin
description: Gets the HTTP Origin of the current request.
returnDescription: URL of the origin. Empty string if no origin.

name: get_id_from_blogname
description: Retrieves a site's ID given its (subdomain or directory) slug.

params.name: slug
params.description: A site's slug.

returnDescription: The site ID, or null if no site is found for the given slug.

name: get_image_send_to_editor
description: Retrieves the image HTML to send to the editor.

params.name: id
params.description: Image attachment ID.

params.name: caption
params.description: Image caption.

params.name: title
params.description: Image title attribute.

params.name: align
params.description: Image CSS alignment property.

params.name: url
params.description: Optional. Image src URL. Default empty.

params.name: rel
params.description: Optional. Value for rel attribute or whether to add a default value. Default false.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'medium'.

params.name: alt
params.description: Optional. Image alt attribute. Default empty.

returnDescription: The HTML output to insert into the editor.

name: get_image_tag
description: Gets an img tag for an image attachment, scaling it down if requested. The {@see 'get_image_tag_class'} filter allows for changing the class name for the image without having to use regular expressions on the HTML content. The parameters are: what WordPress will use for the class, the Attachment ID, image align value, and the size the image should be. The second filter, {@see 'get_image_tag'}, has the HTML content, which can then be further manipulated by a plugin to change all attribute values and even HTML content.

params.name: id
params.description: Attachment ID.

params.name: alt
params.description: Image description for the alt attribute.

params.name: title
params.description: Image description for the title attribute.

params.name: align
params.description: Part of the class name for aligning the image.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'medium'.

returnDescription: HTML IMG element for given image attachment.

name: get_importers
description: Retrieves the list of importers.

name: get_index_rel_link
description: Get site index relational link.

name: get_index_template
description: Retrieves path of index template in current or parent template. The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'index'.
returnDescription: Full path to index template file.

name: get_inline_data
description: Adds hidden fields with the data for use in the inline editor for posts and pages.

params.name: post
params.description: Post object.

name: get_intermediate_image_sizes
description: Gets the available intermediate image size names.
returnDescription: An array of image size names.

name: get_language_attributes
description: Gets the language attributes for the 'html' tag. Builds up a set of HTML attributes containing the text direction and language information for the page.

params.name: doctype
params.description: Optional. The type of HTML document. Accepts 'xhtml' or 'html'. Default 'html'.

returnDescription: A space-separated list of language attributes.

name: get_last_updated
description: Gets a list of most recently updated blogs.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Not used.

params.name: start
params.description: Optional. Number of blogs to offset the query. Used to build LIMIT clause. Can be used for pagination. Default 0.

params.name: quantity

name: get_lastcommentmodified
description: Retrieves the date the last comment was modified.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Which timezone to use in reference to 'gmt', 'blog', or 'server' locations.

returnDescription: Last comment modified date on success, false on failure.

name: get_lastpostdate
description: Retrieves the most recent time that a post on the site was published. The server timezone is the default and is the difference between GMT and server time. The 'blog' value is the date when the last post was posted. The 'gmt' is when the last post was posted in GMT formatted date.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Optional. The timezone for the timestamp. Accepts 'server', 'blog', or 'gmt'. 'server' uses the server's internal timezone. 'blog' uses the `post_date` field, which proxies to the timezone set for the site. 'gmt' uses the `post_date_gmt` field. Default 'server'.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Optional. The post type to check. Default 'any'.

returnDescription: The date of the last post, or false on failure.

name: get_lastpostmodified
description: Gets the most recent time that a post on the site was modified. The server timezone is the default and is the difference between GMT and server time. The 'blog' value is just when the last post was modified. The 'gmt' is when the last post was modified in GMT time.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Optional. The timezone for the timestamp. See get_lastpostdate() for information on accepted values. Default 'server'.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Optional. The post type to check. Default 'any'.

returnDescription: The timestamp in 'Y-m-d H:i:s' format, or false on failure.

name: get_linkobjects
description: Gets an array of link objects associated with category n. Usage: $links = get_linkobjects(1); if ($links) { foreach ($links as $link) { echo ' '.$link->link_name.' '.$link->link_description.' '; } } Fields are: - link_id - link_url - link_name - link_image - link_target - link_category - link_description - link_visible - link_owner - link_rating - link_updated - link_rel - link_notes

params.name: category
params.description: Optional. The category to use. If no category supplied, uses all. Default 0.

params.name: orderby
params.description: Optional. The order to output the links. E.g. 'id', 'name', 'url', 'description', 'rating', or 'owner'. Default 'name'. If you start the name with an underscore, the order will be reversed. Specifying 'rand' as the order will return links in a random order.

params.name: limit
params.description: Optional. Limit to X entries. If not specified, all entries are shown. Default 0.

name: get_linkobjectsbyname

name: get_locale
description: Retrieves the current locale. If the locale is set, then it will filter the locale in the {@see 'locale'} filter hook and return the value. If the locale is not set already, then the WPLANG constant is used if it is defined. Then it is filtered through the {@see 'locale'} filter hook and the value for the locale global set and the locale is returned. The process to get the locale should only be done once, but the locale will always be filtered using the {@see 'locale'} hook.
returnDescription: The locale of the blog or from the {@see 'locale'} hook.

name: get_locale_stylesheet_uri
description: Retrieves the localized stylesheet URI. The stylesheet directory for the localized stylesheet files are located, by default, in the base theme directory. The name of the locale file will be the locale followed by '.css'. If that does not exist, then the text direction stylesheet will be checked for existence, for example 'ltr.css'. The theme may change the location of the stylesheet directory by either using the {@see 'stylesheet_directory_uri'} or {@see 'locale_stylesheet_uri'} filters. If you want to change the location of the stylesheet files for the entire WordPress workflow, then change the former. If you just have the locale in a separate folder, then change the latter.
returnDescription: URI to active theme's localized stylesheet.

name: get_metadata
description: Retrieves the value of a metadata field for the specified object type and ID. If the meta field exists, a single value is returned if `$single` is true, or an array of values if it's false. If the meta field does not exist, the result depends on get_metadata_default(). By default, an empty string is returned if `$single` is true, or an empty array if it's false.

params.name: meta_type
params.description: Type of object metadata is for. Accepts 'blog', 'post', 'comment', 'term', 'user', or any other object type with an associated meta table.

params.name: object_id
params.description: ID of the object metadata is for.

params.name: meta_key
params.description: Optional. Metadata key. If not specified, retrieve all metadata for the specified object. Default empty string.

params.name: single
params.description: Optional. If true, return only the first value of the specified `$meta_key`. This parameter has no effect if `$meta_key` is not specified. Default false.

returnDescription: An array of values if `$single` is false. The value of the meta field if `$single` is true. False for an invalid `$object_id` (non-numeric, zero, or negative value), or if `$meta_type` is not specified. An empty array if a valid but non-existing object ID is passed and `$single` is false. An empty string if a valid but non-existing object ID is passed and `$single` is true. Note: Non-serialized values are returned as strings: - false values are returned as empty strings ('') - true values are returned as '1' - numbers (both integer and float) are returned as strings Arrays and objects retain their original type.

name: get_metadata_by_mid
description: Retrieves metadata by meta ID.

params.name: meta_type
params.description: Type of object metadata is for. Accepts 'blog', 'post', 'comment', 'term', 'user', or any other object type with an associated meta table.

params.name: meta_id
params.description: ID for a specific meta row.

returnDescription: { Metadata object, or boolean `false` if the metadata doesn't exist. @type string $meta_key The meta key. @type mixed $meta_value The unserialized meta value. @type string $meta_id Optional. The meta ID when the meta type is any value except 'user'. @type string $umeta_id Optional. The meta ID when the meta type is 'user'. @type string $blog_id Optional. The object ID when the meta type is 'blog'. @type string $post_id Optional. The object ID when the meta type is 'post'. @type string $comment_id Optional. The object ID when the meta type is 'comment'. @type string $term_id Optional. The object ID when the meta type is 'term'. @type string $user_id Optional. The object ID when the meta type is 'user'. }

name: get_metadata_default
description: Retrieves default metadata value for the specified meta key and object. By default, an empty string is returned if `$single` is true, or an empty array if it's false.

params.name: meta_type
params.description: Type of object metadata is for. Accepts 'blog', 'post', 'comment', 'term', 'user', or any other object type with an associated meta table.

params.name: object_id
params.description: ID of the object metadata is for.

params.name: meta_key
params.description: Metadata key.

params.name: single
params.description: Optional. If true, return only the first value of the specified `$meta_key`. This parameter has no effect if `$meta_key` is not specified. Default false.

returnDescription: An array of default values if `$single` is false. The default value of the meta field if `$single` is true.

name: get_metadata_raw
description: Retrieves raw metadata value for the specified object.

params.name: meta_type
params.description: Type of object metadata is for. Accepts 'blog', 'post', 'comment', 'term', 'user', or any other object type with an associated meta table.

params.name: object_id
params.description: ID of the object metadata is for.

params.name: meta_key
params.description: Optional. Metadata key. If not specified, retrieve all metadata for the specified object. Default empty string.

params.name: single
params.description: Optional. If true, return only the first value of the specified `$meta_key`. This parameter has no effect if `$meta_key` is not specified. Default false.

returnDescription: An array of values if `$single` is false. The value of the meta field if `$single` is true. False for an invalid `$object_id` (non-numeric, zero, or negative value), or if `$meta_type` is not specified. Null if the value does not exist.

name: get_object_taxonomies
description: Returns the names or objects of the taxonomies which are registered for the requested object or object type, such as a post object or post type name. Example: $taxonomies = get_object_taxonomies( 'post' ); This results in: Array( 'category', 'post_tag' )

name: get_object_term_cache
description: Retrieves the cached term objects for the given object ID. Upstream functions (like get_the_terms() and is_object_in_term()) are responsible for populating the object-term relationship cache. The current function only fetches relationship data that is already in the cache.

params.name: id
params.description: Term object ID, for example a post, comment, or user ID.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

returnDescription: Array of `WP_Term` objects, if cached. False if cache is empty for `$taxonomy` and `$id`. WP_Error if get_term() returns an error object for any term.

name: get_objects_in_term
description: Retrieves object IDs of valid taxonomy and term. The strings of `$taxonomies` must exist before this function will continue. On failure of finding a valid taxonomy, it will return a WP_Error. The `$terms` aren't checked the same as `$taxonomies`, but still need to exist for object IDs to be returned. It is possible to change the order that object IDs are returned by using `$args` with either ASC or DESC array. The value should be in the key named 'order'.

params.name: term_ids
params.description: Term ID or array of term IDs of terms that will be used.

params.name: taxonomies
params.description: String of taxonomy name or Array of string values of taxonomy names.

params.name: args
params.description: { Change the order of the object IDs. @type string $order Order to retrieve terms. Accepts 'ASC' or 'DESC'. Default 'ASC'. }

returnDescription: An array of object IDs as numeric strings on success, WP_Error if the taxonomy does not exist.
