name: get_settings_errors
description: Fetches settings errors registered by add_settings_error(). Checks the $wp_settings_errors array for any errors declared during the current pageload and returns them. If changes were just submitted ($_GET['settings-updated']) and settings errors were saved to the 'settings_errors' transient then those errors will be returned instead. This is used to pass errors back across pageloads. Use the $sanitize argument to manually re-sanitize the option before returning errors. This is useful if you have errors or notices you want to show even when the user hasn't submitted data (i.e. when they first load an options page, or in the {@see 'admin_notices'} action hook).

params.name: setting
params.description: Optional. Slug title of a specific setting whose errors you want.

params.name: sanitize
params.description: Optional. Whether to re-sanitize the setting value before returning errors.

returnDescription: { Array of settings error arrays. @type array ...$0 { Associative array of setting error data. @type string $setting Slug title of the setting to which this error applies. @type string $code Slug-name to identify the error. Used as part of 'id' attribute in HTML output. @type string $message The formatted message text to display to the user (will be shown inside styled ` ` and ` ` tags). @type string $type Optional. Message type, controls HTML class. Possible values include 'error', 'success', 'warning', 'info'. Default 'error'. } }

name: get_shortcode_atts_regex
description: Retrieves the shortcode attributes regex.
returnDescription: The shortcode attribute regular expression.

name: get_shortcode_regex
description: Retrieves the shortcode regular expression for searching. The regular expression combines the shortcode tags in the regular expression in a regex class. The regular expression contains 6 different sub matches to help with parsing. 1 - An extra [ to allow for escaping shortcodes with double [[]] 2 - The shortcode name 3 - The shortcode argument list 4 - The self closing / 5 - The content of a shortcode when it wraps some content. 6 - An extra ] to allow for escaping shortcodes with double [[]]

params.name: tagnames
params.description: Optional. List of shortcodes to find. Defaults to all registered shortcodes.

returnDescription: The shortcode search regular expression.

name: get_shortcode_tags_in_content
description: Returns a list of registered shortcode names found in the given content. Example usage: get_shortcode_tags_in_content( '[audio src="file.mp3"][/audio] [foo] [gallery ids="1,2,3"]' ); // array( 'audio', 'gallery' )

params.name: content
params.description: The content to check.

returnDescription: An array of registered shortcode names found in the content.

name: get_shortcut_link
description: Retrieves the Press This bookmarklet link.

name: get_sidebar
description: Loads sidebar template. Includes the sidebar template for a theme or if a name is specified then a specialized sidebar will be included. For the parameter, if the file is called "sidebar-special.php" then specify "special".

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the specialized sidebar. Default null.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments passed to the sidebar template. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Void on success, false if the template does not exist.

name: get_single_template
description: Retrieves path of single template in current or parent template. Applies to single Posts, single Attachments, and single custom post types. The hierarchy for this template looks like: 1. {Post Type Template}.php 2. single-{post_type}-{post_name}.php 3. single-{post_type}.php 4. single.php An example of this is: 1. templates/full-width.php 2. single-post-hello-world.php 3. single-post.php 4. single.php The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'single'.
returnDescription: Full path to single template file.

name: get_singular_template
description: Retrieves the path of the singular template in current or parent template. The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'singular'.
returnDescription: Full path to singular template file.

name: get_site
description: Retrieves site data given a site ID or site object. Site data will be cached and returned after being passed through a filter. If the provided site is empty, the current site global will be used.

params.name: site
params.description: Optional. Site to retrieve. Default is the current site.

returnDescription: The site object or null if not found.

name: get_site_allowed_themes
description: Deprecated functionality for getting themes network-enabled themes.

name: get_site_by_path
description: Retrieves the closest matching site object by its domain and path. This will not necessarily return an exact match for a domain and path. Instead, it breaks the domain and path into pieces that are then used to match the closest possibility from a query. The intent of this method is to match a site object during bootstrap for a requested site address

params.name: domain
params.description: Domain to check.

params.name: path
params.description: Path to check.

params.name: segments
params.description: Path segments to use. Defaults to null, or the full path.

returnDescription: Site object if successful. False when no site is found.

name: get_site_icon_url
description: Returns the Site Icon URL.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Size of the site icon. Default 512 (pixels).

params.name: url
params.description: Optional. Fallback url if no site icon is found. Default empty.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. ID of the blog to get the site icon for. Default current blog.

returnDescription: Site Icon URL.

name: get_site_meta
description: Retrieves metadata for a site.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Site ID.

params.name: key
params.description: Optional. The meta key to retrieve. By default, returns data for all keys. Default empty.

params.name: single
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a single value. This parameter has no effect if `$key` is not specified. Default false.

returnDescription: An array of values if `$single` is false. The value of meta data field if `$single` is true. False for an invalid `$site_id` (non-numeric, zero, or negative value). An empty array if a valid but non-existing site ID is passed and `$single` is false. An empty string if a valid but non-existing site ID is passed and `$single` is true. Note: Non-serialized values are returned as strings: - false values are returned as empty strings ('') - true values are returned as '1' - numbers (both integer and float) are returned as strings Arrays and objects retain their original type.

name: get_site_option
description: Retrieve an option value for the current network based on name of option.

params.name: option
params.description: Name of the option to retrieve. Expected to not be SQL-escaped.

params.name: default_value
params.description: Optional. Value to return if the option doesn't exist. Default false.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Whether to use cache. Multisite only. Always set to true.

returnDescription: Value set for the option.

name: get_site_screen_help_sidebar_content
description: Returns the content for the help sidebar on the Edit Site screens.
returnDescription: Help sidebar content.

name: get_site_screen_help_tab_args
description: Returns the arguments for the help tab on the Edit Site screens.
returnDescription: Help tab arguments.

name: get_site_transient
description: Retrieves the value of a site transient. If the transient does not exist, does not have a value, or has expired, then the return value will be false.

params.name: transient
params.description: Transient name. Expected to not be SQL-escaped.

returnDescription: Value of transient.

name: get_site_url
description: Retrieves the URL for a given site where WordPress application files (e.g. wp-blog-header.php or the wp-admin/ folder) are accessible. Returns the 'site_url' option with the appropriate protocol, 'https' if is_ssl() and 'http' otherwise. If `$scheme` is 'http' or 'https', `is_ssl()` is overridden.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. Site ID. Default null (current site).

params.name: path
params.description: Optional. Path relative to the site URL. Default empty.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Optional. Scheme to give the site URL context. Accepts 'http', 'https', 'login', 'login_post', 'admin', or 'relative'. Default null.

returnDescription: Site URL link with optional path appended.

name: get_sitemap_url
description: Retrieves the full URL for a sitemap.

params.name: name
params.description: The sitemap name.

params.name: subtype_name
params.description: The sitemap subtype name. Default empty string.

params.name: page
params.description: The page of the sitemap. Default 1.

returnDescription: The sitemap URL or false if the sitemap doesn't exist.

name: get_sites
description: Retrieves a list of sites matching requested arguments.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Array or string of arguments. See WP_Site_Query::__construct() for information on accepted arguments. Default empty array.

returnDescription: List of WP_Site objects, a list of site IDs when 'fields' is set to 'ids', or the number of sites when 'count' is passed as a query var.

name: get_sitestats
description: Gets the network's site and user counts.
returnDescription: { Site and user count for the network. @type int $blogs Number of sites on the network. @type int $users Number of users on the network. }

name: get_space_allowed
description: Returns the upload quota for the current blog.
returnDescription: Quota in megabytes.

name: get_space_used
description: Returns the space used by the current site.
returnDescription: Used space in megabytes.

name: get_status_header_desc
description: Retrieves the description for the HTTP status.

params.name: code
params.description: HTTP status code.

returnDescription: Status description if found, an empty string otherwise.

name: get_stylesheet
description: Retrieves name of the current stylesheet. The theme name that is currently set as the front end theme. For all intents and purposes, the template name and the stylesheet name are going to be the same for most cases.
returnDescription: Stylesheet name.

name: get_stylesheet_directory
description: Retrieves stylesheet directory path for the active theme.
returnDescription: Path to active theme's stylesheet directory.

name: get_stylesheet_directory_uri
description: Retrieves stylesheet directory URI for the active theme.
returnDescription: URI to active theme's stylesheet directory.

name: get_stylesheet_uri
description: Retrieves stylesheet URI for the active theme. The stylesheet file name is 'style.css' which is appended to the stylesheet directory URI path. See get_stylesheet_directory_uri().
returnDescription: URI to active theme's stylesheet.

name: get_subdirectory_reserved_names
description: Retrieves a list of reserved site on a sub-directory Multisite installation.
returnDescription: Array of reserved names.

name: get_submit_button
description: Returns a submit button, with provided text and appropriate class.

params.name: text
params.description: Optional. The text of the button. Defaults to 'Save Changes'.

params.name: type
params.description: Optional. The type and CSS class(es) of the button. Core values include 'primary', 'small', and 'large'. Default 'primary large'.

params.name: name
params.description: Optional. The HTML name of the submit button. If no `id` attribute is given in the `$other_attributes` parameter, `$name` will be used as the button's `id`. Default 'submit'.

params.name: wrap
params.description: Optional. True if the output button should be wrapped in a paragraph tag, false otherwise. Default true.

params.name: other_attributes
params.description: Optional. Other attributes that should be output with the button, mapping attributes to their values, e.g. `array( 'id' => 'search-submit' )`. These key/value attribute pairs will be output as `attribute="value"`, where attribute is the key. Attributes can also be provided as a string, e.g. `id="search-submit"`, though the array format is generally preferred. Default empty string.

returnDescription: Submit button HTML.

name: get_super_admins
description: Retrieves a list of super admins.
returnDescription: List of super admin logins.

name: get_tag
description: Retrieves a post tag by tag ID or tag object. If you pass the $tag parameter an object, which is assumed to be the tag row object retrieved from the database, it will cache the tag data. If you pass $tag an integer of the tag ID, then that tag will be retrieved from the database, if it isn't already cached, and passed back. If you look at get_term(), both types will be passed through several filters and finally sanitized based on the $filter parameter value.

params.name: tag
params.description: A tag ID or object.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The required return type. One of OBJECT, ARRAY_A, or ARRAY_N, which correspond to a WP_Term object, an associative array, or a numeric array, respectively. Default OBJECT.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional. How to sanitize tag fields. Default 'raw'.

returnDescription: Tag data in type defined by $output parameter. WP_Error if $tag is empty, null if it does not exist.

name: get_tag_feed_link
description: Retrieves the permalink for a tag feed.

params.name: tag
params.description: The ID or term object whose feed link will be retrieved.

params.name: feed
params.description: Optional. Feed type. Possible values include 'rss2', 'atom'. Default is the value of get_default_feed().

returnDescription: The feed permalink for the given tag.

name: get_tag_link
description: Retrieves the link to the tag.

params.name: tag
params.description: Tag ID or object.

returnDescription: Link on success, empty string if tag does not exist.

name: get_tag_regex
description: Returns RegEx body to liberally match an opening HTML tag. Matches an opening HTML tag that: 1. Is self-closing or 2. Has no body but has a closing tag of the same name or 3. Contains a body and a closing tag of the same name Note: this RegEx does not balance inner tags and does not attempt to produce valid HTML

params.name: tag
params.description: An HTML tag name. Example: 'video'.

returnDescription: Tag RegEx.

name: get_tag_template
description: Retrieves path of tag template in current or parent template. The hierarchy for this template looks like: 1. tag-{slug}.php 2. tag-{id}.php 3. tag.php An example of this is: 1. tag-wordpress.php 2. tag-3.php 3. tag.php The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'tag'.
returnDescription: Full path to tag template file.

name: get_tags
description: Retrieves all post tags.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Arguments to retrieve tags. See get_terms() for additional options. @type string $taxonomy Taxonomy to retrieve terms for. Default 'post_tag'. }

returnDescription: Array of 'post_tag' term objects, a count thereof, or WP_Error if any of the taxonomies do not exist.

name: get_tags_to_edit
description: Gets comma-separated list of tags available to edit.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. The taxonomy for which to retrieve terms. Default 'post_tag'.

name: get_tax_sql
description: Given a taxonomy query, generates SQL to be appended to a main query.

params.name: tax_query
params.description: A compact tax query

name: get_taxonomies
description: Retrieves a list of registered taxonomy names or objects.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. An array of `key => value` arguments to match against the taxonomy objects. Default empty array.

returnDescription: An array of taxonomy names or objects.

name: get_taxonomies_for_attachments
description: Retrieves all of the taxonomies that are registered for attachments. Handles mime-type-specific taxonomies such as attachment:image and attachment:video.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The type of taxonomy output to return. Accepts 'names' or 'objects'. Default 'names'.

returnDescription: Array of names or objects of registered taxonomies for attachments.

name: get_taxonomy
description: Retrieves the taxonomy object of $taxonomy. The get_taxonomy function will first check that the parameter string given is a taxonomy object and if it is, it will return it.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Name of taxonomy object to return.

returnDescription: The taxonomy object or false if $taxonomy doesn't exist.

name: get_taxonomy_labels
description: Builds an object with all taxonomy labels out of a taxonomy object.

params.name: tax
params.description: Taxonomy object.

returnDescription: { Taxonomy labels object. The first default value is for non-hierarchical taxonomies (like tags) and the second one is for hierarchical taxonomies (like categories). @type string $name General name for the taxonomy, usually plural. The same as and overridden by `$tax->label`. Default 'Tags'/'Categories'. @type string $singular_name Name for one object of this taxonomy. Default 'Tag'/'Category'. @type string $search_items Default 'Search Tags'/'Search Categories'. @type string $popular_items This label is only used for non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default 'Popular Tags'. @type string $all_items Default 'All Tags'/'All Categories'. @type string $parent_item This label is only used for hierarchical taxonomies. Default 'Parent Category'. @type string $parent_item_colon The same as `parent_item`, but with colon `:` in the end. @type string $name_field_description Description for the Name field on Edit Tags screen. Default 'The name is how it appears on your site'. @type string $slug_field_description Description for the Slug field on Edit Tags screen. Default 'The “slug” is the URL-friendly version of the name. It is usually all lowercase and contains only letters, numbers, and hyphens'. @type string $parent_field_description Description for the Parent field on Edit Tags screen. Default 'Assign a parent term to create a hierarchy. The term Jazz, for example, would be the parent of Bebop and Big Band'. @type string $desc_field_description Description for the Description field on Edit Tags screen. Default 'The description is not prominent by default; however, some themes may show it'. @type string $edit_item Default 'Edit Tag'/'Edit Category'. @type string $view_item Default 'View Tag'/'View Category'. @type string $update_item Default 'Update Tag'/'Update Category'. @type string $add_new_item Default 'Add Tag'/'Add Category'. @type string $new_item_name Default 'New Tag Name'/'New Category Name'. @type string $template_name Default 'Tag Archives'/'Category Archives'. @type string $separate_items_with_commas This label is only used for non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default 'Separate tags with commas', used in the meta box. @type string $add_or_remove_items This label is only used for non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default 'Add or remove tags', used in the meta box when JavaScript is disabled. @type string $choose_from_most_used This label is only used on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default 'Choose from the most used tags', used in the meta box. @type string $not_found Default 'No tags found'/'No categories found', used in the meta box and taxonomy list table. @type string $no_terms Default 'No tags'/'No categories', used in the posts and media list tables. @type string $filter_by_item This label is only used for hierarchical taxonomies. Default 'Filter by category', used in the posts list table. @type string $items_list_navigation Label for the table pagination hidden heading. @type string $items_list Label for the table hidden heading. @type string $most_used Title for the Most Used tab. Default 'Most Used'. @type string $back_to_items Label displayed after a term has been updated. @type string $item_link Used in the block editor. Title for a navigation link block variation. Default 'Tag Link'/'Category Link'. @type string $item_link_description Used in the block editor. Description for a navigation link block variation. Default 'A link to a tag'/'A link to a category'. }

name: get_taxonomy_template
description: Retrieves path of custom taxonomy term template in current or parent template. The hierarchy for this template looks like: 1. taxonomy-{taxonomy_slug}-{term_slug}.php 2. taxonomy-{taxonomy_slug}-{term_id}.php 3. taxonomy-{taxonomy_slug}.php 4. taxonomy.php An example of this is: 1. taxonomy-location-texas.php 2. taxonomy-location-67.php 3. taxonomy-location.php 4. taxonomy.php The template hierarchy and template path are filterable via the {@see '$type_template_hierarchy'} and {@see '$type_template'} dynamic hooks, where `$type` is 'taxonomy'.
returnDescription: Full path to custom taxonomy term template file.

name: get_temp_dir
description: Determines a writable directory for temporary files. Function's preference is the return value of `sys_get_temp_dir()`, followed by the `upload_tmp_dir` value from `php.ini`, followed by `WP_CONTENT_DIR`, before finally defaulting to `/tmp/`. Note that `sys_get_temp_dir()` honors the `TMPDIR` environment variable. In the event that this function does not find a writable location, it may be overridden by the `WP_TEMP_DIR` constant in your `wp-config.php` file.
returnDescription: Writable temporary directory.

name: get_template
description: Retrieves name of the active theme.
returnDescription: Template name.

name: get_template_directory
description: Retrieves template directory path for the active theme.
returnDescription: Path to active theme's template directory.

name: get_template_directory_uri
description: Retrieves template directory URI for the active theme.
returnDescription: URI to active theme's template directory.

name: get_template_hierarchy
description: Gets the template hierarchy for the given template slug to be created. Note: Always add `index` as the last fallback template.

params.name: slug
params.description: The template slug to be created.

params.name: is_custom
params.description: Optional. Indicates if a template is custom or part of the template hierarchy. Default false.

params.name: template_prefix
params.description: Optional. The template prefix for the created template. Used to extract the main template type, e.g. in `taxonomy-books` the `taxonomy` is extracted. Default empty string.

returnDescription: The template hierarchy.

name: get_template_part
description: Loads a template part into a template. Provides a simple mechanism for child themes to overload reusable sections of code in the theme. Includes the named template part for a theme or if a name is specified then a specialized part will be included. If the theme contains no {slug}.php file then no template will be included. The template is included using require, not require_once, so you may include the same template part multiple times. For the $name parameter, if the file is called "{slug}-special.php" then specify "special".

params.name: slug
params.description: The slug name for the generic template.

params.name: name
params.description: Optional. The name of the specialized template. Default null.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments passed to the template. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Void on success, false if the template does not exist.

name: get_term
description: Gets all term data from database by term ID. The usage of the get_term function is to apply filters to a term object. It is possible to get a term object from the database before applying the filters. $term ID must be part of $taxonomy, to get from the database. Failure, might be able to be captured by the hooks. Failure would be the same value as $wpdb returns for the get_row method. There are two hooks, one is specifically for each term, named 'get_term', and the second is for the taxonomy name, 'term_$taxonomy'. Both hooks gets the term object, and the taxonomy name as parameters. Both hooks are expected to return a term object. {@see 'get_term'} hook - Takes two parameters the term Object and the taxonomy name. Must return term object. Used in get_term() as a catch-all filter for every $term. {@see 'get_$taxonomy'} hook - Takes two parameters the term Object and the taxonomy name. Must return term object. $taxonomy will be the taxonomy name, so for example, if 'category', it would be 'get_category' as the filter name. Useful for custom taxonomies or plugging into default taxonomies.

params.name: term
params.description: If integer, term data will be fetched from the database, or from the cache if available. If stdClass object (as in the results of a database query), will apply filters and return a `WP_Term` object with the `$term` data. If `WP_Term`, will return `$term`.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy name that `$term` is part of.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The required return type. One of OBJECT, ARRAY_A, or ARRAY_N, which correspond to a WP_Term object, an associative array, or a numeric array, respectively. Default OBJECT.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional. How to sanitize term fields. Default 'raw'.

returnDescription: WP_Term instance (or array) on success, depending on the `$output` value. WP_Error if `$taxonomy` does not exist. Null for miscellaneous failure.

name: get_term_by
description: Gets all term data from database by term field and data. Warning: $value is not escaped for 'name' $field. You must do it yourself, if required. The default $field is 'id', therefore it is possible to also use null for field, but not recommended that you do so. If $value does not exist, the return value will be false. If $taxonomy exists and $field and $value combinations exist, the term will be returned. This function will always return the first term that matches the `$field`- `$value`-`$taxonomy` combination specified in the parameters. If your query is likely to match more than one term (as is likely to be the case when `$field` is 'name', for example), consider using get_terms() instead; that way, you will get all matching terms, and can provide your own logic for deciding which one was intended.

params.name: field
params.description: Either 'slug', 'name', 'term_id' (or 'id', 'ID'), or 'term_taxonomy_id'.

params.name: value
params.description: Search for this term value.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name. Optional, if `$field` is 'term_taxonomy_id'.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The required return type. One of OBJECT, ARRAY_A, or ARRAY_N, which correspond to a WP_Term object, an associative array, or a numeric array, respectively. Default OBJECT.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional. How to sanitize term fields. Default 'raw'.

returnDescription: WP_Term instance (or array) on success, depending on the `$output` value. False if `$taxonomy` does not exist or `$term` was not found.

name: get_term_children
description: Merges all term children into a single array of their IDs. This recursive function will merge all of the children of $term into the same array of term IDs. Only useful for taxonomies which are hierarchical. Will return an empty array if $term does not exist in $taxonomy.

params.name: term_id
params.description: ID of term to get children.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

returnDescription: List of term IDs. WP_Error returned if `$taxonomy` does not exist.

name: get_term_feed_link
description: Retrieves the feed link for a term. Returns a link to the feed for all posts in a given term. A specific feed can be requested or left blank to get the default feed.

params.name: term
params.description: The ID or term object whose feed link will be retrieved.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy of `$term_id`.

params.name: feed
params.description: Optional. Feed type. Possible values include 'rss2', 'atom'. Default is the value of get_default_feed().

returnDescription: Link to the feed for the term specified by `$term` and `$taxonomy`.

name: get_term_field
description: Gets sanitized term field. The function is for contextual reasons and for simplicity of usage.

params.name: field
params.description: Term field to fetch.

params.name: term
params.description: Term ID or object.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy name. Default empty.

params.name: context
params.description: Optional. How to sanitize term fields. Look at sanitize_term_field() for available options. Default 'display'.

returnDescription: Will return an empty string if $term is not an object or if $field is not set in $term.

name: get_term_link
description: Generates a permalink for a taxonomy term archive.

params.name: term
params.description: The term object, ID, or slug whose link will be retrieved.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy. Default empty.

returnDescription: URL of the taxonomy term archive on success, WP_Error if term does not exist.

name: get_term_meta
description: Retrieves metadata for a term.

params.name: term_id
params.description: Term ID.

params.name: key
params.description: Optional. The meta key to retrieve. By default, returns data for all keys. Default empty.

params.name: single
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a single value. This parameter has no effect if `$key` is not specified. Default false.

returnDescription: An array of values if `$single` is false. The value of the meta field if `$single` is true. False for an invalid `$term_id` (non-numeric, zero, or negative value). An empty array if a valid but non-existing term ID is passed and `$single` is false. An empty string if a valid but non-existing term ID is passed and `$single` is true. Note: Non-serialized values are returned as strings: - false values are returned as empty strings ('') - true values are returned as '1' - numbers are returned as strings Arrays and objects retain their original type.

name: get_term_parents_list
description: Retrieves term parents with separator.

params.name: term_id
params.description: Term ID.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

params.name: args
params.description: { Array of optional arguments. @type string $format Use term names or slugs for display. Accepts 'name' or 'slug'. Default 'name'. @type string $separator Separator for between the terms. Default '/'. @type bool $link Whether to format as a link. Default true. @type bool $inclusive Include the term to get the parents for. Default true. }

returnDescription: A list of term parents on success, WP_Error or empty string on failure.

name: get_term_to_edit
description: Sanitizes term for editing. Return value is sanitize_term() and usage is for sanitizing the term for editing. Function is for contextual and simplicity.

params.name: id
params.description: Term ID or object.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

returnDescription: Will return empty string if $term is not an object.

name: get_terms
description: Retrieves the terms in a given taxonomy or list of taxonomies. You can fully inject any customizations to the query before it is sent, as well as control the output with a filter. The return type varies depending on the value passed to `$args['fields']`. See WP_Term_Query::get_terms() for details. In all cases, a `WP_Error` object will be returned if an invalid taxonomy is requested. The {@see 'get_terms'} filter will be called when the cache has the term and will pass the found term along with the array of $taxonomies and array of $args. This filter is also called before the array of terms is passed and will pass the array of terms, along with the $taxonomies and $args. The {@see 'list_terms_exclusions'} filter passes the compiled exclusions along with the $args. The {@see 'get_terms_orderby'} filter passes the `ORDER BY` clause for the query along with the $args array. Taxonomy or an array of taxonomies should be passed via the 'taxonomy' argument in the `$args` array: $terms = get_terms( array( 'taxonomy' => 'post_tag', 'hide_empty' => false, ) ); Prior to 4.5.0, taxonomy was passed as the first parameter of `get_terms()`. {@internal The `$deprecated` parameter is parsed for backward compatibility only.}

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Array or string of arguments. See WP_Term_Query::__construct() for information on accepted arguments. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Array of terms, a count thereof as a numeric string, or WP_Error if any of the taxonomies do not exist. See the function description for more information.

name: get_terms_to_edit
description: Gets comma-separated list of terms available to edit for the given post ID.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. The taxonomy for which to retrieve terms. Default 'post_tag'.

name: get_the_archive_description
description: Retrieves the description for an author, post type, or term archive.
returnDescription: Archive description.

name: get_the_archive_title
description: Retrieves the archive title based on the queried object.
returnDescription: Archive title.

name: get_the_attachment_link
description: Retrieve HTML content of attachment image with link.

params.name: id
params.description: Optional. Post ID.

params.name: fullsize
params.description: Optional. Whether to use full size image. Default false.

params.name: max_dims
params.description: Optional. Max image dimensions.

params.name: permalink
params.description: Optional. Whether to include permalink to image. Default false.

name: get_the_author
description: Retrieves the author of the current post.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Deprecated.

returnDescription: The author's display name, empty string if unknown.

name: get_the_author_aim
description: Retrieve the AIM address of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's AIM address.

name: get_the_author_description
description: Retrieve the description of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's description.

name: get_the_author_email
description: Retrieve the email of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's username.

name: get_the_author_firstname
description: Retrieve the first name of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's first name.

name: get_the_author_icq
description: Retrieve the ICQ number of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's ICQ number.

name: get_the_author_ID
description: Retrieve the ID of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's ID.

name: get_the_author_lastname
description: Retrieve the last name of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's last name.

name: get_the_author_link
description: Retrieves either author's link or author's name. If the author has a home page set, return an HTML link, otherwise just return the author's name.
returnDescription: An HTML link if the author's URL exists in user meta, otherwise the result of get_the_author().

name: get_the_author_login
description: Retrieve the login name of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's login name (username).

name: get_the_author_meta
description: Retrieves the requested data of the author of the current post. Valid values for the `$field` parameter include: - admin_color - comment_shortcuts - description - display_name - first_name - ID - last_name - nickname - plugins_last_view - plugins_per_page - rich_editing - syntax_highlighting - user_activation_key - user_description - user_email - user_firstname - user_lastname - user_level - user_login - user_nicename - user_pass - user_registered - user_status - user_url

params.name: field
params.description: Optional. The user field to retrieve. Default empty.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. User ID. Defaults to the current post author.

returnDescription: The author's field from the current author's DB object, otherwise an empty string.

name: get_the_author_msn
description: Retrieve the MSN address of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's MSN address.

name: get_the_author_nickname
description: Retrieve the nickname of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's nickname.

name: get_the_author_posts
description: Retrieves the number of posts by the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The number of posts by the author.

name: get_the_author_posts_link
description: Retrieves an HTML link to the author page of the current post's author. Returns an HTML-formatted link using get_author_posts_url().
returnDescription: An HTML link to the author page, or an empty string if $authordata is not set.

name: get_the_author_url
description: Retrieve the URL to the home page of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The URL to the author's page.

name: get_the_author_yim
description: Retrieve the Yahoo! IM name of the author of the current post.
returnDescription: The author's Yahoo! IM name.

name: get_the_block_template_html
description: Returns the markup for the current template.
returnDescription: Block template markup.

name: get_the_category
description: Retrieves post categories. This tag may be used outside The Loop by passing a post ID as the parameter. Note: This function only returns results from the default "category" taxonomy. For custom taxonomies use get_the_terms().

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. The post ID. Defaults to current post ID.

returnDescription: Array of WP_Term objects, one for each category assigned to the post.

name: get_the_category_by_ID
description: Retrieves category name based on category ID.

params.name: cat_id
params.description: Category ID.

returnDescription: Category name on success, WP_Error on failure.

name: get_the_category_list
description: Retrieves category list for a post in either HTML list or custom format. Generally used for quick, delimited (e.g. comma-separated) lists of categories, as part of a post entry meta. For a more powerful, list-based function, see wp_list_categories().

params.name: separator
params.description: Optional. Separator between the categories. By default, the links are placed in an unordered list. An empty string will result in the default behavior.

params.name: parents
params.description: Optional. How to display the parents. Accepts 'multiple', 'single', or empty. Default empty string.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. ID of the post to retrieve categories for. Defaults to the current post.

returnDescription: Category list for a post.

name: get_the_category_rss
description: Retrieves all of the post categories, formatted for use in feeds. All of the categories for the current post in the feed loop, will be retrieved and have feed markup added, so that they can easily be added to the RSS2, Atom, or RSS1 and RSS0.91 RDF feeds.

params.name: type
params.description: Optional, default is the type returned by get_default_feed().

returnDescription: All of the post categories for displaying in the feed.

name: get_the_comments_navigation
description: Retrieves navigation to next/previous set of comments, when applicable.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Default comments navigation arguments. @type string $prev_text Anchor text to display in the previous comments link. Default 'Older comments'. @type string $next_text Anchor text to display in the next comments link. Default 'Newer comments'. @type string $screen_reader_text Screen reader text for the nav element. Default 'Comments navigation'. @type string $aria_label ARIA label text for the nav element. Default 'Comments'. @type string $class Custom class for the nav element. Default 'comment-navigation'. }

returnDescription: Markup for comments links.

name: get_the_comments_pagination
description: Retrieves a paginated navigation to next/previous set of comments, when applicable.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Default pagination arguments. @type string $screen_reader_text Screen reader text for the nav element. Default 'Comments pagination'. @type string $aria_label ARIA label text for the nav element. Default 'Comments pagination'. @type string $class Custom class for the nav element. Default 'comments-pagination'. }

returnDescription: Markup for pagination links.

name: get_the_content
description: Retrieves the post content.

params.name: more_link_text
params.description: Optional. Content for when there is more text.

params.name: strip_teaser
params.description: Optional. Strip teaser content before the more text. Default false.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. WP_Post instance or Post ID/object. Default null.

name: get_the_content_feed
description: Retrieves the post content for feeds.

params.name: feed_type
params.description: The type of feed. rss2 | atom | rss | rdf

returnDescription: The filtered content.

name: get_the_date
description: Retrieves the date of the post. Unlike the_date() this function will always return the date. Modify output with the {@see 'get_the_date'} filter.

params.name: format
params.description: Optional. PHP date format. Defaults to the 'date_format' option.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default current post.

returnDescription: Date the current post was written. False on failure.

name: get_the_excerpt
description: Retrieves the post excerpt.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

returnDescription: Post excerpt.

name: get_the_generator
description: Creates the generator XML or Comment for RSS, ATOM, etc. Returns the correct generator type for the requested output format. Allows for a plugin to filter generators on an individual basis using the {@see 'get_the_generator_$type'} filter.

params.name: type
params.description: The type of generator to return - (html|xhtml|atom|rss2|rdf|comment|export).

returnDescription: The HTML content for the generator.

name: get_the_guid
description: Retrieves the Post Global Unique Identifier (guid). The guid will appear to be a link, but should not be used as an link to the post. The reason you should not use it as a link, is because of moving the blog across domains.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Default is global $post.

name: get_the_ID
description: Retrieves the ID of the current item in the WordPress Loop.
returnDescription: The ID of the current item in the WordPress Loop. False if $post is not set.

name: get_the_modified_author
description: Retrieves the author who last edited the current post.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Default is global `$post` object.

returnDescription: The author's display name. Empty string if user is unavailable. Null if there was no last editor or the post is invalid.

name: get_the_modified_date
description: Retrieves the date on which the post was last modified.

params.name: format
params.description: Optional. PHP date format. Defaults to the 'date_format' option.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default current post.

returnDescription: Date the current post was modified. False on failure.

name: get_the_modified_time
description: Retrieves the time at which the post was last modified.

params.name: format
params.description: Optional. Format to use for retrieving the time the post was modified. Accepts 'G', 'U', or PHP date format. Defaults to the 'time_format' option.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default current post.

returnDescription: Formatted date string or Unix timestamp. False on failure.

name: get_the_password_form
description: Retrieves protected post password form content.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

returnDescription: HTML content for password form for password-protected post.

name: get_the_permalink
description: Retrieves the full permalink for the current post or post ID. This function is an alias for get_permalink().

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Default is the global `$post`.

params.name: leavename
params.description: Optional. Whether to keep post name or page name. Default false.

returnDescription: The permalink URL. False if the post does not exist.

name: get_the_post_navigation
description: Retrieves the navigation to next/previous post, when applicable.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Default post navigation arguments. Default empty array. @type string $prev_text Anchor text to display in the previous post link. Default '%title'. @type string $next_text Anchor text to display in the next post link. Default '%title'. @type bool $in_same_term Whether link should be in the same taxonomy term. Default false. @type int[]|string $excluded_terms Array or comma-separated list of excluded term IDs. Default empty. @type string $taxonomy Taxonomy, if `$in_same_term` is true. Default 'category'. @type string $screen_reader_text Screen reader text for the nav element. Default 'Post navigation'. @type string $aria_label ARIA label text for the nav element. Default 'Posts'. @type string $class Custom class for the nav element. Default 'post-navigation'. }

returnDescription: Markup for post links.

name: get_the_post_thumbnail
description: Retrieves the post thumbnail. When a theme adds 'post-thumbnail' support, a special 'post-thumbnail' image size is registered, which differs from the 'thumbnail' image size managed via the Settings > Media screen. When using the_post_thumbnail() or related functions, the 'post-thumbnail' image size is used by default, though a different size can be specified instead as needed.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global `$post`.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'post-thumbnail'.

params.name: attr
params.description: Optional. Query string or array of attributes. Default empty.

returnDescription: The post thumbnail image tag.

name: get_the_post_thumbnail_caption
description: Returns the post thumbnail caption.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global `$post`.

returnDescription: Post thumbnail caption.

name: get_the_post_thumbnail_url
description: Returns the post thumbnail URL.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global `$post`.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Registered image size to retrieve the source for or a flat array of height and width dimensions. Default 'post-thumbnail'.

returnDescription: Post thumbnail URL or false if no image is available. If `$size` does not match any registered image size, the original image URL will be returned.

name: get_the_post_type_description
description: Retrieves the description for a post type archive.
returnDescription: The post type description.

name: get_the_posts_navigation
description: Returns the navigation to next/previous set of posts, when applicable.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Default posts navigation arguments. Default empty array. @type string $prev_text Anchor text to display in the previous posts link. Default 'Older posts'. @type string $next_text Anchor text to display in the next posts link. Default 'Newer posts'. @type string $screen_reader_text Screen reader text for the nav element. Default 'Posts navigation'. @type string $aria_label ARIA label text for the nav element. Default 'Posts'. @type string $class Custom class for the nav element. Default 'posts-navigation'. }

returnDescription: Markup for posts links.

name: get_the_posts_pagination
description: Retrieves a paginated navigation to next/previous set of posts, when applicable.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Default pagination arguments, see paginate_links(). @type string $screen_reader_text Screen reader text for navigation element. Default 'Posts pagination'. @type string $aria_label ARIA label text for the nav element. Default 'Posts pagination'. @type string $class Custom class for the nav element. Default 'pagination'. }

returnDescription: Markup for pagination links.

name: get_the_privacy_policy_link
description: Returns the privacy policy link with formatting, when applicable.

params.name: before
params.description: Optional. Display before privacy policy link. Default empty.

params.name: after
params.description: Optional. Display after privacy policy link. Default empty.

returnDescription: Markup for the link and surrounding elements. Empty string if it doesn't exist.

name: get_the_tag_list
description: Retrieves the tags for a post formatted as a string.

params.name: before
params.description: Optional. String to use before the tags. Default empty.

params.name: sep
params.description: Optional. String to use between the tags. Default empty.

params.name: after
params.description: Optional. String to use after the tags. Default empty.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. Post ID. Defaults to the current post ID.

returnDescription: A list of tags on success, false if there are no terms, WP_Error on failure.

name: get_the_tags
description: Retrieves the tags for a post.

params.name: post
params.description: Post ID or object.

returnDescription: Array of WP_Term objects on success, false if there are no terms or the post does not exist, WP_Error on failure.

name: get_the_taxonomies
description: Retrieves all taxonomies associated with a post. This function can be used within the loop. It will also return an array of the taxonomies with links to the taxonomy and name.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Arguments about how to format the list of taxonomies. Default empty array. @type string $template Template for displaying a taxonomy label and list of terms. Default is "Label: Terms." @type string $term_template Template for displaying a single term in the list. Default is the term name linked to its archive. }

returnDescription: List of taxonomies.

name: get_the_term_list
description: Retrieves a post's terms as a list with specified format. Terms are linked to their respective term listing pages.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Post ID.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

params.name: before
params.description: Optional. String to use before the terms. Default empty.

params.name: sep
params.description: Optional. String to use between the terms. Default empty.

params.name: after
params.description: Optional. String to use after the terms. Default empty.

returnDescription: A list of terms on success, false if there are no terms, WP_Error on failure.

name: get_the_terms
description: Retrieves the terms of the taxonomy that are attached to the post.

params.name: post
params.description: Post ID or object.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

returnDescription: Array of WP_Term objects on success, false if there are no terms or the post does not exist, WP_Error on failure.

name: get_the_time
description: Retrieves the time of the post.

params.name: format
params.description: Optional. Format to use for retrieving the time the post was written. Accepts 'G', 'U', or PHP date format. Defaults to the 'time_format' option.

params.name: post
params.description: Post ID or post object. Default is global `$post` object.

returnDescription: Formatted date string or Unix timestamp if `$format` is 'U' or 'G'. False on failure.

name: get_the_title
description: Retrieves the post title. If the post is protected and the visitor is not an admin, then "Protected" will be inserted before the post title. If the post is private, then "Private" will be inserted before the post title.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

name: get_the_title_rss
description: Retrieves the current post title for the feed.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

returnDescription: Current post title.

name: get_theme
description: Retrieve theme data.

params.name: theme
params.description: Theme name.

returnDescription: Null, if theme name does not exist. Theme data, if exists.

name: get_theme_data
description: Retrieve theme data from parsed theme file.

params.name: theme_file
params.description: Theme file path.

returnDescription: Theme data.

name: get_theme_feature_list
description: Retrieves list of WordPress theme features (aka theme tags).

params.name: api
params.description: Optional. Whether try to fetch tags from the WordPress.org API. Defaults to true.

returnDescription: Array of features keyed by category with translations keyed by slug.

name: get_theme_file_path
description: Retrieves the path of a file in the theme. Searches in the stylesheet directory before the template directory so themes which inherit from a parent theme can just override one file.

params.name: file
params.description: Optional. File to search for in the stylesheet directory.

returnDescription: The path of the file.

name: get_theme_file_uri
description: Retrieves the URL of a file in the theme. Searches in the stylesheet directory before the template directory so themes which inherit from a parent theme can just override one file.

params.name: file
params.description: Optional. File to search for in the stylesheet directory.

returnDescription: The URL of the file.

name: get_theme_mod
description: Retrieves theme modification value for the active theme. If the modification name does not exist and `$default_value` is a string, then the default will be passed through the {@link https://www.php.net/sprintf sprintf()} PHP function with the template directory URI as the first value and the stylesheet directory URI as the second value.

params.name: name
params.description: Theme modification name.

params.name: default_value
params.description: Optional. Theme modification default value. Default false.

returnDescription: Theme modification value.

name: get_theme_mods
description: Retrieves all theme modifications.
returnDescription: Theme modifications.

name: get_theme_root
description: Retrieves path to themes directory. Does not have trailing slash.

params.name: stylesheet_or_template
params.description: Optional. The stylesheet or template name of the theme. Default is to leverage the main theme root.

returnDescription: Themes directory path.

name: get_theme_root_uri
description: Retrieves URI for themes directory. Does not have trailing slash.

params.name: stylesheet_or_template
params.description: Optional. The stylesheet or template name of the theme. Default is to leverage the main theme root.

params.name: theme_root
params.description: Optional. The theme root for which calculations will be based, preventing the need for a get_raw_theme_root() call. Default empty.

returnDescription: Themes directory URI.

name: get_theme_roots
description: Retrieves theme roots.
returnDescription: An array of theme roots keyed by template/stylesheet or a single theme root if all themes have the same root.

name: get_theme_starter_content
description: Expands a theme's starter content configuration using core-provided data.
returnDescription: Array of starter content.

name: get_theme_support
description: Gets the theme support arguments passed when registering that support. Example usage: get_theme_support( 'custom-logo' ); get_theme_support( 'custom-header', 'width' );

params.name: feature
params.description: The feature to check. See add_theme_support() for the list of possible values.

returnDescription: The array of extra arguments or the value for the registered feature.

name: get_theme_update_available
description: Retrieves the update link if there is a theme update available. Will return a link if there is an update available.

params.name: theme
params.description: WP_Theme object.

returnDescription: HTML for the update link, or false if invalid info was passed.

name: get_theme_updates
description: Retrieves themes with updates available.

name: get_themes
description: Retrieve list of themes with theme data in theme directory. The theme is broken, if it doesn't have a parent theme and is missing either style.css and, or index.php. If the theme has a parent theme then it is broken, if it is missing style.css; index.php is optional.
returnDescription: Theme list with theme data.

name: get_to_ping
description: Retrieves URLs that need to be pinged.

params.name: post
params.description: Post ID or post object.

returnDescription: List of URLs yet to ping.

name: get_trackback_url
description: Retrieves the current post's trackback URL. There is a check to see if permalink's have been enabled and if so, will retrieve the pretty path. If permalinks weren't enabled, the ID of the current post is used and appended to the correct page to go to.
returnDescription: The trackback URL after being filtered.

name: get_transient
description: Retrieves the value of a transient. If the transient does not exist, does not have a value, or has expired, then the return value will be false.

params.name: transient
params.description: Transient name. Expected to not be SQL-escaped.

returnDescription: Value of transient.

name: get_translations_for_domain
description: Returns the Translations instance for a text domain. If there isn't one, returns empty Translations instance.

params.name: domain
params.description: Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings.

returnDescription: A Translations instance.

name: get_typography_classes_for_block_core_search
description: Returns typography classnames depending on whether there are named font sizes/families.

params.name: attributes
params.description: The block attributes.

returnDescription: The typography color classnames to be applied to the block elements.

name: get_typography_styles_for_block_core_search
description: Returns typography styles to be included in an HTML style tag. This excludes text-decoration, which is applied only to the label and button elements of the search block.

params.name: attributes
params.description: The block attributes.

returnDescription: A string of typography CSS declarations.

name: get_udims
description: Calculates the new dimensions for a downsampled image.

params.name: width
params.description: Current width of the image

params.name: height
params.description: Current height of the image

returnDescription: Shrunk dimensions (width, height).

name: get_upload_iframe_src
description: Retrieves the upload iframe source URL.

params.name: type
params.description: Media type.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Post ID.

params.name: tab
params.description: Media upload tab.

returnDescription: Upload iframe source URL.

name: get_upload_space_available
description: Determines if there is any upload space left in the current blog's quota.
returnDescription: of upload space available in bytes.

name: get_uploaded_header_images
description: Gets the header images uploaded for the active theme.

name: get_url_in_content
description: Extracts and returns the first URL from passed content.

params.name: content
params.description: A string which might contain an `A` element with a non-empty `href` attribute.

returnDescription: Database-escaped URL via {@see esc_url()} if found, otherwise `false`.

name: get_user
description: Retrieves user info by user ID.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

returnDescription: WP_User object on success, false on failure.

name: get_user_by
description: Retrieves user info by a given field.

params.name: value
params.description: A value for $field. A user ID, slug, email address, or login name.

name: get_user_meta
description: Retrieves user meta field for a user.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

params.name: key
params.description: Optional. The meta key to retrieve. By default, returns data for all keys.

params.name: single
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a single value. This parameter has no effect if `$key` is not specified. Default false.

returnDescription: An array of values if `$single` is false. The value of meta data field if `$single` is true. False for an invalid `$user_id` (non-numeric, zero, or negative value). An empty array if a valid but non-existing user ID is passed and `$single` is false. An empty string if a valid but non-existing user ID is passed and `$single` is true. Note: Non-serialized values are returned as strings: - false values are returned as empty strings ('') - true values are returned as '1' - numbers (both integer and float) are returned as strings Arrays and objects retain their original type.

name: get_user_option

The option will first check for the per site name and then the per Network name.

name: has_action
description: Checks if any action has been registered for a hook. When using the `$callback` argument, this function may return a non-boolean value that evaluates to false (e.g. 0), so use the `===` operator for testing the return value.

params.name: hook_name
params.description: The name of the action hook.

params.name: callback
params.description: Optional. The callback to check for. This function can be called unconditionally to speculatively check a callback that may or may not exist. Default false.

params.name: priority
params.description: Optional. The specific priority at which to check for the callback. Default false.

returnDescription: If `$callback` is omitted, returns boolean for whether the hook has anything registered. When checking a specific function, the priority of that hook is returned, or false if the function is not attached. If `$callback` and `$priority` are both provided, a boolean is returned for whether the specific function is registered at that priority.

name: has_block
description: Determines whether a $post or a string contains a specific block type. This test optimizes for performance rather than strict accuracy, detecting whether the block type exists but not validating its structure and not checking synced patterns (formerly called reusable blocks). For strict accuracy, you should use the block parser on post content.

params.name: block_name
params.description: Full block type to look for.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post content, post ID, or post object. Defaults to global $post.

returnDescription: Whether the post content contains the specified block.

name: has_blocks
description: Determines whether a post or content string has blocks. This test optimizes for performance rather than strict accuracy, detecting the pattern of a block but not validating its structure. For strict accuracy, you should use the block parser on post content.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post content, post ID, or post object. Defaults to global $post.

returnDescription: Whether the post has blocks.

name: has_category
description: Checks if the current post has any of given category. The given categories are checked against the post's categories' term_ids, names and slugs. Categories given as integers will only be checked against the post's categories' term_ids. If no categories are given, determines if post has any categories.

params.name: category
params.description: Optional. The category name/term_id/slug, or an array of them to check for. Default empty.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post to check. Defaults to the current post.

returnDescription: True if the current post has any of the given categories (or any category, if no category specified). False otherwise.

name: has_custom_header
description: Checks whether a custom header is set or not.
returnDescription: True if a custom header is set. False if not.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

returnDescription: True if the post has a custom excerpt, false otherwise.

name: has_filter

params.name: hook_name
params.description: The name of the filter hook.

params.name: callback
params.description: Optional. The callback to check for. This function can be called unconditionally to speculatively check a callback that may or may not exist. Default false.

params.name: priority
params.description: Optional. The specific priority at which to check for the callback. Default false.

returnDescription: If `$callback` is omitted, returns boolean for whether the hook has anything registered. When checking a specific function, the priority of that hook is returned, or false if the function is not attached. If `$callback` and `$priority` are both provided, a boolean is returned for whether the specific function is registered at that priority.

name: image_constrain_size_for_editor
description: Scales down the default size of an image. This is so that the image is a better fit for the editor and theme. The `$size` parameter accepts either an array or a string. The supported string values are 'thumb' or 'thumbnail' for the given thumbnail size or defaults at 128 width and 96 height in pixels. Also supported for the string value is 'medium', 'medium_large' and 'full'. The 'full' isn't actually supported, but any value other than the supported will result in the content_width size or 500 if that is not set. Finally, there is a filter named {@see 'editor_max_image_size'}, that will be called on the calculated array for width and height, respectively.

params.name: width
params.description: Width of the image in pixels.

params.name: height
params.description: Height of the image in pixels.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'medium'.

params.name: context
params.description: Optional. Could be 'display' (like in a theme) or 'edit' (like inserting into an editor). Default null.

returnDescription: { An array of width and height values. @type int $0 The maximum width in pixels. @type int $1 The maximum height in pixels. }

name: image_downsize
description: Scales an image to fit a particular size (such as 'thumb' or 'medium'). The URL might be the original image, or it might be a resized version. This function won't create a new resized copy, it will just return an already resized one if it exists. A plugin may use the {@see 'image_downsize'} filter to hook into and offer image resizing services for images. The hook must return an array with the same elements that are normally returned from the function.

params.name: id
params.description: Attachment ID for image.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'medium'.

returnDescription: { Array of image data, or boolean false if no image is available. @type string $0 Image source URL. @type int $1 Image width in pixels. @type int $2 Image height in pixels. @type bool $3 Whether the image is a resized image. }

name: image_edit_apply_changes
description: Performs group of changes on Editor specified.

params.name: image
params.description: WP_Image_Editor instance.

params.name: changes
params.description: Array of change operations.

returnDescription: WP_Image_Editor instance with changes applied.

name: image_get_intermediate_size
description: Retrieves the image's intermediate size (resized) path, width, and height. The $size parameter can be an array with the width and height respectively. If the size matches the 'sizes' metadata array for width and height, then it will be used. If there is no direct match, then the nearest image size larger than the specified size will be used. If nothing is found, then the function will break out and return false. The metadata 'sizes' is used for compatible sizes that can be used for the parameter $size value. The url path will be given, when the $size parameter is a string. If you are passing an array for the $size, you should consider using add_image_size() so that a cropped version is generated. It's much more efficient than having to find the closest-sized image and then having the browser scale down the image.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Attachment ID.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'thumbnail'.

returnDescription: { Array of file relative path, width, and height on success. Additionally includes absolute path and URL if registered size is passed to `$size` parameter. False on failure. @type string $file Filename of image. @type int $width Width of image in pixels. @type int $height Height of image in pixels. @type string $path Path of image relative to uploads directory. @type string $url URL of image. }

name: image_hwstring
description: Retrieves width and height attributes using given width and height values. Both attributes are required in the sense that both parameters must have a value, but are optional in that if you set them to false or null, then they will not be added to the returned string. You can set the value using a string, but it will only take numeric values. If you wish to put 'px' after the numbers, then it will be stripped out of the return.

params.name: width
params.description: Image width in pixels.

params.name: height
params.description: Image height in pixels.

returnDescription: HTML attributes for width and, or height.

name: image_link_input_fields
description: Retrieves HTML for the Link URL buttons with the default link type as specified.

name: image_make_intermediate_size
description: Resizes an image to make a thumbnail or intermediate size. The returned array has the file size, the image width, and image height. The {@see 'image_make_intermediate_size'} filter can be used to hook in and change the values of the returned array. The only parameter is the resized file path.

params.name: file
params.description: File path.

params.name: width
params.description: Image width.

params.name: height
params.description: Image height.

params.name: crop
params.description: { Optional. Image cropping behavior. If false, the image will be scaled (default). If true, image will be cropped to the specified dimensions using center positions. If an array, the image will be cropped using the array to specify the crop location: @type string $0 The x crop position. Accepts 'left', 'center', or 'right'. @type string $1 The y crop position. Accepts 'top', 'center', or 'bottom'. }

returnDescription: Metadata array on success. False if no image was created.

name: image_media_send_to_editor

name: image_resize
description: Scale down an image to fit a particular size and save a new copy of the image. The PNG transparency will be preserved using the function, as well as the image type. If the file going in is PNG, then the resized image is going to be PNG. The only supported image types are PNG, GIF, and JPEG. Some functionality requires API to exist, so some PHP version may lose out support. This is not the fault of WordPress (where functionality is downgraded, not actual defects), but of your PHP version.

params.name: file
params.description: Image file path.

params.name: max_w
params.description: Maximum width to resize to.

params.name: max_h
params.description: Maximum height to resize to.

params.name: crop
params.description: Optional. Whether to crop image or resize. Default false.

params.name: suffix
params.description: Optional. File suffix. Default null.

params.name: dest_path
params.description: Optional. New image file path. Default null.

params.name: jpeg_quality
params.description: Optional. Image quality percentage. Default 90.

returnDescription: WP_Error on failure. String with new destination path.

name: image_resize_dimensions
description: Retrieves calculated resize dimensions for use in WP_Image_Editor. Calculates dimensions and coordinates for a resized image that fits within a specified width and height.

params.description: Original width in pixels.

params.name: orig_h
params.description: Original height in pixels.

params.name: dest_w
params.description: New width in pixels.

params.name: dest_h
params.description: New height in pixels.

params.name: crop
params.description: { Optional. Image cropping behavior. If false, the image will be scaled (default). If true, image will be cropped to the specified dimensions using center positions. If an array, the image will be cropped using the array to specify the crop location: @type string $0 The x crop position. Accepts 'left', 'center', or 'right'. @type string $1 The y crop position. Accepts 'top', 'center', or 'bottom'. }

returnDescription: Returned array matches parameters for `imagecopyresampled()`. False on failure.
