name: img_caption_shortcode
description: Builds the Caption shortcode output. Allows a plugin to replace the content that would otherwise be returned. The filter is {@see 'img_caption_shortcode'} and passes an empty string, the attr parameter and the content parameter values. The supported attributes for the shortcode are 'id', 'caption_id', 'align', 'width', 'caption', and 'class'.

params.name: attr
params.description: { Attributes of the caption shortcode. @type string $id ID of the image and caption container element, i.e. ` ` or ` `. @type string $caption_id ID of the caption element, i.e. ` ` or ` `. @type string $align Class name that aligns the caption. Default 'alignnone'. Accepts 'alignleft', 'aligncenter', alignright', 'alignnone'. @type int $width The width of the caption, in pixels. @type string $caption The caption text. @type string $class Additional class name(s) added to the caption container. }

params.name: content
params.description: Optional. Shortcode content. Default empty string.

returnDescription: HTML content to display the caption.

name: in_category
description: Checks if the current post is within any of the given categories. The given categories are checked against the post's categories' term_ids, names and slugs. Categories given as integers will only be checked against the post's categories' term_ids. Prior to v2.5 of WordPress, category names were not supported. Prior to v2.7, category slugs were not supported. Prior to v2.7, only one category could be compared: in_category( $single_category ). Prior to v2.7, this function could only be used in the WordPress Loop. As of 2.7, the function can be used anywhere if it is provided a post ID or post object. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: category
params.description: Category ID, name, slug, or array of such to check against.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post to check. Defaults to the current post.

returnDescription: True if the current post is in any of the given categories.

name: in_the_loop
description: Determines whether the caller is in the Loop. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: True if caller is within loop, false if loop hasn't started or ended.

name: includes_url
description: Retrieves the URL to the includes directory.

params.name: path
params.description: Optional. Path relative to the includes URL. Default empty.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Optional. Scheme to give the includes URL context. Accepts 'http', 'https', or 'relative'. Default null.

returnDescription: Includes URL link with optional path appended.

name: index_rel_link
description: Display relational link for the site index.

name: inject_ignored_hooked_blocks_metadata_attributes
description: Inject ignoredHookedBlocks metadata attributes into a template or template part. Given an object that represents a `wp_template` or `wp_template_part` post object prepared for inserting or updating the database, locate all blocks that have hooked blocks, and inject a `metadata.ignoredHookedBlocks` attribute into the anchor blocks to reflect the latter.

params.name: changes
params.description: An object representing a template or template part prepared for inserting or updating the database.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Deprecated. Not used.

returnDescription: The updated object representing a template or template part.

name: insert_blog
description: Store basic site info in the blogs table. This function creates a row in the wp_blogs table and returns the new blog's ID. It is the first step in creating a new blog.

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain of the new site.

params.name: path
params.description: The path of the new site.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Unless you're running a multi-network install, be sure to set this value to 1.

returnDescription: The ID of the new row

name: insert_hooked_blocks
description: Returns the markup for blocks hooked to the given anchor block in a specific relative position.

params.name: parsed_anchor_block
params.description: The anchor block, in parsed block array format.

params.name: relative_position
params.description: The relative position of the hooked blocks. Can be one of 'before', 'after', 'first_child', or 'last_child'.

params.name: hooked_blocks
params.description: An array of hooked block types, grouped by anchor block and relative position.

params.name: context
params.description: The block template, template part, or pattern that the anchor block belongs to.

name: insert_hooked_blocks_and_set_ignored_hooked_blocks_metadata
description: Returns the markup for blocks hooked to the given anchor block in a specific relative position and then adds a list of hooked block types to an anchor block's ignored hooked block types. This function is meant for internal use only.

params.name: parsed_anchor_block
params.description: The anchor block, in parsed block array format.

params.name: relative_position
params.description: The relative position of the hooked blocks. Can be one of 'before', 'after', 'first_child', or 'last_child'.

params.name: hooked_blocks
params.description: An array of hooked block types, grouped by anchor block and relative position.

params.name: context
params.description: The block template, template part, or pattern that the anchor block belongs to.

name: insert_hooked_blocks_into_rest_response
description: Hooks into the REST API response for the Posts endpoint and adds the first and last inner blocks.

params.name: response
params.description: The response object.

params.name: post
params.description: Post object.

returnDescription: The response object.

name: insert_with_markers
description: Inserts an array of strings into a file (.htaccess), placing it between BEGIN and END markers. Replaces existing marked info. Retains surrounding data. Creates file if none exists.

params.name: filename
params.description: Filename to alter.

params.name: marker
params.description: The marker to alter.

params.name: insertion
params.description: The new content to insert.

returnDescription: True on write success, false on failure.

name: install_blog
description: Install an empty blog. Creates the new blog tables and options. If calling this function directly, be sure to use switch_to_blog() first, so that $wpdb points to the new blog.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: The value returned by wp_insert_site().

params.name: blog_title
params.description: The title of the new site.

name: install_blog_defaults
description: Set blog defaults. This function creates a row in the wp_blogs table.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Ignored in this function.

name: install_dashboard
description: Displays the Featured tab of Add Plugins screen.

name: install_global_terms
description: Install global terms.

name: install_network
description: Install Network.

name: install_plugin_information
description: Displays plugin information in dialog box form.

name: install_plugin_install_status
description: Determines the status we can perform on a plugin.

params.name: api
params.description: Data about the plugin retrieved from the API.

params.name: loop
params.description: Optional. Disable further loops. Default false.

returnDescription: { Plugin installation status data. @type string $status Status of a plugin. Could be one of 'install', 'update_available', 'latest_installed' or 'newer_installed'. @type string $url Plugin installation URL. @type string $version The most recent version of the plugin. @type string $file Plugin filename relative to the plugins directory. }

name: install_plugins_favorites_form
description: Shows a username form for the favorites page.

name: install_plugins_upload
description: Displays a form to upload plugins from zip files.

name: install_popular_tags
description: Retrieves popular WordPress plugin tags.

name: install_search_form
description: Displays a search form for searching plugins.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Not used.

name: install_theme_information
description: Displays theme information in dialog box form.

name: install_theme_search_form
description: Displays search form for searching themes.

name: install_themes_dashboard
description: Displays tags filter for themes.

name: install_themes_feature_list
description: Retrieves the list of WordPress theme features (aka theme tags).

name: install_themes_upload
description: Displays a form to upload themes from zip files.

name: is_404
description: Determines whether the query has resulted in a 404 (returns no results). For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is a 404 error.

name: is_active_sidebar
description: Determines whether a sidebar contains widgets. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: index
params.description: Sidebar name, id or number to check.

returnDescription: True if the sidebar has widgets, false otherwise.

name: is_active_widget
description: Determines whether a given widget is displayed on the front end. Either $callback or $id_base can be used. $id_base is the first argument when extending WP_Widget class. Without the optional $widget_id parameter, returns the ID of the first sidebar in which the first instance of the widget with the given callback or $id_base is found. With the $widget_id parameter, returns the ID of the sidebar where the widget with that callback/$id_base AND that ID is found. NOTE: $widget_id and $id_base are the same for single widgets. To be effective this function has to run after widgets have initialized, at action {@see 'init'} or later. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: callback
params.description: Optional. Widget callback to check. Default false.

params.name: widget_id
params.description: Optional. Widget ID. Optional, but needed for checking. Default false.

params.name: id_base
params.description: Optional. The base ID of a widget created by extending WP_Widget. Default false.

params.name: skip_inactive
params.description: Optional. Whether to check in 'wp_inactive_widgets'. Default true.

returnDescription: ID of the sidebar in which the widget is active, false if the widget is not active.

name: is_admin
description: Determines whether the current request is for an administrative interface page. Does not check if the user is an administrator; use current_user_can() for checking roles and capabilities. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: True if inside WordPress administration interface, false otherwise.

name: is_admin_bar_showing
description: Determines whether the admin bar should be showing. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the admin bar should be showing.

name: is_allowed_http_origin
description: Determines if the HTTP origin is an authorized one.

params.name: origin
params.description: Origin URL. If not provided, the value of get_http_origin() is used.

returnDescription: Origin URL if allowed, empty string if not.

name: is_archive
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing archive page. Archive pages include category, tag, author, date, custom post type, and custom taxonomy based archives. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing archive page.

name: is_archived
description: Checks if a particular blog is archived.

params.name: id
params.description: Blog ID.

returnDescription: Whether the blog is archived or not.

name: is_attachment
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing attachment page. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: attachment
params.description: Optional. Attachment ID, title, slug, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing attachment page.

name: is_author
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing author archive page. If the $author parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the authors specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: author
params.description: Optional. User ID, nickname, nicename, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing author archive page.

name: is_avatar_comment_type
description: Check if this comment type allows avatars to be retrieved.

params.name: comment_type
params.description: Comment type to check.

returnDescription: Whether the comment type is allowed for retrieving avatars.

name: is_blog_admin
description: Determines whether the current request is for a site's administrative interface. e.g. `/wp-admin/` Does not check if the user is an administrator; use current_user_can() for checking roles and capabilities.
returnDescription: True if inside WordPress site administration pages.

name: is_blog_installed
description: Determines whether WordPress is already installed. The cache will be checked first. If you have a cache plugin, which saves the cache values, then this will work. If you use the default WordPress cache, and the database goes away, then you might have problems. Checks for the 'siteurl' option for whether WordPress is installed. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the site is already installed.

name: is_blog_user
description: Checks if the current user belong to a given site.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Site ID

returnDescription: True if the current users belong to $blog_id, false if not.

name: is_category
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing category archive page. If the $category parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the categories specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: category
params.description: Optional. Category ID, name, slug, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing category archive page.

name: is_child_theme
description: Whether a child theme is in use.
returnDescription: True if a child theme is in use, false otherwise.

name: is_comment_feed
description: Is the query for a comments feed?
returnDescription: Whether the query is for a comments feed.

name: is_comments_popup
description: Determines whether the current URL is within the comments popup window. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Always returns false.

name: is_customize_preview
description: Whether the site is being previewed in the Customizer.
returnDescription: True if the site is being previewed in the Customizer, false otherwise.

name: is_date
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing date archive. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing date archive.

name: is_day
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing day archive. A conditional check to test whether the page is a date-based archive page displaying posts for the current day. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing day archive.

name: is_dynamic_sidebar
description: Determines whether the dynamic sidebar is enabled and used by the theme. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: True if using widgets, false otherwise.

name: is_email
description: Verifies that an email is valid. Does not grok i18n domains. Not RFC compliant.

params.name: email
params.description: Email address to verify.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Deprecated.

returnDescription: Valid email address on success, false on failure.

name: is_email_address_unsafe
description: Checks an email address against a list of banned domains. This function checks against the Banned Email Domains list at wp-admin/network/settings.php. The check is only run on self-registrations; user creation at wp-admin/network/users.php bypasses this check.

params.name: user_email
params.description: The email provided by the user at registration.

returnDescription: True when the email address is banned, false otherwise.

name: is_embed
description: Is the query for an embedded post?
returnDescription: Whether the query is for an embedded post.

name: is_favicon
description: Is the query for the favicon.ico file?
returnDescription: Whether the query is for the favicon.ico file.

name: is_feed
description: Determines whether the query is for a feed. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: feeds
params.description: Optional. Feed type or array of feed types to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for a feed.

name: is_front_page
description: Determines whether the query is for the front page of the site. This is for what is displayed at your site's main URL. Depends on the site's "Front page displays" Reading Settings 'show_on_front' and 'page_on_front'. If you set a static page for the front page of your site, this function will return true when viewing that page. Otherwise the same as {@see is_home()}. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for the front page of the site.

name: is_gd_image
description: Determines whether the value is an acceptable type for GD image functions. In PHP 8.0, the GD extension uses GdImage objects for its data structures. This function checks if the passed value is either a GdImage object instance or a resource of type `gd`. Any other type will return false.

params.name: image
params.description: A value to check the type for.

returnDescription: True if `$image` is either a GD image resource or a GdImage instance, false otherwise.

name: is_header_video_active
description: Checks whether the custom header video is eligible to show on the current page.
returnDescription: True if the custom header video should be shown. False if not.

name: is_home
description: Determines whether the query is for the blog homepage. The blog homepage is the page that shows the time-based blog content of the site. is_home() is dependent on the site's "Front page displays" Reading Settings 'show_on_front' and 'page_for_posts'. If a static page is set for the front page of the site, this function will return true only on the page you set as the "Posts page". For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for the blog homepage.

name: is_lighttpd_before_150
description: Determines whether the server is running an earlier than 1.5.0 version of lighttpd.
returnDescription: Whether the server is running lighttpd < 1.5.0.

name: is_local_attachment
description: Determines whether an attachment URI is local and really an attachment. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: url
params.description: URL to check

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: is_locale_switched
description: Determines whether switch_to_locale() is in effect.
returnDescription: True if the locale has been switched, false otherwise.

name: is_login
description: Determines whether the current request is for the login screen.
returnDescription: True if inside WordPress login screen, false otherwise.

name: is_main_blog
description: Deprecated functionality to determine if the current site is the main site.

name: is_main_network
description: Determines whether a network is the main network of the Multisite installation.

params.name: network_id
params.description: Optional. Network ID to test. Defaults to current network.

returnDescription: True if $network_id is the main network, or if not running Multisite.

name: is_main_query
description: Determines whether the query is the main query. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is the main query.

name: is_main_site
description: Determines whether a site is the main site of the current network.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Optional. Site ID to test. Defaults to current site.

params.name: network_id
params.description: Optional. Network ID of the network to check for. Defaults to current network.

returnDescription: True if $site_id is the main site of the network, or if not running Multisite.

name: is_month
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing month archive. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing month archive.

name: is_multi_author
description: Determines whether this site has more than one author. Checks to see if more than one author has published posts. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether or not we have more than one author

name: is_multisite
description: Determines whether Multisite is enabled.
returnDescription: True if Multisite is enabled, false otherwise.

name: is_nav_menu
description: Determines whether the given ID is a navigation menu. Returns true if it is; false otherwise.

params.name: menu
params.description: Menu ID, slug, name, or object of menu to check.

returnDescription: Whether the menu exists.

name: is_nav_menu_item
description: Determines whether the given ID is a nav menu item.

params.name: menu_item_id
params.description: The ID of the potential nav menu item.

returnDescription: Whether the given ID is that of a nav menu item.

name: is_network_admin
description: Determines whether the current request is for the network administrative interface. e.g. `/wp-admin/network/` Does not check if the user is an administrator; use current_user_can() for checking roles and capabilities. Does not check if the site is a Multisite network; use is_multisite() for checking if Multisite is enabled.
returnDescription: True if inside WordPress network administration pages.

name: is_network_only_plugin
description: Checks for "Network: true" in the plugin header to see if this should be activated only as a network wide plugin. The plugin would also work when Multisite is not enabled. Checks for "Site Wide Only: true" for backward compatibility.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: True if plugin is network only, false otherwise.

name: is_new_day
description: Determines whether the publish date of the current post in the loop is different from the publish date of the previous post in the loop. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: 1 when new day, 0 if not a new day.

name: is_object_in_taxonomy
description: Determines if the given object type is associated with the given taxonomy.

params.name: object_type
params.description: Object type string.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Single taxonomy name.

returnDescription: True if object is associated with the taxonomy, otherwise false.

name: is_object_in_term
description: Determines if the given object is associated with any of the given terms. The given terms are checked against the object's terms' term_ids, names and slugs. Terms given as integers will only be checked against the object's terms' term_ids. If no terms are given, determines if object is associated with any terms in the given taxonomy.

params.name: object_id
params.description: ID of the object (post ID, link ID, ...).

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Single taxonomy name.

params.name: terms
params.description: Optional. Term ID, name, slug, or array of such to check against. Default null.

returnDescription: WP_Error on input error.

name: is_page
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing single page. If the $page parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the pages specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: page
params.description: Optional. Page ID, title, slug, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing single page.

name: is_page_template
description: Determines whether the current post uses a page template. This template tag allows you to determine if you are in a page template. You can optionally provide a template filename or array of template filenames and then the check will be specific to that template. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: template
params.description: The specific template filename or array of templates to match.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: is_paged
description: Determines whether the query is for a paged result and not for the first page. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for a paged result.

name: is_php_version_compatible
description: Checks compatibility with the current PHP version.

params.name: required
params.description: Minimum required PHP version.

returnDescription: True if required version is compatible or empty, false if not.

name: is_plugin_active
description: Determines whether a plugin is active. Only plugins installed in the plugins/ folder can be active. Plugins in the mu-plugins/ folder can't be "activated," so this function will return false for those plugins. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: True, if in the active plugins list. False, not in the list.

name: is_plugin_active_for_network
description: Determines whether the plugin is active for the entire network. Only plugins installed in the plugins/ folder can be active. Plugins in the mu-plugins/ folder can't be "activated," so this function will return false for those plugins. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: True if active for the network, otherwise false.

name: is_plugin_inactive
description: Determines whether the plugin is inactive. Reverse of is_plugin_active(). Used as a callback. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: True if inactive. False if active.

name: is_plugin_page
description: Determines whether the current admin page is generated by a plugin. Use global $plugin_page and/or get_plugin_page_hookname() hooks. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

name: is_plugin_paused
description: Determines whether a plugin is technically active but was paused while loading. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: True, if in the list of paused plugins. False, if not in the list.

name: is_post_embeddable
description: Determines whether a post is embeddable.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or `WP_Post` object. Defaults to global $post.

returnDescription: Whether the post should be considered embeddable.

name: is_post_publicly_viewable
description: Determines whether a post is publicly viewable. Posts are considered publicly viewable if both the post status and post type are viewable.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Defaults to global $post.

returnDescription: Whether the post is publicly viewable.

name: is_post_status_viewable
description: Determines whether a post status is considered "viewable". For built-in post statuses such as publish and private, the 'public' value will be evaluated. For all others, the 'publicly_queryable' value will be used.

params.name: post_status
params.description: Post status name or object.

returnDescription: Whether the post status should be considered viewable.

name: is_post_type_archive
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing post type archive page. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: post_types
params.description: Optional. Post type or array of posts types to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing post type archive page.

name: is_post_type_hierarchical
description: Determines whether the post type is hierarchical. A false return value might also mean that the post type does not exist.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Post type name

returnDescription: Whether post type is hierarchical.

name: is_post_type_viewable
description: Determines whether a post type is considered "viewable". For built-in post types such as posts and pages, the 'public' value will be evaluated. For all others, the 'publicly_queryable' value will be used.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Post type name or object.

returnDescription: Whether the post type should be considered viewable.

name: is_preview
description: Determines whether the query is for a post or page preview. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for a post or page preview.

name: is_privacy_policy
description: Determines whether the query is for the Privacy Policy page. The Privacy Policy page is the page that shows the Privacy Policy content of the site. is_privacy_policy() is dependent on the site's "Change your Privacy Policy page" Privacy Settings 'wp_page_for_privacy_policy'. This function will return true only on the page you set as the "Privacy Policy page". For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for the Privacy Policy page.

name: is_protected_ajax_action
description: Determines whether we are currently handling an Ajax action that should be protected against WSODs.
returnDescription: True if the current Ajax action should be protected.

name: is_protected_endpoint
description: Determines whether we are currently on an endpoint that should be protected against WSODs.
returnDescription: True if the current endpoint should be protected.

name: is_protected_meta
description: Determines whether a meta key is considered protected.

params.name: meta_key
params.description: Metadata key.

params.name: meta_type
params.description: Optional. Type of object metadata is for. Accepts 'blog', 'post', 'comment', 'term', 'user', or any other object type with an associated meta table. Default empty string.

returnDescription: Whether the meta key is considered protected.

name: is_random_header_image
description: Checks if random header image is in use. Always true if user expressly chooses the option in Appearance > Header. Also true if theme has multiple header images registered, no specific header image is chosen, and theme turns on random headers with add_theme_support().

params.name: type
params.description: The random pool to use. Possible values include 'any', 'default', 'uploaded'. Default 'any'.

name: is_registered_sidebar
description: Checks if a sidebar is registered.

params.name: sidebar_id
params.description: The ID of the sidebar when it was registered.

returnDescription: True if the sidebar is registered, false otherwise.

name: is_robots
description: Is the query for the robots.txt file?
returnDescription: Whether the query is for the robots.txt file.

name: is_rtl
description: Determines whether the current locale is right-to-left (RTL). For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether locale is RTL.

name: is_search
description: Determines whether the query is for a search. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for a search.

name: is_serialized
description: Checks value to find if it was serialized. If $data is not a string, then returned value will always be false. Serialized data is always a string.

params.name: data
params.description: Value to check to see if was serialized.

params.name: strict
params.description: Optional. Whether to be strict about the end of the string. Default true.

returnDescription: False if not serialized and true if it was.

name: is_serialized_string
description: Checks whether serialized data is of string type.

params.name: data
params.description: Serialized data.

returnDescription: False if not a serialized string, true if it is.

name: is_single
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing single post. Works for any post type, except attachments and pages If the $post parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the Posts specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID, title, slug, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing single post.

name: is_singular
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing single post of any post type (post, attachment, page, custom post types). If the $post_types parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the Posts Types specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: post_types
params.description: Optional. Post type or array of post types to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing single post or any of the given post types.

name: is_site_admin
description: Determine if user is a site admin. Plugins should use is_multisite() instead of checking if this function exists to determine if multisite is enabled. This function must reside in a file included only if is_multisite() due to legacy function_exists() checks to determine if multisite is enabled.

params.name: user_login
params.description: Optional. Username for the user to check. Default empty.

name: is_site_meta_supported
description: Determines whether site meta is enabled. This function checks whether the 'blogmeta' database table exists. The result is saved as a setting for the main network, making it essentially a global setting. Subsequent requests will refer to this setting instead of running the query.
returnDescription: True if site meta is supported, false otherwise.

name: is_ssl
description: Determines if SSL is used.
returnDescription: True if SSL, otherwise false.

name: is_sticky
description: Determines whether a post is sticky. Sticky posts should remain at the top of The Loop. If the post ID is not given, then The Loop ID for the current post will be used. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. Post ID. Default is the ID of the global `$post`.

returnDescription: Whether post is sticky.

name: is_subdomain_install
description: Whether a subdomain configuration is enabled.
returnDescription: True if subdomain configuration is enabled, false otherwise.

name: is_super_admin
description: Determines whether user is a site admin.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. The ID of a user. Defaults to false, to check the current user.

returnDescription: Whether the user is a site admin.

name: is_tag
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing tag archive page. If the $tag parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the tags specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: tag
params.description: Optional. Tag ID, name, slug, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing tag archive page.

name: is_tax
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing custom taxonomy archive page. If the $taxonomy parameter is specified, this function will additionally check if the query is for that specific $taxonomy. If the $term parameter is specified in addition to the $taxonomy parameter, this function will additionally check if the query is for one of the terms specified. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. Taxonomy slug or slugs to check against. Default empty.

params.name: term
params.description: Optional. Term ID, name, slug, or array of such to check against. Default empty.

returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing custom taxonomy archive page. True for custom taxonomy archive pages, false for built-in taxonomies (category and tag archives).

name: is_taxonomy
description: Checks that the taxonomy name exists.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Name of taxonomy object

returnDescription: Whether the taxonomy exists.

name: is_taxonomy_hierarchical
description: Determines whether the taxonomy object is hierarchical. Checks to make sure that the taxonomy is an object first. Then Gets the object, and finally returns the hierarchical value in the object. A false return value might also mean that the taxonomy does not exist. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Name of taxonomy object.

returnDescription: Whether the taxonomy is hierarchical.

name: is_taxonomy_viewable
description: Determines whether a taxonomy is considered "viewable".

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name or object.

returnDescription: Whether the taxonomy should be considered viewable.

name: is_term
description: Check if Term exists.

params.name: term
params.description: The term to check

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: The taxonomy name to use

params.name: parent
params.description: ID of parent term under which to confine the exists search.

returnDescription: Get the term ID or term object, if exists.

name: is_term_publicly_viewable
description: Determines whether a term is publicly viewable. A term is considered publicly viewable if its taxonomy is viewable.

params.name: term
params.description: Term ID or term object.

returnDescription: Whether the term is publicly viewable.

name: is_textdomain_loaded
description: Determines whether there are translations for the text domain.

params.name: domain
params.description: Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings.

returnDescription: Whether there are translations.

name: is_theme_paused
description: Determines whether a theme is technically active but was paused while loading. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.

params.name: theme
params.description: Path to the theme directory relative to the themes directory.

returnDescription: True, if in the list of paused themes. False, not in the list.

name: is_time
description: Determines whether the query is for a specific time. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for a specific time.

name: is_trackback
description: Determines whether the query is for a trackback endpoint call. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for a trackback endpoint call.

name: is_uninstallable_plugin
description: Determines whether the plugin can be uninstalled.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: Whether plugin can be uninstalled.

name: is_upload_space_available
description: Determines if there is any upload space left in the current blog's quota.
returnDescription: True if space is available, false otherwise.

name: is_user_admin
description: Determines whether the current request is for a user admin screen. e.g. `/wp-admin/user/` Does not check if the user is an administrator; use current_user_can() for checking roles and capabilities.
returnDescription: True if inside WordPress user administration pages.

name: is_user_logged_in
description: Determines whether the current visitor is a logged in user. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: True if user is logged in, false if not logged in.

name: is_user_member_of_blog
description: Finds out whether a user is a member of a given blog.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. The unique ID of the user. Defaults to the current user.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. ID of the blog to check. Defaults to the current site.

name: is_user_option_local
description: Check whether a usermeta key has to do with the current blog.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. Defaults to current user.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. Defaults to current blog.

name: is_user_spammy
description: Determines whether a user is marked as a spammer, based on user login.

params.name: user
params.description: Optional. Defaults to current user. WP_User object, or user login name as a string.

name: is_utf8_charset
description: Indicates if a given slug for a character set represents the UTF-8 text encoding. If not provided, examines the current blog's charset. A charset is considered to represent UTF-8 if it is a case-insensitive match of "UTF-8" with or without the hyphen. Example: true === is_utf8_charset( 'UTF-8' ); true === is_utf8_charset( 'utf8' ); false === is_utf8_charset( 'latin1' ); false === is_utf8_charset( 'UTF 8' ); // Only strings match. false === is_utf8_charset( [ 'charset' => 'utf-8' ] ); // Without a given charset, it depends on the site option "blog_charset". $is_utf8 = is_utf8_charset();

params.name: blog_charset
params.description: Optional. Slug representing a text character encoding, or "charset". E.g. "UTF-8", "Windows-1252", "ISO-8859-1", "SJIS". Default value is to infer from "blog_charset" option.

returnDescription: Whether the slug represents the UTF-8 encoding.

name: is_wp_error
description: Checks whether the given variable is a WordPress Error. Returns whether `$thing` is an instance of the `WP_Error` class.

params.name: thing
params.description: The variable to check.

returnDescription: Whether the variable is an instance of WP_Error.

name: is_wp_version_compatible
description: Checks compatibility with the current WordPress version.

params.name: required
params.description: Minimum required WordPress version.

returnDescription: True if required version is compatible or empty, false if not.

name: is_wpmu_sitewide_plugin
description: Deprecated functionality for determining if the current plugin is network-only.

name: is_year
description: Determines whether the query is for an existing year archive. For more information on this and similar theme functions, check out the {@link https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/basics/conditional-tags/ Conditional Tags} article in the Theme Developer Handbook.
returnDescription: Whether the query is for an existing year archive.

name: iso8601_timezone_to_offset
description: Given an ISO 8601 timezone, returns its UTC offset in seconds.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Either 'Z' for 0 offset or '±hhmm'.

returnDescription: The offset in seconds.

name: iso8601_to_datetime
description: Given an ISO 8601 (Ymd\TH:i:sO) date, returns a MySQL DateTime (Y-m-d H:i:s) format used by post_date[_gmt].

params.name: date_string
params.description: Date and time in ISO 8601 format {@link https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601}.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Optional. If set to 'gmt' returns the result in UTC. Default 'user'.

returnDescription: The date and time in MySQL DateTime format - Y-m-d H:i:s, or false on failure.

name: js_escape
description: Escape single quotes, specialchar double quotes, and fix line endings. The filter {@see 'js_escape'} is also applied by esc_js().

params.name: text
params.description: The text to be escaped.

returnDescription: Escaped text.

name: kses_init
description: Sets up most of the KSES filters for input form content. First removes all of the KSES filters in case the current user does not need to have KSES filter the content. If the user does not have `unfiltered_html` capability, then KSES filters are added.

name: kses_init_filters
description: Adds all KSES input form content filters. All hooks have default priority. The `wp_filter_kses()` function is added to the 'pre_comment_content' and 'title_save_pre' hooks. The `wp_filter_post_kses()` function is added to the 'content_save_pre', 'excerpt_save_pre', and 'content_filtered_save_pre' hooks.

name: kses_remove_filters
description: Removes all KSES input form content filters. A quick procedural method to removing all of the filters that KSES uses for content in WordPress Loop. Does not remove the `kses_init()` function from {@see 'init'} hook (priority is default). Also does not remove `kses_init()` function from {@see 'set_current_user'} hook (priority is also default).

name: language_attributes
description: Displays the language attributes for the 'html' tag. Builds up a set of HTML attributes containing the text direction and language information for the page.

params.name: doctype
params.description: Optional. The type of HTML document. Accepts 'xhtml' or 'html'. Default 'html'.

name: like_escape
description: Formerly used to escape strings before searching the DB. It was poorly documented and never worked as described.

params.name: text
params.description: The text to be escaped.

returnDescription: text, safe for inclusion in LIKE query.

name: link_advanced_meta_box
description: Displays advanced link options form fields.

params.name: link
params.description: Current link object.

name: link_categories_meta_box
description: Displays link categories form fields.

params.name: link
params.description: Current link object.

name: link_pages
description: Print list of pages based on arguments.

name: link_submit_meta_box
description: Displays link create form fields.

params.name: link
params.description: Current link object.

name: link_target_meta_box
description: Displays form fields for changing link target.

params.name: link
params.description: Current link object.

name: link_xfn_meta_box
description: Displays XFN form fields.

params.name: link
params.description: Current link object.

name: links_add_base_url
description: Adds a base URL to relative links in passed content. By default, this function supports the 'src' and 'href' attributes. However, this can be modified via the `$attrs` parameter.

params.name: content
params.description: String to search for links in.

params.name: base
params.description: The base URL to prefix to links.

params.name: attrs
params.description: The attributes which should be processed.

returnDescription: The processed content.

name: links_add_target
description: Adds a target attribute to all links in passed content. By default, this function only applies to ` ` tags. However, this can be modified via the `$tags` parameter. *NOTE:* Any current target attribute will be stripped and replaced.

params.name: content
params.description: String to search for links in.

params.name: target
params.description: The target to add to the links.

params.name: tags
params.description: An array of tags to apply to.

returnDescription: The processed content.

name: links_popup_script
description: Show the link to the links popup and the number of links.

params.name: text
params.description: the text of the link

params.name: width
params.description: the width of the popup window

params.name: height
params.description: the height of the popup window

params.name: file
params.description: the page to open in the popup window

params.name: count
params.description: the number of links in the db

name: list_authors
description: Lists authors.

name: list_cats
description: Lists categories.

name: list_core_update
description: Lists available core updates.

name: list_files
description: Returns a listing of all files in the specified folder and all subdirectories up to 100 levels deep. The depth of the recursiveness can be controlled by the $levels param.

params.name: folder
params.description: Optional. Full path to folder. Default empty.

params.name: levels
params.description: Optional. Levels of folders to follow, Default 100 (PHP Loop limit).

params.name: exclusions
params.description: Optional. List of folders and files to skip.

returnDescription: Array of files on success, false on failure.

name: list_meta
description: Outputs a post's public meta data in the Custom Fields meta box.

params.name: meta
params.description: An array of meta data arrays keyed on 'meta_key' and 'meta_value'.

name: list_plugin_updates
description: Display the upgrade plugins form.

name: list_theme_updates
description: Display the upgrade themes form.

name: list_translation_updates
description: Display the update translations form.

name: load_child_theme_textdomain
description: Loads the child theme's translated strings. If the current locale exists as a .mo file in the child theme's root directory, it will be included in the translated strings by the $domain. The .mo files must be named based on the locale exactly.

params.name: domain
params.description: Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings.

params.name: path
params.description: Optional. Path to the directory containing the .mo file. Default false.

returnDescription: True when the theme textdomain is successfully loaded, false otherwise.

name: load_default_textdomain
description: Loads default translated strings based on locale. Loads the .mo file in WP_LANG_DIR constant path from WordPress root. The translated (.mo) file is named based on the locale.

params.name: locale
params.description: Optional. Locale to load. Default is the value of get_locale().

returnDescription: Whether the textdomain was loaded.

name: load_image_to_edit
description: Loads an image resource for editing.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: Attachment ID.

params.name: mime_type
params.description: Image mime type.

params.name: size
params.description: Optional. Image size. Accepts any registered image size name, or an array of width and height values in pixels (in that order). Default 'full'.

returnDescription: The resulting image resource or GdImage instance on success, false on failure.

name: load_muplugin_textdomain
description: Loads the translated strings for a plugin residing in the mu-plugins directory.

params.name: domain
params.description: Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings.

params.name: mu_plugin_rel_path
params.description: Optional. Relative to `WPMU_PLUGIN_DIR` directory in which the .mo file resides. Default empty string.

returnDescription: True when textdomain is successfully loaded, false otherwise.

name: load_plugin_textdomain
description: Loads a plugin's translated strings. If the path is not given then it will be the root of the plugin directory. The .mo file should be named based on the text domain with a dash, and then the locale exactly.

params.name: domain
params.description: Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Optional. Deprecated. Use the $plugin_rel_path parameter instead. Default false.

params.name: plugin_rel_path
params.description: Optional. Relative path to WP_PLUGIN_DIR where the .mo file resides. Default false.

returnDescription: True when textdomain is successfully loaded, false otherwise.

name: load_script_textdomain
description: Loads the script translated strings.

params.name: handle
params.description: Name of the script to register a translation domain to.

params.name: domain
params.description: Optional. Text domain. Default 'default'.

params.name: path
params.description: Optional. The full file path to the directory containing translation files.

returnDescription: The translated strings in JSON encoding on success, false if the script textdomain could not be loaded.

name: load_script_translations
description: Loads the translation data for the given script handle and text domain.

params.name: file
params.description: Path to the translation file to load. False if there isn't one.

params.name: handle
params.description: Name of the script to register a translation domain to.

params.name: domain
params.description: The text domain.

returnDescription: The JSON-encoded translated strings for the given script handle and text domain. False if there are none.

name: load_template
description: Requires the template file with WordPress environment. The globals are set up for the template file to ensure that the WordPress environment is available from within the function. The query variables are also available.

params.name: _template_file
params.description: Path to template file.

params.name: load_once
params.description: Whether to require_once or require. Default true.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments passed to the template. Default empty array.

name: load_textdomain
description: Loads a .mo file into the text domain $domain. If the text domain already exists, the translations will be merged. If both sets have the same string, the translation from the original value will be taken. On success, the .mo file will be placed in the $l10n global by $domain and will be a MO object.

params.name: domain
params.description: Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings.

params.name: mofile
params.description: Path to the .mo file.

params.name: locale
params.description: Optional. Locale. Default is the current locale.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: load_theme_textdomain
description: Loads the theme's translated strings. If the current locale exists as a .mo file in the theme's root directory, it will be included in the translated strings by the $domain. The .mo files must be named based on the locale exactly.

params.name: domain
params.description: Text domain. Unique identifier for retrieving translated strings.

params.name: path
params.description: Optional. Path to the directory containing the .mo file. Default false.

returnDescription: True when textdomain is successfully loaded, false otherwise.

name: locale_stylesheet
description: Displays localized stylesheet link element.

name: locate_block_template
description: Finds a block template with equal or higher specificity than a given PHP template file. Internally, this communicates the block content that needs to be used by the template canvas through a global variable.

params.name: template
params.description: Path to the template. See locate_template().

params.name: type
params.description: Sanitized filename without extension.

params.name: templates
params.description: A list of template candidates, in descending order of priority.

returnDescription: The path to the Site Editor template canvas file, or the fallback PHP template.

name: locate_template
description: Retrieves the name of the highest priority template file that exists. Searches in the stylesheet directory before the template directory and wp-includes/theme-compat so that themes which inherit from a parent theme can just overload one file.

params.name: template_names
params.description: Template file(s) to search for, in order.

params.name: load
params.description: If true the template file will be loaded if it is found.

params.name: load_once
params.description: Whether to require_once or require. Has no effect if `$load` is false. Default true.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments passed to the template. Default empty array.

returnDescription: The template filename if one is located.

name: login_footer
description: Outputs the footer for the login page.

params.name: input_id
params.description: Which input to auto-focus.

name: login_header
description: Outputs the login page header.

params.name: title
params.description: Optional. WordPress login page title to display in the ` ` element. Defaults to 'Log In'.

params.name: message
params.description: Optional. Message to display in header. Default empty.

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. The error to pass. Defaults to a WP_Error instance.

name: logIO
description: logIO() - Writes logging info to a file.

params.name: io
params.description: Whether input or output.

params.name: msg
params.description: Information describing logging reason.

name: maintenance_nag
description: Displays maintenance nag HTML message.

name: make_after_block_visitor
description: Returns a function that injects the hooked blocks after a given block. The returned function can be used as `$post_callback` argument to `traverse_and_serialize_block(s)`, where it will append the markup for any blocks hooked `after` the given block and as its parent's `last_child`, respectively. This function is meant for internal use only.

params.name: hooked_blocks
params.description: An array of blocks hooked to another block.

params.name: context
params.description: A block template, template part, post object, or pattern that the blocks belong to.

params.name: callback
params.description: A function that will be called for each block to generate the markup for a given list of blocks that are hooked to it. Default: 'insert_hooked_blocks'.

returnDescription: A function that returns the serialized markup for the given block, including the markup for any hooked blocks after it.

name: make_before_block_visitor
description: Returns a function that injects the theme attribute into, and hooked blocks before, a given block. The returned function can be used as `$pre_callback` argument to `traverse_and_serialize_block(s)`, where it will inject the `theme` attribute into all Template Part blocks, and prepend the markup for any blocks hooked `before` the given block and as its parent's `first_child`, respectively. This function is meant for internal use only.

params.name: hooked_blocks
params.description: An array of blocks hooked to another given block.

params.name: context
params.description: A block template, template part, post object, or pattern that the blocks belong to.

params.name: callback
params.description: A function that will be called for each block to generate the markup for a given list of blocks that are hooked to it. Default: 'insert_hooked_blocks'.

returnDescription: A function that returns the serialized markup for the given block, including the markup for any hooked blocks before it.

name: make_clickable
description: Converts plaintext URI to HTML links. Converts URI, www and ftp, and email addresses. Finishes by fixing links within links.

params.name: text
params.description: Content to convert URIs.

returnDescription: Content with converted URIs.

name: make_db_current
description: Updates the database tables to a new schema. By default, updates all the tables to use the latest defined schema, but can also be used to update a specific set of tables in wp_get_db_schema().

params.name: tables
params.description: Optional. Which set of tables to update. Default is 'all'.

name: make_db_current_silent
description: Updates the database tables to a new schema, but without displaying results. By default, updates all the tables to use the latest defined schema, but can also be used to update a specific set of tables in wp_get_db_schema().

params.name: tables
params.description: Optional. Which set of tables to update. Default is 'all'.

name: make_site_theme
description: Creates a site theme. {@internal Missing Long Description}}

name: make_site_theme_from_default
description: Creates a site theme from the default theme. {@internal Missing Long Description}}

params.name: theme_name
params.description: The name of the theme.

name: map_deep
description: Maps a function to all non-iterable elements of an array or an object. This is similar to `array_walk_recursive()` but acts upon objects too.

returnDescription: The value with the callback applied to all non-arrays and non-objects inside it.

name: map_meta_cap
description: Maps a capability to the primitive capabilities required of the given user to satisfy the capability being checked. This function also accepts an ID of an object to map against if the capability is a meta capability. Meta capabilities such as `edit_post` and `edit_user` are capabilities used by this function to map to primitive capabilities that a user or role requires, such as `edit_posts` and `edit_others_posts`. Example usage: map_meta_cap( 'edit_posts', $user->ID ); map_meta_cap( 'edit_post', $user->ID, $post->ID ); map_meta_cap( 'edit_post_meta', $user->ID, $post->ID, $meta_key ); This function does not check whether the user has the required capabilities, it just returns what the required capabilities are.

params.name: cap
params.description: Capability being checked.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

returnDescription: Primitive capabilities required of the user.

returnDescription: True on success or if the table already exists. False on failure.

name: mbstring_binary_safe_encoding
description: Sets the mbstring internal encoding to a binary safe encoding when func_overload is enabled. When mbstring.func_overload is in use for multi-byte encodings, the results from strlen() and similar functions respect the utf8 characters, causing binary data to return incorrect lengths. This function overrides the mbstring encoding to a binary-safe encoding, and resets it to the users expected encoding afterwards through the `reset_mbstring_encoding` function. It is safe to recursively call this function, however each `mbstring_binary_safe_encoding()` call must be followed up with an equal number of `reset_mbstring_encoding()` calls.

params.name: reset
params.description: Optional. Whether to reset the encoding back to a previously-set encoding. Default false.

name: ms_load_current_site_and_network
description: Identifies the network and site of a requested domain and path and populates the corresponding network and site global objects as part of the multisite bootstrap process. Prior to 4.6.0, this was a procedural block in `ms-settings.php`. It was wrapped into a function to facilitate unit tests. It should not be used outside of core. Usually, it's easier to query the site first, which then declares its network. In limited situations, we either can or must find the network first. If a network and site are found, a `true` response will be returned so that the request can continue. If neither a network or site is found, `false` or a URL string will be returned so that either an error can be shown or a redirect can occur.

params.name: domain
params.description: The requested domain.

params.name: path
params.description: The requested path.

params.name: subdomain
params.description: Optional. Whether a subdomain (true) or subdirectory (false) configuration. Default false.

returnDescription: True if bootstrap successfully populated `$current_blog` and `$current_site`. False if bootstrap could not be properly completed. Redirect URL if parts exist, but the request as a whole can not be fulfilled.

name: mysql_to_rfc3339
description: Parses and formats a MySQL datetime (Y-m-d H:i:s) for ISO8601 (Y-m-d\TH:i:s). Explicitly strips timezones, as datetimes are not saved with any timezone information. Including any information on the offset could be misleading. Despite historical function name, the output does not conform to RFC3339 format, which must contain timezone.

returnDescription: Date formatted for ISO8601 without time zone.

name: mysql2date
description: Converts given MySQL date string into a different format. - `$format` should be a PHP date format string. - 'U' and 'G' formats will return an integer sum of timestamp with timezone offset. - `$date` is expected to be local time in MySQL format (`Y-m-d H:i:s`). Historically UTC time could be passed to the function to produce Unix timestamp. If `$translate` is true then the given date and format string will be passed to `wp_date()` for translation.

params.name: format
params.description: Format of the date to return.

params.name: date
params.description: Date string to convert.

params.name: translate
params.description: Whether the return date should be translated. Default true.

returnDescription: Integer if `$format` is 'U' or 'G', string otherwise. False on failure.

name: paginate_links
description: Retrieves paginated links for archive post pages. Technically, the function can be used to create paginated link list for any area. The 'base' argument is used to reference the url, which will be used to create the paginated links. The 'format' argument is then used for replacing the page number. It is however, most likely and by default, to be used on the archive post pages. The 'type' argument controls format of the returned value. The default is 'plain', which is just a string with the links separated by a newline character. The other possible values are either 'array' or 'list'. The 'array' value will return an array of the paginated link list to offer full control of display. The 'list' value will place all of the paginated links in an unordered HTML list. The 'total' argument is the total amount of pages and is an integer. The 'current' argument is the current page number and is also an integer. An example of the 'base' argument is "http://example.com/all_posts.php%_%" and the '%_%' is required. The '%_%' will be replaced by the contents of in the 'format' argument. An example for the 'format' argument is "?page=%#%" and the '%#%' is also required. The '%#%' will be replaced with the page number. You can include the previous and next links in the list by setting the 'prev_next' argument to true, which it is by default. You can set the previous text, by using the 'prev_text' argument. You can set the next text by setting the 'next_text' argument. If the 'show_all' argument is set to true, then it will show all of the pages instead of a short list of the pages near the current page. By default, the 'show_all' is set to false and controlled by the 'end_size' and 'mid_size' arguments. The 'end_size' argument is how many numbers on either the start and the end list edges, by default is 1. The 'mid_size' argument is how many numbers to either side of current page, but not including current page. It is possible to add query vars to the link by using the 'add_args' argument and see add_query_arg() for more information. The 'before_page_number' and 'after_page_number' arguments allow users to augment the links themselves. Typically this might be to add context to the numbered links so that screen reader users understand what the links are for. The text strings are added before and after the page number - within the anchor tag.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Array or string of arguments for generating paginated links for archives. @type string $base Base of the paginated url. Default empty. @type string $format Format for the pagination structure. Default empty. @type int $total The total amount of pages. Default is the value WP_Query's `max_num_pages` or 1. @type int $current The current page number. Default is 'paged' query var or 1. @type string $aria_current The value for the aria-current attribute. Possible values are 'page', 'step', 'location', 'date', 'time', 'true', 'false'. Default is 'page'. @type bool $show_all Whether to show all pages. Default false. @type int $end_size How many numbers on either the start and the end list edges. Default 1. @type int $mid_size How many numbers to either side of the current pages. Default 2. @type bool $prev_next Whether to include the previous and next links in the list. Default true. @type string $prev_text The previous page text. Default '« Previous'. @type string $next_text The next page text. Default 'Next »'. @type string $type Controls format of the returned value. Possible values are 'plain', 'array' and 'list'. Default is 'plain'. @type array $add_args An array of query args to add. Default false. @type string $add_fragment A string to append to each link. Default empty. @type string $before_page_number A string to appear before the page number. Default empty. @type string $after_page_number A string to append after the page number. Default empty. }

returnDescription: String of page links or array of page links, depending on 'type' argument. Void if total number of pages is less than 2.

name: parse_blocks
description: Parses blocks out of a content string. Given an HTML document, this function fully-parses block content, producing a tree of blocks and their contents, as well as top-level non-block content, which will appear as a block with no `blockName`. This function can be memory heavy for certain documents, particularly those with deeply-nested blocks or blocks with extensive attribute values. Further, this function must parse an entire document in one atomic operation. If the entire parsed document is not necessary, consider using {@see WP_Block_Processor} instead, as it provides a streaming and low-overhead interface for finding blocks.

params.name: content
params.description: Post content.

returnDescription: { Array of block structures. @type array ...$0 { An associative array of a single parsed block object. See WP_Block_Parser_Block. @type string|null $blockName Name of block. @type array $attrs Attributes from block comment delimiters. @type array[] $innerBlocks List of inner blocks. An array of arrays that have the same structure as this one. @type string $innerHTML HTML from inside block comment delimiters. @type array $innerContent List of string fragments and null markers where inner blocks were found. } }
