name: wp_get_block_css_selector
description: Determines the CSS selector for the block type and property provided, returning it if available.

params.name: block_type
params.description: The block's type.

params.name: target
params.description: The desired selector's target, `root` or array path.

params.name: fallback
params.description: Whether to fall back to broader selector.

returnDescription: CSS selector or `null` if no selector available.

name: wp_get_block_default_classname
description: Gets the generated classname from a given block name.

params.name: block_name
params.description: Block Name.

returnDescription: Generated classname.

name: wp_get_block_name_from_theme_json_path
description: Gets the block name from a given theme.json path.

params.name: path
params.description: An array of keys describing the path to a property in theme.json.

returnDescription: Identified block name, or empty string if none found.

name: wp_get_block_style_variation_name_from_class
description: Determines the block style variation names within a CSS class string.

params.name: class_string
params.description: CSS class string to look for a variation in.

returnDescription: The block style variation name if found.

name: wp_get_canonical_url
description: Returns the canonical URL for a post. When the post is the same as the current requested page the function will handle the pagination arguments too.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or object. Default is global `$post`.

returnDescription: The canonical URL. False if the post does not exist or has not been published yet.

name: wp_get_code_editor_settings
description: Generates and returns code editor settings.

params.name: args
params.description: { Args. @type string $type The MIME type of the file to be edited. @type string $file Filename to be edited. Extension is used to sniff the type. Can be supplied as alternative to `$type` param. @type WP_Theme $theme Theme being edited when on the theme file editor. @type string $plugin Plugin being edited when on the plugin file editor. @type array $codemirror Additional CodeMirror setting overrides. @type array $csslint CSSLint rule overrides. @type array $jshint JSHint rule overrides. @type array $htmlhint HTMLHint rule overrides. }

returnDescription: Settings for the code editor.

name: wp_get_comment_fields_max_lengths
description: Retrieves the maximum character lengths for the comment form fields.
returnDescription: Array of maximum lengths keyed by field name.

name: wp_get_comment_status
description: Retrieves the status of a comment by comment ID.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Comment ID or WP_Comment object

returnDescription: Status might be 'trash', 'approved', 'unapproved', 'spam'. False on failure.

name: wp_get_computed_fluid_typography_value
description: Internal implementation of CSS clamp() based on available min/max viewport width and min/max font sizes.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. An associative array of values to calculate a fluid formula for font size. Default is empty array. @type string $maximum_viewport_width Maximum size up to which type will have fluidity. @type string $minimum_viewport_width Minimum viewport size from which type will have fluidity. @type string $maximum_font_size Maximum font size for any clamp() calculation. @type string $minimum_font_size Minimum font size for any clamp() calculation. @type int $scale_factor A scale factor to determine how fast a font scales within boundaries. }

returnDescription: A font-size value using clamp() on success, otherwise null.

name: wp_get_cookie_login
description: Gets the user cookie login. This function is deprecated. This function is deprecated and should no longer be extended as it won't be used anywhere in WordPress. Also, plugins shouldn't use it either.
returnDescription: Always returns false

name: wp_get_current_commenter
description: Gets current commenter's name, email, and URL. Expects cookies content to already be sanitized. User of this function might wish to recheck the returned array for validity.
returnDescription: { An array of current commenter variables. @type string $comment_author The name of the current commenter, or an empty string. @type string $comment_author_email The email address of the current commenter, or an empty string. @type string $comment_author_url The URL address of the current commenter, or an empty string. }

name: wp_get_current_user
description: Retrieves the current user object. Will set the current user, if the current user is not set. The current user will be set to the logged-in person. If no user is logged-in, then it will set the current user to 0, which is invalid and won't have any permissions.
returnDescription: Current WP_User instance.

name: wp_get_custom_css
description: Fetches the saved Custom CSS content for rendering.

params.name: stylesheet
params.description: Optional. A theme object stylesheet name. Defaults to the active theme.

returnDescription: The Custom CSS Post content.

name: wp_get_custom_css_post
description: Fetches the `custom_css` post for a given theme.

params.name: stylesheet
params.description: Optional. A theme object stylesheet name. Defaults to the active theme.

returnDescription: The custom_css post or null if none exists.

name: wp_get_db_schema
description: Retrieve the SQL for creating database tables.

params.name: scope
params.description: Optional. The tables for which to retrieve SQL. Can be all, global, ms_global, or blog tables. Defaults to all.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Optional. The site ID for which to retrieve SQL. Default is the current site ID.

returnDescription: The SQL needed to create the requested tables.

name: wp_get_default_extension_for_mime_type
description: Returns the first matched extension for the mime type, as mapped from wp_get_mime_types().

params.name: mime_type
params.description: The mime type to search.

returnDescription: The first matching file extension, or false if no extensions are found for the given mime type.

name: wp_get_default_update_https_url
description: Gets the default URL to learn more about updating the site to use HTTPS. Do not use this function to retrieve this URL. Instead, use {@see wp_get_update_https_url()} when relying on the URL. This function does not allow modifying the returned URL, and is only used to compare the actually used URL with the default one.
returnDescription: Default URL to learn more about updating to HTTPS.

name: wp_get_default_update_php_url
description: Gets the default URL to learn more about updating the PHP version the site is running on. Do not use this function to retrieve this URL. Instead, use {@see wp_get_update_php_url()} when relying on the URL. This function does not allow modifying the returned URL, and is only used to compare the actually used URL with the default one.
returnDescription: Default URL to learn more about updating PHP.

name: wp_get_development_mode
description: Retrieves the current development mode. The development mode affects how certain parts of the WordPress application behave, which is relevant when developing for WordPress. Development mode can be set via the `WP_DEVELOPMENT_MODE` constant in `wp-config.php`. Possible values are 'core', 'plugin', 'theme', 'all', or an empty string to disable development mode. 'all' is a special value to signify that all three development modes ('core', 'plugin', and 'theme') are enabled. Development mode is considered separately from `WP_DEBUG` and wp_get_environment_type(). It does not affect debugging output, but rather functional nuances in WordPress. This function retrieves the currently set development mode value. To check whether a specific development mode is enabled, use wp_is_development_mode().
returnDescription: The current development mode.

name: wp_get_direct_php_update_url
description: Gets the URL for directly updating the PHP version the site is running on. A URL will only be returned if the `WP_DIRECT_UPDATE_PHP_URL` environment variable is specified or by using the {@see 'wp_direct_php_update_url'} filter. This allows hosts to send users directly to the page where they can update PHP to a newer version.
returnDescription: URL for directly updating PHP or empty string.

name: wp_get_direct_update_https_url
description: Gets the URL for directly updating the site to use HTTPS. A URL will only be returned if the `WP_DIRECT_UPDATE_HTTPS_URL` environment variable is specified or by using the {@see 'wp_direct_update_https_url'} filter. This allows hosts to send users directly to the page where they can update their site to use HTTPS.
returnDescription: URL for directly updating to HTTPS or empty string.

name: wp_get_document_title
description: Returns document title for the current page.
returnDescription: Tag with the document title.

name: wp_get_duotone_filter_id
description: Returns the prefixed id for the duotone filter for use as a CSS id.

params.name: preset
params.description: Duotone preset value as seen in theme.json.

returnDescription: Duotone filter CSS id.

name: wp_get_duotone_filter_property
description: Returns the CSS filter property url to reference the rendered SVG.

params.name: preset
params.description: Duotone preset value as seen in theme.json.

returnDescription: Duotone CSS filter property url value.

name: wp_get_duotone_filter_svg
description: Returns the duotone filter SVG string for the preset.

params.name: preset
params.description: Duotone preset value as seen in theme.json.

returnDescription: Duotone SVG filter.

name: wp_get_elements_class_name
description: Gets the elements class names.

params.name: block
params.description: Block object.

returnDescription: The unique class name.

name: wp_get_environment_type
description: Retrieves the current environment type. The type can be set via the `WP_ENVIRONMENT_TYPE` global system variable, or a constant of the same name. Possible values are 'local', 'development', 'staging', and 'production'. If not set, the type defaults to 'production'.
returnDescription: The current environment type.

name: wp_get_ext_types
description: Retrieves the list of common file extensions and their types.
returnDescription: Multi-dimensional array of file extensions types keyed by the type of file.

name: wp_get_extension_error_description
description: Get a human readable description of an extension's error.

params.name: error
params.description: Error details from `error_get_last()`.

returnDescription: Formatted error description.

name: wp_get_first_block
description: Finds the first occurrence of a specific block in an array of blocks.

params.name: blocks
params.description: Array of blocks.

params.name: block_name
params.description: Name of the block to find.

returnDescription: Found block, or empty array if none found.

name: wp_get_font_dir
description: Retrieves font uploads directory information. Same as wp_font_dir() but "light weight" as it doesn't attempt to create the font uploads directory. Intended for use in themes, when only 'basedir' and 'baseurl' are needed, generally in all cases when not uploading files.
returnDescription: See wp_font_dir() for description.

name: wp_get_footnotes_from_revision
description: Gets the footnotes field from the revision for the revisions screen.

params.name: revision_field
params.description: The field value, but $revision->$field (footnotes) does not exist.

params.name: field
params.description: The field name, in this case "footnotes".

params.name: revision
params.description: The revision object to compare against.

returnDescription: The field value.

name: wp_get_global_settings
description: Gets the settings resulting of merging core, theme, and user data.

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the specific setting to retrieve. Optional. If empty, will return all settings.

returnDescription: The settings array or individual setting value to retrieve.

name: wp_get_global_styles
description: Gets the styles resulting of merging core, theme, and user data.

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the specific style to retrieve. Optional. If empty, will return all styles.

returnDescription: The styles array or individual style value to retrieve.

name: wp_get_global_styles_custom_css
description: Gets the global styles custom CSS from theme.json.
returnDescription: The global styles custom CSS.

name: wp_get_global_styles_svg_filters
description: Returns a string containing the SVGs to be referenced as filters (duotone).

name: wp_get_global_stylesheet
description: Returns the stylesheet resulting of merging core, theme, and user data.

params.name: types
params.description: Optional. Types of styles to load. See {@see 'WP_Theme_JSON::get_stylesheet'} for all valid types. If empty, it'll load the following: - for themes without theme.json: 'variables', 'presets', 'base-layout-styles'. - for themes with theme.json: 'variables', 'presets', 'styles'.

returnDescription: Stylesheet.

name: wp_get_http
description: Perform a HTTP HEAD or GET request. If $file_path is a writable filename, this will do a GET request and write the file to that path.

params.name: url
params.description: URL to fetch.

params.name: file_path
params.description: Optional. File path to write request to. Default false.

params.name: red
params.description: Optional. The number of Redirects followed, Upon 5 being hit, returns false. Default 1.

returnDescription: Headers on success, false on failure.

name: wp_get_http_headers
description: Retrieves HTTP Headers from URL.

params.name: url
params.description: URL to retrieve HTTP headers from.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Not Used.

returnDescription: Headers on success, false on failure.

name: wp_get_https_detection_errors
description: Runs a remote HTTPS request to detect whether HTTPS supported, and stores potential errors. This function checks for HTTPS support by making an HTTP request. As this process can be resource-intensive, it should be used cautiously, especially in performance-sensitive environments. It is called when HTTPS support needs to be validated.
returnDescription: An array containing potential detection errors related to HTTPS, or an empty array if no errors are found.

name: wp_get_image_editor
description: Returns a WP_Image_Editor instance and loads file into it.

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the file to load.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Additional arguments for retrieving the image editor. Default empty array.

returnDescription: The WP_Image_Editor object on success, a WP_Error object otherwise.

name: wp_get_image_editor_output_format
description: Determines the output format for the image editor.

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the image.

params.name: mime_type
params.description: The source image mime type.

returnDescription: An array of mime type mappings.

name: wp_get_image_mime
description: Returns the real mime type of an image file. This depends on exif_imagetype() or getimagesize() to determine real mime types.

params.name: file
params.description: Full path to the file.

returnDescription: The actual mime type or false if the type cannot be determined.

name: wp_get_inline_script_tag
description: Constructs an inline script tag. It is possible to inject attributes in the ` ` tag via the {@see 'wp_inline_script_attributes'} filter. Automatically injects type attribute if needed.

params.name: data
params.description: Data for script tag: JavaScript, importmap, speculationrules, etc.

params.name: attributes
params.description: Optional. Key-value pairs representing ` ` tag attributes.

returnDescription: String containing inline JavaScript code wrapped around ` ` tag.

name: wp_get_installed_translations
description: Gets installed translations. Looks in the wp-content/languages directory for translations of plugins or themes.

params.name: type
params.description: What to search for. Accepts 'plugins', 'themes', 'core'.

returnDescription: Array of language data.

name: wp_get_l10n_php_file_data
description: Extracts headers from a PHP translation file.

params.name: php_file
params.description: Path to a `.l10n.php` file.

returnDescription: Array of file header values keyed by header name.

name: wp_get_latest_revision_id_and_total_count
description: Returns the latest revision ID and count of revisions for a post.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global $post.

returnDescription: { Returns associative array with latest revision ID and total count, or a WP_Error if the post does not exist or revisions are not enabled. @type int $latest_id The latest revision post ID or 0 if no revisions exist. @type int $count The total count of revisions for the given post. }

name: wp_get_layout_definitions
description: Returns layout definitions, keyed by layout type. Provides a common definition of slugs, classnames, base styles, and spacing styles for each layout type. When making changes or additions to layout definitions, the corresponding JavaScript definitions should also be updated.
returnDescription: Layout definitions.

name: wp_get_layout_style
description: Generates the CSS corresponding to the provided layout.

params.name: selector
params.description: CSS selector.

params.name: layout
params.description: Layout object. The one that is passed has already checked the existence of default block layout.

params.name: has_block_gap_support
params.description: Optional. Whether the theme has support for the block gap. Default false.

params.name: gap_value
params.description: Optional. The block gap value to apply. Default null.

params.name: should_skip_gap_serialization
params.description: Optional. Whether to skip applying the user-defined value set in the editor. Default false.

params.name: fallback_gap_value
params.description: Optional. The block gap value to apply. Default '0.5em'.

params.name: block_spacing
params.description: Optional. Custom spacing set on the block. Default null.

returnDescription: CSS styles on success. Else, empty string.

name: wp_get_link_cats
description: Retrieves the link category IDs associated with the link specified.

params.name: link_id
params.description: Link ID to look up.

returnDescription: The IDs of the requested link's categories.

name: wp_get_links
description: Gets the links associated with category.

params.name: args
params.description: a query string

name: wp_get_linksbyname
description: Gets the links associated with the named category.

params.name: category
params.description: The category to use.

name: wp_get_list_item_separator
description: Retrieves the list item separator based on the locale.
returnDescription: Locale-specific list item separator.

name: wp_get_loading_attr_default
description: Gets the default value to use for a `loading` attribute on an element. This function should only be called for a tag and context if lazy-loading is generally enabled. The function usually returns 'lazy', but uses certain heuristics to guess whether the current element is likely to appear above the fold, in which case it returns a boolean `false`, which will lead to the `loading` attribute being omitted on the element. The purpose of this refinement is to avoid lazy-loading elements that are within the initial viewport, which can have a negative performance impact. Under the hood, the function uses {@see wp_increase_content_media_count()} every time it is called for an element within the main content. If the element is the very first content element, the `loading` attribute will be omitted. This default threshold of 3 content elements to omit the `loading` attribute for can be customized using the {@see 'wp_omit_loading_attr_threshold'} filter.

params.name: context
params.description: Context for the element for which the `loading` attribute value is requested.

returnDescription: The default `loading` attribute value. Either 'lazy', 'eager', or a boolean `false`, to indicate that the `loading` attribute should be skipped.

name: wp_get_loading_optimization_attributes
description: Gets loading optimization attributes. This function returns an array of attributes that should be merged into the given attributes array to optimize loading performance. Potential attributes returned by this function are: - `loading` attribute with a value of "lazy" - `fetchpriority` attribute with a value of "high" - `decoding` attribute with a value of "async" If any of these attributes are already present in the given attributes, they will not be modified. Note that no element should have both `loading="lazy"` and `fetchpriority="high"`, so the function will trigger a warning in case both attributes are present with those values.

params.name: tag_name
params.description: The tag name.

params.name: attr
params.description: Array of the attributes for the tag.

params.name: context
params.description: Context for the element for which the loading optimization attribute is requested.

returnDescription: Loading optimization attributes.

name: wp_get_media_creation_timestamp
description: Parses creation date from media metadata. The getID3 library doesn't have a standard method for getting creation dates, so the location of this data can vary based on the MIME type.

params.name: metadata
params.description: The metadata returned by getID3::analyze().

returnDescription: A UNIX timestamp for the media's creation date if available or a boolean FALSE if a timestamp could not be determined.

name: wp_get_mime_types
description: Retrieves the list of mime types and file extensions.
returnDescription: Array of mime types keyed by the file extension regex corresponding to those types.

name: wp_get_missing_image_subsizes
description: Compare the existing image sub-sizes (as saved in the attachment meta) to the currently registered image sub-sizes, and return the difference. Registered sub-sizes that are larger than the image are skipped.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: The image attachment post ID.

returnDescription: Associative array of arrays of image sub-size information for missing image sizes, keyed by image size name.

name: wp_get_mu_plugins
description: Retrieves an array of must-use plugin files. The default directory is wp-content/mu-plugins. To change the default directory manually, define `WPMU_PLUGIN_DIR` and `WPMU_PLUGIN_URL` in wp-config.php.
returnDescription: Array of absolute paths of files to include.

name: wp_get_nav_menu_items
description: Retrieves all menu items of a navigation menu. Note: Most arguments passed to the `$args` parameter – save for 'output_key' – are specifically for retrieving nav_menu_item posts from get_posts() and may only indirectly affect the ultimate ordering and content of the resulting nav menu items that get returned from this function.

params.name: menu
params.description: Menu ID, slug, name, or object.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Arguments to pass to get_posts(). @type string $order How to order nav menu items as queried with get_posts(). Will be ignored if 'output' is ARRAY_A. Default 'ASC'. @type string $orderby Field to order menu items by as retrieved from get_posts(). Supply an orderby field via 'output_key' to affect the output order of nav menu items. Default 'menu_order'. @type string $post_type Menu items post type. Default 'nav_menu_item'. @type string $post_status Menu items post status. Default 'publish'. @type string $output How to order outputted menu items. Default ARRAY_A. @type string $output_key Key to use for ordering the actual menu items that get returned. Note that that is not a get_posts() argument and will only affect output of menu items processed in this function. Default 'menu_order'. @type bool $nopaging Whether to retrieve all menu items (true) or paginate (false). Default true. @type bool $update_menu_item_cache Whether to update the menu item cache. Default true. }

returnDescription: Array of menu items, otherwise false.

name: wp_get_nav_menu_name
description: Returns the name of a navigation menu.

params.name: location
params.description: Menu location identifier.

returnDescription: Menu name.

name: wp_get_nav_menu_object
description: Returns a navigation menu object.

params.name: menu
params.description: Menu ID, slug, name, or object.

returnDescription: Menu object on success, false if $menu param isn't supplied or term does not exist.

name: wp_get_nav_menu_to_edit
description: Returns the menu formatted to edit.

params.name: menu_id
params.description: Optional. The ID of the menu to format. Default 0.

returnDescription: The menu formatted to edit or error object on failure. Null if the `$menu_id` parameter is not supplied or the term does not exist.

name: wp_get_nav_menus
description: Returns all navigation menu objects.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Array of arguments passed on to get_terms(). Default empty array.

returnDescription: An array of menu objects.

name: wp_get_network
description: Retrieves an object containing information about the requested network. {@internal In 4.6.0, converted to use get_network()}

params.name: network
params.description: The network's database row or ID.

returnDescription: Object containing network information if found, false if not.

name: wp_get_nocache_headers
description: Gets the HTTP header information to prevent caching. The several different headers cover the different ways cache prevention is handled by different browsers or intermediate caches such as proxy servers.
returnDescription: The associative array of header names and field values.

name: wp_get_object_terms
description: Retrieves the terms associated with the given object(s), in the supplied taxonomies.

params.name: object_ids
params.description: The ID(s) of the object(s) to retrieve.

params.name: taxonomies
params.description: The taxonomy names to retrieve terms from.

params.name: args
params.description: See WP_Term_Query::__construct() for supported arguments.

returnDescription: Array of terms, a count thereof as a numeric string, or WP_Error if any of the taxonomies do not exist. See WP_Term_Query::get_terms() for more information.

name: wp_get_original_image_path
description: Retrieves the path to an uploaded image file. Similar to `get_attached_file()` however some images may have been processed after uploading to make them suitable for web use. In this case the attached "full" size file is usually replaced with a scaled down version of the original image. This function always returns the path to the originally uploaded image file.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: Attachment ID.

params.name: unfiltered
params.description: Optional. Passed through to `get_attached_file()`. Default false.

returnDescription: Path to the original image file or false if the attachment is not an image.

name: wp_get_original_image_url
description: Retrieves the URL to an original attachment image. Similar to `wp_get_attachment_url()` however some images may have been processed after uploading. In this case this function returns the URL to the originally uploaded image file.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: Attachment post ID.

returnDescription: Attachment image URL, false on error or if the attachment is not an image.

name: wp_get_original_referer
description: Retrieves original referer that was posted, if it exists.
returnDescription: Original referer URL on success, false on failure.

name: wp_get_password_hint
description: Gets the text suggesting how to create strong passwords.
returnDescription: The password hint text.

name: wp_get_plugin_action_button
description: Gets the markup for the plugin install action button.

params.name: name
params.description: Plugin name.

params.name: data
params.description: { An array or object of plugin data. Can be retrieved from the API. @type string $slug The plugin slug. @type string[] $requires_plugins An array of plugin dependency slugs. @type string $version The plugin's version string. Used when getting the install status. }

params.name: compatible_php
params.description: The result of a PHP compatibility check.

params.name: compatible_wp
params.description: The result of a WP compatibility check.

returnDescription: The markup for the dependency row button. An empty string if the user does not have capabilities.

name: wp_get_plugin_error
description: Gets the error that was recorded for a paused plugin.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: Array of error information as returned by `error_get_last()`, or false if none was recorded.

name: wp_get_plugin_file_editable_extensions
description: Gets the list of file extensions that are editable in plugins.

params.name: plugin
params.description: Path to the plugin file relative to the plugins directory.

returnDescription: Array of editable file extensions.

name: wp_get_pomo_file_data
description: Extracts headers from a PO file.

params.name: po_file
params.description: Path to PO file.

returnDescription: Array of PO file header values keyed by header name.

name: wp_get_popular_importers
description: Returns a list from WordPress.org of popular importer plugins.
returnDescription: Importers with metadata for each.

name: wp_get_post_autosave
description: Retrieves the autosaved data of the specified post. Returns a post object with the information that was autosaved for the specified post. If the optional $user_id is passed, returns the autosave for that user, otherwise returns the latest autosave.

params.name: post_id
params.description: The post ID.

params.name: user_id
params.description: Optional. The post author ID. Default 0.

returnDescription: The autosaved data or false on failure or when no autosave exists.

name: wp_get_post_categories
description: Retrieves the list of categories for a post. Compatibility layer for themes and plugins. Also an easy layer of abstraction away from the complexity of the taxonomy layer.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. The Post ID. Does not default to the ID of the global $post. Default 0.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Category query parameters. Default empty array. See WP_Term_Query::__construct() for supported arguments.

returnDescription: List of categories. If the `$fields` argument passed via `$args` is 'all' or 'all_with_object_id', an array of WP_Term objects will be returned. If `$fields` is 'ids', an array of category IDs. If `$fields` is 'names', an array of category names. WP_Error object if 'category' taxonomy doesn't exist.

name: wp_get_post_cats
description: Retrieves a list of post categories.

params.name: blogid
params.description: Not Used

name: wp_get_post_content_block_attributes
description: Retrieves Post Content block attributes from the current post template.
returnDescription: Post Content block attributes array or null if Post Content block doesn't exist.

name: wp_get_post_parent_id
description: Returns the ID of the post's parent.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Defaults to global $post.

returnDescription: Post parent ID (which can be 0 if there is no parent), or false if the post does not exist.

name: wp_get_post_revision
description: Gets a post revision.

params.name: post
params.description: Post ID or post object.

params.name: output
params.description: Optional. The required return type. One of OBJECT, ARRAY_A, or ARRAY_N, which correspond to a WP_Post object, an associative array, or a numeric array, respectively. Default OBJECT.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional sanitization filter. See sanitize_post(). Default 'raw'.

returnDescription: WP_Post (or array) on success, or null on failure.

name: wp_get_post_revisions
description: Returns all revisions of specified post.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global `$post`.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Arguments for retrieving post revisions. Default null.

returnDescription: Array of revision objects or IDs, or an empty array if none.

name: wp_get_post_revisions_url
description: Returns the url for viewing and potentially restoring revisions of a given post.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or WP_Post object. Default is global `$post`.

returnDescription: The URL for editing revisions on the given post, otherwise null.

name: wp_get_post_tags
description: Retrieves the tags for a post. There is only one default for this function, called 'fields' and by default is set to 'all'. There are other defaults that can be overridden in wp_get_object_terms().

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. The Post ID. Does not default to the ID of the global $post. Default 0.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Tag query parameters. Default empty array. See WP_Term_Query::__construct() for supported arguments.

returnDescription: Array of WP_Term objects on success or empty array if no tags were found. WP_Error object if 'post_tag' taxonomy doesn't exist.

name: wp_get_post_terms
description: Retrieves the terms for a post.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. The Post ID. Does not default to the ID of the global $post. Default 0.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Optional. The taxonomy slug or array of slugs for which to retrieve terms. Default 'post_tag'.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Term query parameters. See WP_Term_Query::__construct() for supported arguments. @type string $fields Term fields to retrieve. Default 'all'. }

returnDescription: Array of WP_Term objects on success or empty array if no terms were found. WP_Error object if `$taxonomy` doesn't exist.

name: wp_get_raw_referer
description: Retrieves unvalidated referer from the '_wp_http_referer' URL query variable or the HTTP referer. If the value of the '_wp_http_referer' URL query variable is not a string then it will be ignored. Do not use for redirects, use wp_get_referer() instead.
returnDescription: Referer URL on success, false on failure.

name: wp_get_ready_cron_jobs
description: Retrieves cron jobs ready to be run. Returns the results of _get_cron_array() limited to events ready to be run, ie, with a timestamp in the past.
returnDescription: Array of cron job arrays ready to be run.

name: wp_get_recent_posts
description: Retrieves a number of recent posts.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Arguments to retrieve posts. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Array of recent posts, where the type of each element is determined by the `$output` parameter. Empty array on failure.

name: wp_get_referer
description: Retrieves referer from '_wp_http_referer' or HTTP referer. If it's the same as the current request URL, will return false.
returnDescription: Referer URL on success, false on failure.

name: wp_get_registered_image_subsizes
description: Returns a normalized list of all currently registered image sub-sizes.
returnDescription: Associative array of arrays of image sub-size information, keyed by image size name.

name: wp_get_revision_ui_diff
description: Get the revision UI diff.

params.name: post
params.description: The post object or post ID.

params.name: compare_from
params.description: The revision ID to compare from.

params.name: compare_to
params.description: The revision ID to come to.

returnDescription: Associative array of a post's revisioned fields and their diffs. Or, false on failure.

name: wp_get_schedule
description: Retrieves the name of the recurrence schedule for an event.

params.name: hook
params.description: Action hook to identify the event.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Arguments passed to the event's callback function. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Schedule name on success, false if no schedule.

name: wp_get_scheduled_event
description: Retrieves a scheduled event. Retrieves the full event object for a given event, if no timestamp is specified the next scheduled event is returned.

params.name: hook
params.description: Action hook of the event.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Array containing each separate argument to pass to the hook's callback function. Although not passed to a callback, these arguments are used to uniquely identify the event, so they should be the same as those used when originally scheduling the event. Default empty array.

returnDescription: { The event object. False if the event does not exist. @type string $hook Action hook to execute when the event is run. @type int $timestamp Unix timestamp (UTC) for when to next run the event. @type string|false $schedule How often the event should subsequently recur. @type array $args Array containing each separate argument to pass to the hook's callback function. @type int $interval Optional. The interval time in seconds for the schedule. Only present for recurring events. }

name: wp_get_schedules
description: Retrieves supported event recurrence schedules. The default supported recurrences are 'hourly', 'twicedaily', 'daily', and 'weekly'. A plugin may add more by hooking into the {@see 'cron_schedules'} filter. The filter accepts an array of arrays. The outer array has a key that is the name of the schedule, for example 'monthly'. The value is an array with two keys, one is 'interval' and the other is 'display'. The 'interval' is a number in seconds of when the cron job should run. So for 'hourly' the time is `HOUR_IN_SECONDS` (`60 * 60` or `3600`). For 'monthly', the value would be `MONTH_IN_SECONDS` (`30 * 24 * 60 * 60` or `2592000`). The 'display' is the description. For the 'monthly' key, the 'display' would be `__( 'Once Monthly' )`. For your plugin, you will be passed an array. You can add your schedule by doing the following: // Filter parameter variable name is 'array'. $array['monthly'] = array( 'interval' => MONTH_IN_SECONDS, 'display' => __( 'Once Monthly' ) );
returnDescription: { The array of cron schedules keyed by the schedule name. @type array ...$0 { Cron schedule information. @type int $interval The schedule interval in seconds. @type string $display The schedule display name. } }

name: wp_get_script_polyfill
description: Returns contents of an inline script used in appending polyfill scripts for browsers which fail the provided tests. The provided array is a mapping from a condition to verify feature support to its polyfill script handle.

params.name: scripts
params.description: WP_Scripts object.

params.name: tests
params.description: Features to detect.

returnDescription: Conditional polyfill inline script.

name: wp_get_script_tag
description: Formats ` ` loader tags. It is possible to inject attributes in the ` ` tag via the {@see 'wp_script_attributes'} filter. Automatically injects type attribute if needed.

params.name: attributes
params.description: Key-value pairs representing ` ` tag attributes.

returnDescription: String containing ` ` opening and closing tags.

name: wp_get_server_protocol
description: Returns the HTTP protocol sent by the server.
returnDescription: The HTTP protocol. Default: HTTP/1.0.

name: wp_get_session_token
description: Retrieves the current session token from the logged_in cookie.
returnDescription: Token.

name: wp_get_shortlink
description: Returns a shortlink for a post, page, attachment, or site. This function exists to provide a shortlink tag that all themes and plugins can target. A plugin must hook in to provide the actual shortlinks. Default shortlink support is limited to providing ?p= style links for posts. Plugins can short-circuit this function via the {@see 'pre_get_shortlink'} filter or filter the output via the {@see 'get_shortlink'} filter.

params.name: id
params.description: Optional. A post or site ID. Default is 0, which means the current post or site.

params.name: context
params.description: Optional. Whether the ID is a 'site' ID, 'post' ID, or 'media' ID. If 'post', the post_type of the post is consulted. If 'query', the current query is consulted to determine the ID and context. Default 'post'.

params.name: allow_slugs
params.description: Optional. Whether to allow post slugs in the shortlink. It is up to the plugin how and whether to honor this. Default true.

returnDescription: A shortlink or an empty string if no shortlink exists for the requested resource or if shortlinks are not enabled.

name: wp_get_sidebar
description: Retrieves the registered sidebar with the given ID.

params.name: id
params.description: The sidebar ID.

returnDescription: The discovered sidebar, or null if it is not registered.

name: wp_get_sidebars_widgets
description: Retrieves the full list of sidebars and their widget instance IDs. Will upgrade sidebar widget list, if needed. Will also save updated list, if needed.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Not used (argument deprecated).

returnDescription: Upgraded list of widgets to version 3 array format when called from the admin.

name: wp_get_single_post
description: Retrieve a single post, based on post ID. Has categories in 'post_category' property or key. Has tags in 'tags_input' property or key.

params.name: postid
params.description: Post ID.

params.name: mode
params.description: How to return result, either OBJECT, ARRAY_N, or ARRAY_A.

returnDescription: Post object or array holding post contents and information

name: wp_get_sitemap_providers
description: Gets an array of sitemap providers.
returnDescription: Array of sitemap providers.

name: wp_get_sites
description: Return an array of sites for a network or networks.

params.name: args
params.description: { Array of default arguments. Optional. @type int|int[] $network_id A network ID or array of network IDs. Set to null to retrieve sites from all networks. Defaults to current network ID. @type int $public Retrieve public or non-public sites. Default null, for any. @type int $archived Retrieve archived or non-archived sites. Default null, for any. @type int $mature Retrieve mature or non-mature sites. Default null, for any. @type int $spam Retrieve spam or non-spam sites. Default null, for any. @type int $deleted Retrieve deleted or non-deleted sites. Default null, for any. @type int $limit Number of sites to limit the query to. Default 100. @type int $offset Exclude the first x sites. Used in combination with the $limit parameter. Default 0. }

returnDescription: An empty array if the installation is considered "large" via wp_is_large_network(). Otherwise, an associative array of WP_Site data as arrays.

name: wp_get_speculation_rules
description: Returns the full speculation rules data based on the configuration. Plugins with features that rely on frontend URLs to exclude from prefetching or prerendering should use the {@see 'wp_speculation_rules_href_exclude_paths'} filter to ensure those URL patterns are excluded. Additional speculation rules other than the default rule from WordPress Core can be provided by using the {@see 'wp_load_speculation_rules'} action and amending the passed WP_Speculation_Rules object.
returnDescription: Object representing the speculation rules to use, or null if speculative loading is disabled in the current context.

name: wp_get_speculation_rules_configuration
description: Returns the speculation rules configuration.
returnDescription: string>|null Associative array with 'mode' and 'eagerness' keys, or null if speculative loading is disabled.

name: wp_get_split_term
description: Gets the new term ID corresponding to a previously split term.

params.name: old_term_id
params.description: Term ID. This is the old, pre-split term ID.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy that the term belongs to.

returnDescription: If a previously split term is found corresponding to the old term_id and taxonomy, the new term_id will be returned. If no previously split term is found matching the parameters, returns false.

name: wp_get_split_terms
description: Gets data about terms that previously shared a single term_id, but have since been split.

params.name: old_term_id
params.description: Term ID. This is the old, pre-split term ID.

returnDescription: Array of new term IDs, keyed by taxonomy.

name: wp_get_term_taxonomy_parent_id
description: Returns the term's parent's term ID.

params.name: term_id
params.description: Term ID.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: Taxonomy name.

returnDescription: Parent term ID on success, false on failure.

name: wp_get_theme
description: Gets a WP_Theme object for a theme.

params.name: stylesheet
params.description: Optional. Directory name for the theme. Defaults to active theme.

params.name: theme_root
params.description: Optional. Absolute path of the theme root to look in. If not specified, get_raw_theme_root() is used to calculate the theme root for the $stylesheet provided (or active theme).

returnDescription: Theme object. Be sure to check the object's exists() method if you need to confirm the theme's existence.

name: wp_get_theme_data_custom_templates
description: Returns the metadata for the custom templates defined by the theme via theme.json.
returnDescription: Associative array of `$template_name => $template_data` pairs, with `$template_data` having "title" and "postTypes" fields.

name: wp_get_theme_data_template_parts
description: Returns the metadata for the template parts defined by the theme.
returnDescription: Associative array of `$part_name => $part_data` pairs, with `$part_data` having "title" and "area" fields.

name: wp_get_theme_directory_pattern_slugs
description: Returns the current theme's wanted patterns (slugs) to be registered from Pattern Directory.

name: wp_get_theme_error
description: Gets the error that was recorded for a paused theme.

params.name: theme
params.description: Path to the theme directory relative to the themes directory.

returnDescription: Array of error information as it was returned by `error_get_last()`, or false if none was recorded.

name: wp_get_theme_file_editable_extensions
description: Gets the list of file extensions that are editable for a given theme.

params.name: theme
params.description: Theme object.

returnDescription: Array of editable file extensions.

name: wp_get_theme_preview_path
description: Filters the blog option to return the path for the previewed theme.

params.name: current_stylesheet
params.description: The current theme's stylesheet or template path.

returnDescription: The previewed theme's stylesheet or template path.

name: wp_get_themes
description: Returns an array of WP_Theme objects based on the arguments. Despite advances over get_themes(), this function is quite expensive, and grows linearly with additional themes. Stick to wp_get_theme() if possible.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. The search arguments. @type mixed $errors True to return themes with errors, false to return themes without errors, null to return all themes. Default false. @type mixed $allowed (Multisite) True to return only allowed themes for a site. False to return only disallowed themes for a site. 'site' to return only site-allowed themes. 'network' to return only network-allowed themes. Null to return all themes. Default null. @type int $blog_id (Multisite) The blog ID used to calculate which themes are allowed. Default 0, synonymous for the current blog. }

returnDescription: Array of WP_Theme objects.

name: wp_get_translation_updates
description: Retrieves a list of all language updates available.
returnDescription: Array of translation objects that have available updates.

name: wp_get_typography_font_size_value
description: Returns a font-size value based on a given font-size preset. Takes into account fluid typography parameters and attempts to return a CSS formula depending on available, valid values.

params.name: preset
params.description: { Required. fontSizes preset value as seen in theme.json. @type string $name Name of the font size preset. @type string $slug Kebab-case, unique identifier for the font size preset. @type string|int|float $size CSS font-size value, including units if applicable. }

params.name: settings
params.description: Optional Theme JSON settings array that overrides any global theme settings. Default is false.

returnDescription: Font-size value or null if a size is not passed in $preset.

name: wp_get_typography_value_and_unit
description: Checks a string for a unit and value and returns an array consisting of `'value'` and `'unit'`, e.g. array( '42', 'rem' ).

params.name: raw_value
params.description: Raw size value from theme.json.

params.name: options
params.description: { Optional. An associative array of options. Default is empty array. @type string $coerce_to Coerce the value to rem or px. Default `'rem'`. @type int $root_size_value Value of root font size for rem|em px conversion. Default `16`. @type string[] $acceptable_units An array of font size units. Default `array( 'rem', 'px', 'em' )`; }

returnDescription: An array consisting of `'value'` and `'unit'` properties on success. `null` on failure.

name: wp_get_unapproved_comment_author_email
description: Gets unapproved comment author's email. Used to allow the commenter to see their pending comment.
returnDescription: The unapproved comment author's email (when supplied).

name: wp_get_update_data
description: Collects counts and UI strings for available updates.
returnDescription: { Fetched update data. @type int[] $counts An array of counts for available plugin, theme, and WordPress updates. @type string $update_title Titles of available updates. }

name: wp_get_update_https_url
description: Gets the URL to learn more about updating the site to use HTTPS. This URL can be overridden by specifying an environment variable `WP_UPDATE_HTTPS_URL` or by using the {@see 'wp_update_https_url'} filter. Providing an empty string is not allowed and will result in the default URL being used. Furthermore the page the URL links to should preferably be localized in the site language.
returnDescription: URL to learn more about updating to HTTPS.

name: wp_get_update_php_annotation
description: Returns the default annotation for the web hosting altering the "Update PHP" page URL. This function is to be used after {@see wp_get_update_php_url()} to return a consistent annotation if the web host has altered the default "Update PHP" page URL.
returnDescription: Update PHP page annotation. An empty string if no custom URLs are provided.

name: wp_get_update_php_url
description: Gets the URL to learn more about updating the PHP version the site is running on. This URL can be overridden by specifying an environment variable `WP_UPDATE_PHP_URL` or by using the {@see 'wp_update_php_url'} filter. Providing an empty string is not allowed and will result in the default URL being used. Furthermore the page the URL links to should preferably be localized in the site language.
returnDescription: URL to learn more about updating PHP.

name: wp_get_upload_dir
description: Retrieves uploads directory information. Same as wp_upload_dir() but "light weight" as it doesn't attempt to create the uploads directory. Intended for use in themes, when only 'basedir' and 'baseurl' are needed, generally in all cases when not uploading files.
returnDescription: See wp_upload_dir() for description.

name: wp_get_user_contact_methods
description: Sets up the user contact methods. Default contact methods were removed for new installations in WordPress 3.6 and completely removed from the codebase in WordPress 6.9. Use the {@see 'user_contactmethods'} filter to add or remove contact methods.

params.name: user
params.description: Optional. WP_User object.

returnDescription: Array of contact method labels keyed by contact method.

name: wp_get_user_request
description: Returns the user request object for the specified request ID.

params.name: request_id
params.description: The ID of the user request.

name: wp_get_user_request_data
description: Return the user request object for the specified request ID.

params.name: request_id
params.description: The ID of the user request.

name: wp_get_users_with_no_role
description: Gets the user IDs of all users with no role on this site.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Optional. The site ID to get users with no role for. Defaults to the current site.

returnDescription: Array of user IDs as strings.

name: wp_get_video_extensions
description: Returns a filtered list of supported video formats.
returnDescription: List of supported video formats.

name: wp_get_webp_info
description: Extracts meta information about a WebP file: width, height, and type.

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to a WebP file.

returnDescription: { An array of WebP image information. @type int|false $width Image width on success, false on failure. @type int|false $height Image height on success, false on failure. @type string|false $type The WebP type: one of 'lossy', 'lossless' or 'animated-alpha'. False on failure. }

name: wp_get_widget_defaults
description: Retrieves default registered sidebars list.

name: wp_get_word_count_type
description: Retrieves the word count type based on the locale.
returnDescription: Locale-specific word count type. Possible values are `characters_excluding_spaces`, `characters_including_spaces`, or `words`. Defaults to `words`.

name: wp_get_wp_version
description: Returns the current WordPress version. Returns an unmodified value of `$wp_version`. Some plugins modify the global in an attempt to improve security through obscurity. This practice can cause errors in WordPress, so the ability to get an unmodified version is needed.
returnDescription: The current WordPress version.

name: wp_getimagesize
description: Allows PHP's getimagesize() to be debuggable when necessary.

params.name: filename
params.description: The file path.

params.name: image_info
params.description: Optional. Extended image information (passed by reference).

returnDescription: Array of image information or false on failure.

name: wp_global_styles_render_svg_filters
description: Renders the SVG filters supplied by theme.json. Note that this doesn't render the per-block user-defined filters which are handled by wp_render_duotone_support, but it should be rendered before the filtered content in the body to satisfy Safari's rendering quirks.

name: wp_guess_url
description: Guesses the URL for the site. Will remove wp-admin links to retrieve only return URLs not in the wp-admin directory.
returnDescription: The guessed URL.

name: wp_handle_comment_submission
description: Handles the submission of a comment, usually posted to wp-comments-post.php via a comment form. This function expects unslashed data, as opposed to functions such as `wp_new_comment()` which expect slashed data.

params.name: comment_data
params.description: { Comment data. @type string|int $comment_post_ID The ID of the post that relates to the comment. @type string $author The name of the comment author. @type string $email The comment author email address. @type string $url The comment author URL. @type string $comment The content of the comment. @type string|int $comment_parent The ID of this comment's parent, if any. Default 0. @type string $_wp_unfiltered_html_comment The nonce value for allowing unfiltered HTML. }

returnDescription: A WP_Comment object on success, a WP_Error object on failure.

name: wp_handle_sideload
description: Wrapper for _wp_handle_upload(). Passes the {@see 'wp_handle_sideload'} action.

params.name: file
params.description: Reference to a single element of `$_FILES`. Call the function once for each uploaded file. See _wp_handle_upload() for accepted values.

params.name: overrides
params.description: Optional. An associative array of names => values to override default variables. Default false. See _wp_handle_upload() for accepted values.

params.name: time
params.description: Optional. Time formatted in 'yyyy/mm'. Default null.

returnDescription: See _wp_handle_upload() for return value.

name: wp_handle_upload
description: Wrapper for _wp_handle_upload(). Passes the {@see 'wp_handle_upload'} action.

params.name: file
params.description: Reference to a single element of `$_FILES`. Call the function once for each uploaded file. See _wp_handle_upload() for accepted values.

params.name: overrides
params.description: Optional. An associative array of names => values to override default variables. Default false. See _wp_handle_upload() for accepted values.

params.name: time
params.description: Optional. Time formatted in 'yyyy/mm'. Default null.

returnDescription: See _wp_handle_upload() for return value.

name: wp_has_ability
description: Checks if an ability is registered. Use this for conditional logic and feature detection before attempting to retrieve or use an ability. Example: // Displays different UI based on available abilities. if ( wp_has_ability( 'premium-plugin/advanced-export' ) ) { echo 'Export with Premium Features'; } else { echo 'Basic Export'; }

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the ability to check, including namespace prefix (e.g., 'my-plugin/my-ability').

returnDescription: `true` if the ability is registered, `false` otherwise.

name: wp_has_ability_category
description: Checks if an ability category is registered. Use this for conditional logic and feature detection before attempting to retrieve or use an ability category. Example: // Displays different UI based on available ability categories. if ( wp_has_ability_category( 'premium-features' ) ) { echo 'Premium Features Available'; } else { echo 'Standard Features'; }

params.name: slug
params.description: The slug of the ability category to check.

returnDescription: `true` if the ability category is registered, `false` otherwise.

name: wp_has_border_feature_support
description: Checks whether the current block type supports the border feature requested. If the `__experimentalBorder` support flag is a boolean `true` all border support features are available. Otherwise, the specific feature's support flag nested under `experimentalBorder` must be enabled for the feature to be opted into.

params.name: block_type
params.description: Block type to check for support.

params.name: feature
params.description: Name of the feature to check support for.

params.name: default_value
params.description: Fallback value for feature support, defaults to false.

returnDescription: Whether the feature is supported.

name: wp_has_noncharacters
description: Returns whether the given string contains Unicode noncharacters. XML recommends against using noncharacters and HTML forbids their use in attribute names. Unicode recommends that they not be used in open exchange of data. Noncharacters are code points within the following ranges: - U+FDD0–U+FDEF - U+FFFE–U+FFFF - U+1FFFE, U+1FFFF, U+2FFFE, U+2FFFF, …, U+10FFFE, U+10FFFF

params.name: text
params.description: Are there noncharacters in this string?

returnDescription: Whether noncharacters were found in the string.

name: wp_hash
description: Gets the hash of the given string. The default algorithm is md5 but can be changed to any algorithm supported by `hash_hmac()`. Use the `hash_hmac_algos()` function to check the supported algorithms.

params.name: data
params.description: Plain text to hash.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Authentication scheme (auth, secure_auth, logged_in, nonce).

params.name: algo
params.description: Hashing algorithm to use. Default: 'md5'.

returnDescription: Hash of $data.

name: wp_hash_password
description: Creates a hash of a plain text password. For integration with other applications, this function can be overwritten to instead use the other package password hashing algorithm.

params.name: password
params.description: Plain text user password to hash.

returnDescription: The hash string of the password.

name: wp_head
description: Fires the wp_head action. See {@see 'wp_head'}.

name: wp_heartbeat_set_suspension
description: Disables suspension of Heartbeat on the Add/Edit Post screens.

params.name: settings
params.description: An array of Heartbeat settings.

returnDescription: Filtered Heartbeat settings.

name: wp_heartbeat_settings
description: Default settings for heartbeat. Outputs the nonce used in the heartbeat XHR.
returnDescription: Heartbeat settings.

name: wp_high_priority_element_flag
description: Accesses a flag that indicates if an element is a possible candidate for `fetchpriority='high'`.

params.name: value
params.description: Optional. Used to change the static variable. Default null.

returnDescription: Returns true if high-priority element was marked already, otherwise false.

name: wp_hoist_late_printed_styles
description: Adds the hooks needed for CSS output to be delayed until after the content of the page has been established.

name: wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name
description: Returns a corresponding HTML attribute name for the given name, if that name were found in a JS element’s `dataset` property. Example: 'data-post-id' === wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name( 'postId' ); 'data--before' === wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name( 'Before' ); 'data---one---two---' === wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name( '-One--Two---' ); // Not every attribute name will be interpreted as a custom data attribute. null === wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name( '/not-an-attribute/' ); null === wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name( 'no spaces' ); // Some very surprising names will; for example, a property whose name is the empty string. 'data-' === wp_html_custom_data_attribute_name( '' );

params.name: js_dataset_name
params.description: Name of JS `dataset` property to transform.

returnDescription: Corresponding name of an HTML custom data attribute for the given dataset name, if possible to represent in HTML, otherwise `null`.

name: wp_html_excerpt
description: Safely extracts not more than the first $count characters from HTML string. UTF-8, tags and entities safe prefix extraction. Entities inside will *NOT* be counted as one character. For example & will be counted as 4, < as 3, etc.

params.name: str
params.description: String to get the excerpt from.

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of characters to take.

params.name: more
params.description: Optional. What to append if $str needs to be trimmed. Defaults to empty string.

returnDescription: The excerpt.

name: wp_html_split
description: Separates HTML elements and comments from the text.

params.name: input
params.description: The text which has to be formatted.

returnDescription: Array of the formatted text.

name: wp_htmledit_pre
description: Formats text for the HTML editor. Unless $output is empty it will pass through htmlspecialchars before the {@see 'htmledit_pre'} filter is applied.

params.name: output
params.description: The text to be formatted.

returnDescription: Formatted text after filter applied.

name: wp_http_supports
description: Determines if there is an HTTP Transport that can process this request.

params.name: capabilities
params.description: Array of capabilities to test or a wp_remote_request() $args array.

name: wp_http_validate_url
description: Validates a URL as safe for use in the HTTP API. The only supported protocols are `http` and `https`. Examples of URLs that are considered unsafe: - `ftp://example.com/caniload.php` - Invalid protocol - only http and https are allowed. - `http:///example.com/caniload.php` - Malformed URL. - `http://user:pass@example.com/caniload.php` - Login information. - `http://example.invalid/caniload.php` - Invalid hostname, as the IP cannot be looked up in DNS. Examples of URLs that are considered unsafe by default but can be allowed with filters: - `http://192.168.0.1/caniload.php` - IP address from LAN network. This can be changed with the {@see 'http_request_host_is_external'} filter. - `http://198.143.164.252:81/caniload.php` - By default, only ports 80, 443, and 8080 are allowed. This can be changed with the {@see 'http_allowed_safe_ports'} filter.

params.name: url
params.description: Request URL.

returnDescription: Returns false if the URL is not safe, or the original URL if it is safe.

name: wp_img_tag_add_decoding_attr
description: Adds `decoding` attribute to an `img` HTML tag. The `decoding` attribute allows developers to indicate whether the browser can decode the image off the main thread (`async`), on the main thread (`sync`) or as determined by the browser (`auto`). By default WordPress adds `decoding="async"` to images but developers can use the {@see 'wp_img_tag_add_decoding_attr'} filter to modify this to remove the attribute or set it to another accepted value.

params.name: image
params.description: The HTML `img` tag where the attribute should be added.

params.name: context
params.description: Additional context to pass to the filters.

returnDescription: Converted `img` tag with `decoding` attribute added.

description: Adds `loading` attribute to an `img` HTML tag.

Default is 'Site %d' where %d is the site ID. @type array $options Custom option $key => $value pairs to use. Default empty array. @type array $meta Custom site metadata $key => $value pairs to use. Default empty array. }

returnDescription: True on success, or error object on failure.

name: wp_initialize_site_preview_hooks

description: Add filters and actions to enable Block Theme Previews in the Site Editor. The filters and actions should be added after `pluggable.php` is included as they may trigger code that uses `current_user_can()` which requires functionality from `pluggable.php`.

name: wp_insert_attachment
description: Inserts an attachment. If you set the 'ID' in the $args parameter, it will mean that you are updating and attempt to update the attachment. You can also set the attachment name or title by setting the key 'post_name' or 'post_title'. You can set the dates for the attachment manually by setting the 'post_date' and 'post_date_gmt' keys' values. By default, the comments will use the default settings for whether the comments are allowed. You can close them manually or keep them open by setting the value for the 'comment_status' key.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments for inserting an attachment.

params.name: file
params.description: Optional. Filename. Default false.

params.name: parent_post_id
params.description: Optional. Parent post ID or 0 for no parent. Default 0.

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a WP_Error on failure. Default false.

params.name: fire_after_hooks
params.description: Optional. Whether to fire the after insert hooks. Default true.

returnDescription: The attachment ID on success. The value 0 or WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_insert_category
description: Updates an existing Category or creates a new Category.

params.name: catarr
params.description: { Array of arguments for inserting a new category. @type int $cat_ID Category ID. A non-zero value updates an existing category. Default 0. @type string $taxonomy Taxonomy slug. Default 'category'. @type string $cat_name Category name. Default empty. @type string $category_description Category description. Default empty. @type string $category_nicename Category nice (display) name. Default empty. @type int|string $category_parent Category parent ID. Default empty. }

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. Default false.

returnDescription: The ID number of the new or updated Category on success. Zero or a WP_Error on failure, depending on param `$wp_error`.

name: wp_insert_comment
description: Inserts a comment into the database.

params.name: commentdata
params.description: { Array of arguments for inserting a new comment. @type string $comment_agent The HTTP user agent of the `$comment_author` when the comment was submitted. Default empty. @type int|string $comment_approved Whether the comment has been approved. Default 1. @type string $comment_author The name of the author of the comment. Default empty. @type string $comment_author_email The email address of the `$comment_author`. Default empty. @type string $comment_author_IP The IP address of the `$comment_author`. Default empty. @type string $comment_author_url The URL address of the `$comment_author`. Default empty. @type string $comment_content The content of the comment. Default empty. @type string $comment_date The date the comment was submitted. To set the date manually, `$comment_date_gmt` must also be specified. Default is the current time. @type string $comment_date_gmt The date the comment was submitted in the GMT timezone. Default is `$comment_date` in the site's GMT timezone. @type int $comment_karma The karma of the comment. Default 0. @type int $comment_parent ID of this comment's parent, if any. Default 0. @type int $comment_post_ID ID of the post that relates to the comment, if any. Default 0. @type string $comment_type Comment type. Default 'comment'. @type array $comment_meta Optional. Array of key/value pairs to be stored in commentmeta for the new comment. @type int $user_id ID of the user who submitted the comment. Default 0. }

returnDescription: The new comment's ID on success, false on failure.

name: wp_insert_link
description: Inserts a link into the database, or updates an existing link. Runs all the necessary sanitizing, provides default values if arguments are missing, and finally saves the link.

params.name: linkdata
params.description: { Elements that make up the link to insert. @type int $link_id Optional. The ID of the existing link if updating. @type string $link_url The URL the link points to. @type string $link_name The title of the link. @type string $link_image Optional. A URL of an image. @type string $link_target Optional. The target element for the anchor tag. @type string $link_description Optional. A short description of the link. @type string $link_visible Optional. 'Y' means visible, anything else means not. @type int $link_owner Optional. A user ID. @type int $link_rating Optional. A rating for the link. @type string $link_rel Optional. A relationship of the link to you. @type string $link_notes Optional. An extended description of or notes on the link. @type string $link_rss Optional. A URL of an associated RSS feed. @type int $link_category Optional. The term ID of the link category. If empty, uses default link category. }

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a WP_Error object on failure. Default false.

returnDescription: Value 0 or WP_Error on failure. The link ID on success.

name: wp_insert_post
description: Inserts or update a post. If the $postarr parameter has 'ID' set to a value, then post will be updated. You can set the post date manually, by setting the values for 'post_date' and 'post_date_gmt' keys. You can close the comments or open the comments by setting the value for 'comment_status' key.

params.name: postarr
params.description: { An array of elements that make up a post to update or insert. @type int $ID The post ID. If equal to something other than 0, the post with that ID will be updated. Default 0. @type int $post_author The ID of the user who added the post. Default is the current user ID. @type string $post_date The date of the post. Default is the current time. @type string $post_date_gmt The date of the post in the GMT timezone. Default is the value of `$post_date`. @type string $post_content The post content. Default empty. @type string $post_content_filtered The filtered post content. Default empty. @type string $post_title The post title. Default empty. @type string $post_excerpt The post excerpt. Default empty. @type string $post_status The post status. Default 'draft'. @type string $post_type The post type. Default 'post'. @type string $comment_status Whether the post can accept comments. Accepts 'open' or 'closed'. Default is the value of 'default_comment_status' option. @type string $ping_status Whether the post can accept pings. Accepts 'open' or 'closed'. Default is the value of 'default_ping_status' option. @type string $post_password The password to access the post. Default empty. @type string $post_name The post name. Default is the sanitized post title when creating a new post. @type string $to_ping Space or carriage return-separated list of URLs to ping. Default empty. @type string $pinged Space or carriage return-separated list of URLs that have been pinged. Default empty. @type int $post_parent Set this for the post it belongs to, if any. Default 0. @type int $menu_order The order the post should be displayed in. Default 0. @type string $post_mime_type The mime type of the post. Default empty. @type string $guid Global Unique ID for referencing the post. Default empty. @type int $import_id The post ID to be used when inserting a new post. If specified, must not match any existing post ID. Default 0. @type int[] $post_category Array of category IDs. Defaults to value of the 'default_category' option. @type array $tags_input Array of tag names, slugs, or IDs. Default empty. @type array $tax_input An array of taxonomy terms keyed by their taxonomy name. If the taxonomy is hierarchical, the term list needs to be either an array of term IDs or a comma-separated string of IDs. If the taxonomy is non-hierarchical, the term list can be an array that contains term names or slugs, or a comma-separated string of names or slugs. This is because, in hierarchical taxonomy, child terms can have the same names with different parent terms, so the only way to connect them is using ID. Default empty. @type array $meta_input Array of post meta values keyed by their post meta key. Default empty. @type string $page_template Page template to use. }

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a WP_Error on failure. Default false.

params.name: fire_after_hooks
params.description: Optional. Whether to fire the after insert hooks. Default true.

returnDescription: The post ID on success. The value 0 or WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_insert_site
description: Inserts a new site into the database.

params.name: data
params.description: { Data for the new site that should be inserted. @type string $domain Site domain. Default empty string. @type string $path Site path. Default '/'. @type int $network_id The site's network ID. Default is the current network ID. @type string $registered When the site was registered, in SQL datetime format. Default is the current time. @type string $last_updated When the site was last updated, in SQL datetime format. Default is the value of $registered. @type int $public Whether the site is public. Default 1. @type int $archived Whether the site is archived. Default 0. @type int $mature Whether the site is mature. Default 0. @type int $spam Whether the site is spam. Default 0. @type int $deleted Whether the site is deleted. Default 0. @type int $lang_id The site's language ID. Currently unused. Default 0. @type int $user_id User ID for the site administrator. Passed to the `wp_initialize_site` hook. @type string $title Site title. Default is 'Site %d' where %d is the site ID. Passed to the `wp_initialize_site` hook. @type array $options Custom option $key => $value pairs to use. Default empty array. Passed to the `wp_initialize_site` hook. @type array $meta Custom site metadata $key => $value pairs to use. Default empty array. Passed to the `wp_initialize_site` hook. }

returnDescription: The new site's ID on success, or error object on failure.

name: wp_insert_term
description: Adds a new term to the database. A non-existent term is inserted in the following sequence: 1. The term is added to the term table, then related to the taxonomy. 2. If everything is correct, several actions are fired. 3. The 'term_id_filter' is evaluated. 4. The term cache is cleaned. 5. Several more actions are fired. 6. An array is returned containing the `term_id` and `term_taxonomy_id`. If the 'slug' argument is not empty, then it is checked to see if the term is invalid. If it is not a valid, existing term, it is added and the term_id is given. If the taxonomy is hierarchical, and the 'parent' argument is not empty, the term is inserted and the term_id will be given. Error handling: If `$taxonomy` does not exist or `$term` is empty, a WP_Error object will be returned. If the term already exists on the same hierarchical level, or the term slug and name are not unique, a WP_Error object will be returned.

params.name: term
params.description: The term name to add.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: The taxonomy to which to add the term.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Array or query string of arguments for inserting a term. @type string $alias_of Slug of the term to make this term an alias of. Default empty string. Accepts a term slug. @type string $description The term description. Default empty string. @type int $parent The id of the parent term. Default 0. @type string $slug The term slug to use. Default empty string. }

returnDescription: { An array of the new term data, WP_Error otherwise. @type int $term_id The new term ID. @type int|string $term_taxonomy_id The new term taxonomy ID. Can be a numeric string. }

name: wp_insert_user
description: Inserts a user into the database. Most of the `$userdata` array fields have filters associated with the values. Exceptions are 'ID', 'rich_editing', 'syntax_highlighting', 'comment_shortcuts', 'admin_color', 'use_ssl', 'user_registered', 'user_activation_key', 'spam', and 'role'. The filters have the prefix 'pre_user_' followed by the field name. An example using 'description' would have the filter called 'pre_user_description' that can be hooked into.

params.name: userdata
params.description: { An array, object, or WP_User object of user data arguments. @type int $ID User ID. If supplied, the user will be updated. @type string $user_pass The plain-text user password for new users. Hashed password for existing users. @type string $user_login The user's login username. @type string $user_nicename The URL-friendly user name. @type string $user_url The user URL. @type string $user_email The user email address. @type string $display_name The user's display name. Default is the user's username. @type string $nickname The user's nickname. Default is the user's username. @type string $first_name The user's first name. For new users, will be used to build the first part of the user's display name if `$display_name` is not specified. @type string $last_name The user's last name. For new users, will be used to build the second part of the user's display name if `$display_name` is not specified. @type string $description The user's biographical description. @type string $rich_editing Whether to enable the rich-editor for the user. Accepts 'true' or 'false' as a string literal, not boolean. Default 'true'. @type string $syntax_highlighting Whether to enable the rich code editor for the user. Accepts 'true' or 'false' as a string literal, not boolean. Default 'true'. @type string $comment_shortcuts Whether to enable comment moderation keyboard shortcuts for the user. Accepts 'true' or 'false' as a string literal, not boolean. Default 'false'. @type string $admin_color Admin color scheme for the user. Default 'fresh'. @type bool $use_ssl Whether the user should always access the admin over https. Default false. @type string $user_registered Date the user registered in UTC. Format is 'Y-m-d H:i:s'. @type string $user_activation_key Password reset key. Default empty. @type bool $spam Multisite only. Whether the user is marked as spam.
