description: Checks whether separate styles should be loaded for core blocks. When this function returns true, other functions ensure that core blocks use their own separate stylesheets. When this function returns false, all core blocks will use the single combined 'wp-block-library' stylesheet. As a side effect, the return value will by default result in block assets to be loaded on demand, via the {@see wp_should_load_block_assets_on_demand()} function. This behavior can be separately altered via that function. This only affects front end and not the block editor screens.
returnDescription: Whether separate core block assets will be loaded.

name: wp_should_output_buffer_template_for_enhancement
description: Checks whether the template should be output buffered for enhancement. By default, an output buffer is only started if a {@see 'wp_template_enhancement_output_buffer'} filter has been added by the time a template is included at the {@see 'wp_before_include_template'} action. This allows template responses to be streamed as much as possible when no template enhancements are registered to apply.
returnDescription: Whether the template should be output-buffered for enhancement.

name: wp_should_replace_insecure_home_url
description: Checks whether WordPress should replace old HTTP URLs to the site with their HTTPS counterpart. If a WordPress site had its URL changed from HTTP to HTTPS, by default this will return `true`, causing WordPress to add frontend filters to replace insecure site URLs that may be present in older database content. The {@see 'wp_should_replace_insecure_home_url'} filter can be used to modify that behavior.
returnDescription: True if insecure URLs should replaced, false otherwise.

name: wp_should_skip_block_supports_serialization
description: Checks whether serialization of the current block's supported properties should occur.

params.name: block_type
params.description: Block type.

params.name: feature_set
params.description: Name of block support feature set..

params.name: feature
params.description: Optional name of individual feature to check.

returnDescription: Whether to serialize block support styles & classes.

name: wp_should_upgrade_global_tables
description: Determine if global tables should be upgraded. This function performs a series of checks to ensure the environment allows for the safe upgrading of global WordPress database tables. It is necessary because global tables will commonly grow to millions of rows on large installations, and the ability to control their upgrade routines can be critical to the operation of large networks. In a future iteration, this function may use `wp_is_large_network()` to more- intelligently prevent global table upgrades. Until then, we make sure WordPress is on the main site of the main network, to avoid running queries more than once in multi-site or multi-network environments.
returnDescription: Whether to run the upgrade routines on global tables.

name: wp_show_heic_upload_error
description: Callback to enable showing of the user error when uploading .heic images.

params.name: plupload_settings
params.description: The settings for Plupload.js.

returnDescription: Modified settings for Plupload.js.

name: wp_shrink_dimensions
description: Calculates the new dimensions for a downsampled image.

params.name: width
params.description: Current width of the image

params.name: height
params.description: Current height of the image

params.name: wmax
params.description: Maximum wanted width

params.name: hmax
params.description: Maximum wanted height

returnDescription: Shrunk dimensions (width, height).

name: wp_sidebar_description
description: Retrieves description for a sidebar. When registering sidebars a 'description' parameter can be included that describes the sidebar for display on the widget administration panel.

params.name: id
params.description: sidebar ID.

returnDescription: Sidebar description, if available.

name: wp_signon
description: Authenticates and logs a user in with 'remember' capability. The credentials is an array that has 'user_login', 'user_password', and 'remember' indices. If the credentials is not given, then the log in form will be assumed and used if set. The various authentication cookies will be set by this function and will be set for a longer period depending on if the 'remember' credential is set to true. Note: wp_signon() doesn't handle setting the current user. This means that if the function is called before the {@see 'init'} hook is fired, is_user_logged_in() will evaluate as false until that point. If is_user_logged_in() is needed in conjunction with wp_signon(), wp_set_current_user() should be called explicitly.

params.name: credentials
params.description: { Optional. User info in order to sign on. @type string $user_login Username. @type string $user_password User password. @type bool $remember Whether to 'remember' the user. Increases the time that the cookie will be kept. Default false. }

returnDescription: WP_User on success, WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_simplepie_autoload
description: WordPress autoloader for SimplePie.

params.name: class
params.description: Class name.

name: wp_site_admin_email_change_notification
description: Sends an email to the old site admin email address when the site admin email address changes.

params.name: old_email
params.description: The old site admin email address.

params.name: new_email
params.description: The new site admin email address.

params.name: option_name
params.description: The relevant database option name.

name: wp_site_icon
description: Displays site icon meta tags.

name: wp_sitemaps_get_max_urls
description: Gets the maximum number of URLs for a sitemap.

params.name: object_type
params.description: Object type for sitemap to be filtered (e.g. 'post', 'term', 'user').

returnDescription: The maximum number of URLs.

name: wp_sitemaps_get_server
description: Retrieves the current Sitemaps server instance.
returnDescription: Sitemaps instance.

name: wp_sizes_attribute_includes_valid_auto
description: Checks whether the given 'sizes' attribute includes the 'auto' keyword as the first item in the list. Per the HTML spec, if present it must be the first entry.

params.name: sizes_attr
params.description: The 'sizes' attribute value.

returnDescription: True if the 'auto' keyword is present, false otherwise.

name: wp_skip_border_serialization
description: Checks whether serialization of the current block's border properties should occur.

params.name: block_type
params.description: Block type.

returnDescription: Whether serialization of the current block's border properties should occur.

name: wp_skip_dimensions_serialization
description: Checks whether serialization of the current block's dimensions properties should occur.

params.name: block_type
params.description: Block type.

returnDescription: Whether to serialize spacing support styles & classes.

name: wp_skip_paused_plugins
description: Filters a given list of plugins, removing any paused plugins from it.

params.name: plugins
params.description: Array of absolute plugin main file paths.

returnDescription: Filtered array of plugins, without any paused plugins.

name: wp_skip_paused_themes
description: Filters a given list of themes, removing any paused themes from it.

params.name: themes
params.description: Array of absolute theme directory paths.

returnDescription: Filtered array of absolute paths to themes, without any paused themes.

name: wp_skip_spacing_serialization
description: Checks whether serialization of the current block's spacing properties should occur.

params.name: block_type
params.description: Block type.

returnDescription: Whether to serialize spacing support styles & classes.

name: wp_slash
description: Adds slashes to a string or recursively adds slashes to strings within an array. This should be used when preparing data for core API that expects slashed data. This should not be used to escape data going directly into an SQL query.

params.name: value
params.description: String or array of data to slash.

returnDescription: Slashed `$value`, in the same type as supplied.

name: wp_slash_strings_only
description: Adds slashes to only string values in an array of values. This should be used when preparing data for core APIs that expect slashed data. This should not be used to escape data going directly into an SQL query.

params.name: value
params.description: Scalar or array of scalars.

returnDescription: Slashes $value

name: wp_spaces_regexp
description: Returns the regexp for common whitespace characters. By default, spaces include new lines, tabs, nbsp entities, and the UTF-8 nbsp. This is designed to replace the PCRE \s sequence. In ticket #22692, that sequence was found to be unreliable due to random inclusion of the A0 byte.
returnDescription: The spaces regexp.

name: wp_spam_comment
description: Marks a comment as Spam.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Comment ID or WP_Comment object.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_specialchars
description: Legacy escaping for HTML blocks.

params.name: text
params.description: Text to escape.

params.name: quote_style
params.description: Unused.

params.name: charset
params.description: Unused.

params.name: double_encode
params.description: Whether to double encode. Unused.

returnDescription: Escaped `$text`.

name: wp_specialchars_decode
description: Converts a number of HTML entities into their special characters. Specifically deals with: `&`, ` `, `"`, and `'`. `$quote_style` can be set to ENT_COMPAT to decode `"` entities, or ENT_QUOTES to do both `"` and `'`. Default is ENT_NOQUOTES where no quotes are decoded.

params.name: text
params.description: The text which is to be decoded.

params.name: quote_style
params.description: Optional. Converts double quotes if set to ENT_COMPAT, both single and double if set to ENT_QUOTES or none if set to ENT_NOQUOTES. Also compatible with old _wp_specialchars() values; converting single quotes if set to 'single', double if set to 'double' or both if otherwise set. Default is ENT_NOQUOTES.

returnDescription: The decoded text without HTML entities.

name: wp_sprintf
description: WordPress' implementation of PHP sprintf() with filters.

params.name: pattern
params.description: The string which formatted args are inserted.

returnDescription: The formatted string.

name: wp_sprintf_l
description: Localizes list items before the rest of the content. The '%l' must be at the first characters can then contain the rest of the content. The list items will have ', ', ', and', and ' and ' added depending on the amount of list items in the $args parameter.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Content containing '%l' at the beginning.

params.name: args
params.description: List items to prepend to the content and replace '%l'.

returnDescription: Localized list items and rest of the content.

name: wp_ssl_constants
description: Defines SSL-related WordPress constants.

name: wp_star_rating
description: Outputs a HTML element with a star rating for a given rating. Outputs a HTML element with the star rating exposed on a 0..5 scale in half star increments (ie. 1, 1.5, 2 stars). Optionally, if specified, the number of ratings may also be displayed by passing the $number parameter.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Array of star ratings arguments. @type int|float $rating The rating to display, expressed in either a 0.5 rating increment, or percentage. Default 0. @type string $type Format that the $rating is in. Valid values are 'rating' (default), or, 'percent'. Default 'rating'. @type int $number The number of ratings that makes up this rating. Default 0. @type bool $echo Whether to echo the generated markup. False to return the markup instead of echoing it. Default true. }

returnDescription: Star rating HTML.

name: wp_start_object_cache
description: Starts the WordPress object cache. If an object-cache.php file exists in the wp-content directory, it uses that drop-in as an external object cache.

name: wp_start_scraping_edited_file_errors
description: Starts scraping edited file errors.

name: wp_start_template_enhancement_output_buffer
description: Starts the template enhancement output buffer. This function is called immediately before the template is included.
returnDescription: Whether the output buffer successfully started.

name: wp_staticize_emoji
description: Converts emoji to a static img element.

params.name: text
params.description: The content to encode.

returnDescription: The encoded content.

name: wp_staticize_emoji_for_email
description: Converts emoji in emails into static images.

params.name: mail
params.description: The email data array.

returnDescription: The email data array, with emoji in the message staticized.

name: wp_stream_image
description: Streams image in WP_Image_Editor to browser.

params.name: image
params.description: The image editor instance.

params.name: mime_type
params.description: The mime type of the image.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: The image's attachment post ID.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_strict_cross_origin_referrer
description: Displays a referrer `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` meta tag. Outputs a referrer `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` meta tag that tells the browser not to send the full URL as a referrer to other sites when cross-origin assets are loaded. Typical usage is as a {@see 'wp_head'} callback: add_action( 'wp_head', 'wp_strict_cross_origin_referrer' );

name: wp_strip_all_tags
description: Properly strips all HTML tags including 'script' and 'style'. This differs from strip_tags() because it removes the contents of the ` ` and ` ` tags. E.g. `strip_tags( ' something ' )` will return 'something'. wp_strip_all_tags() will return an empty string.

params.name: text
params.description: String containing HTML tags

params.name: remove_breaks
params.description: Optional. Whether to remove left over line breaks and white space chars

returnDescription: The processed string.

name: wp_style_add_data
description: Adds metadata to a CSS stylesheet. Works only if the stylesheet has already been registered. Possible values for $key and $value: 'rtl' bool|string To declare an RTL stylesheet. 'suffix' string Optional suffix, used in combination with RTL. 'alt' bool For rel="alternate stylesheet". 'title' string For preferred/alternate stylesheets. 'path' string The absolute path to a stylesheet. Stylesheet will load inline when 'path' is set.

params.name: handle
params.description: Name of the stylesheet.

params.name: key
params.description: Name of data point for which we're storing a value. Accepts 'rtl' and 'suffix', 'alt', 'title' and 'path'.

params.name: value
params.description: String containing the CSS data to be added.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_style_engine_get_styles
description: Global public interface method to generate styles from a single style object, e.g. the value of a block's attributes.style object or the top level styles in theme.json. Example usage: $styles = wp_style_engine_get_styles( array( 'color' => array( 'text' => '#cccccc' ), ) ); Returns: array( 'css' => 'color: #cccccc', 'declarations' => array( 'color' => '#cccccc' ), 'classnames' => 'has-color', )

params.name: block_styles
params.description: The style object.

params.name: options
params.description: { Optional. An array of options. Default empty array. @type string|null $context An identifier describing the origin of the style object, e.g. 'block-supports' or 'global-styles'. Default null. When set, the style engine will attempt to store the CSS rules, where a selector is also passed. @type bool $convert_vars_to_classnames Whether to skip converting incoming CSS var patterns, e.g. `var:preset| | `, to `var( --wp--preset--* )` values. Default false. @type string $selector Optional. When a selector is passed, the value of `$css` in the return value will comprise a full CSS rule `$selector { ...$css_declarations }`, otherwise, the value will be a concatenated string of CSS declarations. }

returnDescription: { @type string $css A CSS ruleset or declarations block formatted to be placed in an HTML `style` attribute or tag. @type string[] $declarations An associative array of CSS definitions, e.g. `array( "$property" => "$value", "$property" => "$value" )`. @type string $classnames Classnames separated by a space. }

name: wp_style_engine_get_stylesheet_from_context
description: Returns compiled CSS from a store, if found.

params.name: context
params.description: A valid context name, corresponding to an existing store key.

params.name: options
params.description: { Optional. An array of options. Default empty array. @type bool $optimize Whether to optimize the CSS output, e.g. combine rules. Default false. @type bool $prettify Whether to add new lines and indents to output. Defaults to whether the `SCRIPT_DEBUG` constant is defined. }

returnDescription: A compiled CSS string.

name: wp_style_engine_get_stylesheet_from_css_rules
description: Returns compiled CSS from a collection of selectors and declarations. Useful for returning a compiled stylesheet from any collection of CSS selector + declarations. Example usage: $css_rules = array( array( 'selector' => '.elephant-are-cool', 'declarations' => array( 'color' => 'gray', 'width' => '3em', ), ), ); $css = wp_style_engine_get_stylesheet_from_css_rules( $css_rules ); Returns: .elephant-are-cool{color:gray;width:3em}

params.name: css_rules
params.description: { Required. A collection of CSS rules. @type array ...$0 { @type string $rules_group A parent CSS selector in the case of nested CSS, or a CSS nested @rule, such as `@media (min-width: 80rem)` or `@layer module`. @type string $selector A CSS selector. @type string[] $declarations An associative array of CSS definitions, e.g. `array( "$property" => "$value", "$property" => "$value" )`. } }

params.name: options
params.description: { Optional. An array of options. Default empty array. @type string|null $context An identifier describing the origin of the style object, e.g. 'block-supports' or 'global-styles'. Default 'block-supports'. When set, the style engine will attempt to store the CSS rules. @type bool $optimize Whether to optimize the CSS output, e.g. combine rules. Default false. @type bool $prettify Whether to add new lines and indents to output. Defaults to whether the `SCRIPT_DEBUG` constant is defined. }

returnDescription: A string of compiled CSS declarations, or empty string.

name: wp_style_is
description: Checks whether a CSS stylesheet has been added to the queue.

params.name: handle
params.description: Name of the stylesheet.

params.name: status
params.description: Optional. Status of the stylesheet to check. Default 'enqueued'. Accepts 'enqueued', 'registered', 'queue', 'to_do', and 'done'.

returnDescription: Whether style is queued.

name: wp_style_loader_src
description: Administration Screen CSS for changing the styles. If installing the 'wp-admin/' directory will be replaced with './'. The $_wp_admin_css_colors global manages the Administration Screens CSS stylesheet that is loaded. The option that is set is 'admin_color' and is the color and key for the array. The value for the color key is an object with a 'url' parameter that has the URL path to the CSS file. The query from $src parameter will be appended to the URL that is given from the $_wp_admin_css_colors array value URL.

params.name: src
params.description: Source URL.

params.name: handle
params.description: Either 'colors' or 'colors-rtl'.

returnDescription: URL path to CSS stylesheet for Administration Screens.

name: wp_styles
description: Initializes $wp_styles if it has not been set.
returnDescription: WP_Styles instance.

name: wp_suspend_cache_addition
description: Temporarily suspends cache additions. Stops more data being added to the cache, but still allows cache retrieval. This is useful for actions, such as imports, when a lot of data would otherwise be almost uselessly added to the cache. Suspension lasts for a single page load at most. Remember to call this function again if you wish to re-enable cache adds earlier.

params.name: suspend
params.description: Optional. Suspends additions if true, re-enables them if false. Defaults to not changing the current setting.

returnDescription: The current suspend setting.

name: wp_suspend_cache_invalidation
description: Suspends cache invalidation. Turns cache invalidation on and off. Useful during imports where you don't want to do invalidations every time a post is inserted. Callers must be sure that what they are doing won't lead to an inconsistent cache when invalidation is suspended.

params.name: suspend
params.description: Optional. Whether to suspend or enable cache invalidation. Default true.

returnDescription: The current suspend setting.

name: wp_switch_roles_and_user
description: Switches the initialized roles and current user capabilities to another site.

params.name: new_site_id
params.description: New site ID.

params.name: old_site_id
params.description: Old site ID.

name: wp_tag_cloud
description: Displays a tag cloud. Outputs a list of tags in what is called a 'tag cloud', where the size of each tag is determined by how many times that particular tag has been assigned to posts.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Array or string of arguments for displaying a tag cloud. See wp_generate_tag_cloud() and get_terms() for the full lists of arguments that can be passed in `$args`. @type int $number The number of tags to display. Accepts any positive integer or zero to return all. Default 45. @type string $link Whether to display term editing links or term permalinks. Accepts 'edit' and 'view'. Default 'view'. @type string $post_type The post type. Used to highlight the proper post type menu on the linked edit page. Defaults to the first post type associated with the taxonomy. @type bool $echo Whether or not to echo the return value. Default true. }

returnDescription: Void if 'echo' argument is true, or on failure. Otherwise, tag cloud as a string or an array, depending on 'format' argument.

name: wp_targeted_link_rel
description: Adds `rel="noopener"` to all HTML A elements that have a target.

params.name: text
params.description: Content that may contain HTML A elements.

returnDescription: Converted content.

name: wp_targeted_link_rel_callback
description: Callback to add `rel="noopener"` string to HTML A element. Will not duplicate an existing 'noopener' value to avoid invalidating the HTML.

params.name: matches
params.description: Single match.

returnDescription: HTML A Element with `rel="noopener"` in addition to any existing values.

name: wp_templating_constants
description: Defines templating-related WordPress constants.

name: wp_tempnam
description: Returns a filename of a temporary unique file. Please note that the calling function must delete or move the file. The filename is based off the passed parameter or defaults to the current unix timestamp, while the directory can either be passed as well, or by leaving it blank, default to a writable temporary directory.

params.name: filename
params.description: Optional. Filename to base the Unique file off. Default empty.

params.name: dir
params.description: Optional. Directory to store the file in. Default empty.

returnDescription: A writable filename.

name: wp_term_is_shared
description: Determines whether a term is shared between multiple taxonomies. Shared taxonomy terms began to be split in 4.3, but failed cron tasks or other delays in upgrade routines may cause shared terms to remain.

params.name: term_id
params.description: Term ID.

returnDescription: Returns false if a term is not shared between multiple taxonomies or if splitting shared taxonomy terms is finished.

name: wp_terms_checklist
description: Outputs an unordered list of checkbox input elements labelled with term names. Taxonomy-independent version of wp_category_checklist().

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. Post ID. Default 0.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Array or string of arguments for generating a terms checklist. Default empty array. @type int $descendants_and_self ID of the category to output along with its descendants. Default 0. @type int[] $selected_cats Array of category IDs to mark as checked. Default false. @type int[] $popular_cats Array of category IDs to receive the "popular-category" class. Default false. @type Walker $walker Walker object to use to build the output. Default empty which results in a Walker_Category_Checklist instance being used. @type string $taxonomy Taxonomy to generate the checklist for. Default 'category'. @type bool $checked_ontop Whether to move checked items out of the hierarchy and to the top of the list. Default true. @type bool $echo Whether to echo the generated markup. False to return the markup instead of echoing it. Default true. }

returnDescription: HTML list of input elements.

name: wp_text_diff
description: Displays a human readable HTML representation of the difference between two strings. The Diff is available for getting the changes between versions. The output is HTML, so the primary use is for displaying the changes. If the two strings are equivalent, then an empty string will be returned.

params.name: left_string
params.description: "old" (left) version of string.

params.name: right_string
params.description: "new" (right) version of string.

params.name: args
params.description: { Associative array of options to pass to WP_Text_Diff_Renderer_Table(). @type string $title Titles the diff in a manner compatible with the output. Default empty. @type string $title_left Change the HTML to the left of the title. Default empty. @type string $title_right Change the HTML to the right of the title. Default empty. @type bool $show_split_view True for split view (two columns), false for un-split view (single column). Default true. }

returnDescription: Empty string if strings are equivalent or HTML with differences.

name: wp_theme_auto_update_setting_template
description: Returns the JavaScript template used to display the auto-update setting for a theme.
returnDescription: The template for displaying the auto-update setting link.

name: wp_theme_get_element_class_name
description: Given an element name, returns a class name. Alias of WP_Theme_JSON::get_element_class_name.

params.name: element
params.description: The name of the element.

returnDescription: The name of the class.

name: wp_theme_has_theme_json
description: Checks whether a theme or its parent has a theme.json file.
returnDescription: Returns true if theme or its parent has a theme.json file, false otherwise.

name: wp_theme_update_row
description: Displays update information for a theme.

params.name: theme_key
params.description: Theme stylesheet.

params.name: theme
params.description: Theme object.

name: wp_theme_update_rows
description: Adds a callback to display update information for themes with updates available.

name: wp_throttle_comment_flood
description: Determines whether a comment should be blocked because of comment flood.

params.name: block
params.description: Whether plugin has already blocked comment.

params.name: time_lastcomment
params.description: Timestamp for last comment.

params.name: time_newcomment
params.description: Timestamp for new comment.

returnDescription: Whether comment should be blocked.

name: wp_timezone
description: Retrieves the timezone of the site as a `DateTimeZone` object. Timezone can be based on a PHP timezone string or a ±HH:MM offset.
returnDescription: Timezone object.

name: wp_timezone_choice
description: Gives a nicely-formatted list of timezone strings.

params.name: selected_zone
params.description: Selected timezone.

params.name: locale
params.description: Optional. Locale to load the timezones in. Default current site locale.

name: wp_timezone_override_offset
description: Modifies gmt_offset for smart timezone handling. Overrides the gmt_offset option if we have a timezone_string available.
returnDescription: Timezone GMT offset, false otherwise.

name: wp_timezone_string
description: Retrieves the timezone of the site as a string. Uses the `timezone_string` option to get a proper timezone name if available, otherwise falls back to a manual UTC ± offset. Example return values: - 'Europe/Rome' - 'America/North_Dakota/New_Salem' - 'UTC' - '-06:30' - '+00:00' - '+08:45'
returnDescription: PHP timezone name or a ±HH:MM offset.

name: wp_timezone_supported
description: Check for PHP timezone support

name: wp_tiny_mce
description: Outputs the TinyMCE editor.

name: wp_tinycolor_bound01
description: Takes input from [0, n] and returns it as [0, 1]. Direct port of TinyColor's function, lightly simplified to maintain consistency with TinyColor.

params.name: n
params.description: Number of unknown type.

params.name: max
params.description: Upper value of the range to bound to.

returnDescription: Value in the range [0, 1].

name: wp_tinycolor_hsl_to_rgb
description: Converts an HSL object to an RGB object with converted and rounded values. Direct port of TinyColor's function, lightly simplified to maintain consistency with TinyColor.

params.name: hsl_color
params.description: HSL object.

returnDescription: Rounded and converted RGB object.

name: wp_tinycolor_hue_to_rgb
description: Helper function for hsl to rgb conversion. Direct port of TinyColor's function, lightly simplified to maintain consistency with TinyColor.

params.name: p
params.description: first component.

params.name: q
params.description: second component.

params.name: t
params.description: third component.

returnDescription: R, G, or B component.

name: wp_tinycolor_rgb_to_rgb
description: Rounds and converts values of an RGB object. Direct port of TinyColor's function, lightly simplified to maintain consistency with TinyColor.

params.name: rgb_color
params.description: RGB object.

returnDescription: Rounded and converted RGB object.

name: wp_tinycolor_string_to_rgb
description: Parses hex, hsl, and rgb CSS strings using the same regex as TinyColor v1.4.2 used in the JavaScript. Only colors output from react-color are implemented. Direct port of TinyColor's function, lightly simplified to maintain consistency with TinyColor.

params.name: color_str
params.description: CSS color string.

returnDescription: RGB object.

name: wp_tinymce_inline_scripts
description: Adds inline scripts required for the TinyMCE in the block editor. These TinyMCE init settings are used to extend and override the default settings from `_WP_Editors::default_settings()` for the Classic block.

name: wp_title
description: Displays or retrieves page title for all areas of blog. By default, the page title will display the separator before the page title, so that the blog title will be before the page title. This is not good for title display, since the blog title shows up on most tabs and not what is important, which is the page that the user is looking at. There are also SEO benefits to having the blog title after or to the 'right' of the page title. However, it is mostly common sense to have the blog title to the right with most browsers supporting tabs. You can achieve this by using the seplocation parameter and setting the value to 'right'. This change was introduced around 2.5.0, in case backward compatibility of themes is important.

params.name: sep
params.description: Optional. How to separate the various items within the page title. Default '»'.

params.name: display
params.description: Optional. Whether to display or retrieve title. Default true.

params.name: seplocation
params.description: Optional. Location of the separator (either 'left' or 'right').

returnDescription: String when `$display` is false, nothing otherwise.

name: wp_title_rss
description: Displays the blog title for display of the feed title.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Unused.

name: wp_transition_comment_status
description: Calls hooks for when a comment status transition occurs. Calls hooks for comment status transitions. If the new comment status is not the same as the previous comment status, then two hooks will be ran, the first is {@see 'transition_comment_status'} with new status, old status, and comment data. The next action called is {@see 'comment_$old_status_to_$new_status'}. It has the comment data. The final action will run whether or not the comment statuses are the same. The action is named {@see 'comment_$new_status_$comment->comment_type'}.

params.name: new_status
params.description: New comment status.

params.name: old_status
params.description: Previous comment status.

params.name: comment
params.description: Comment object.

name: wp_transition_post_status
description: Fires actions related to the transitioning of a post's status. When a post is saved, the post status is "transitioned" from one status to another, though this does not always mean the status has actually changed before and after the save. This function fires a number of action hooks related to that transition: the generic {@see 'transition_post_status'} action, as well as the dynamic hooks {@see '$old_status_to_$new_status'} and {@see '$new_status_$post->post_type'}. Note that the function does not transition the post object in the database. For instance: When publishing a post for the first time, the post status may transition from 'draft' – or some other status – to 'publish'. However, if a post is already published and is simply being updated, the "old" and "new" statuses may both be 'publish' before and after the transition.

params.name: new_status
params.description: Transition to this post status.

params.name: old_status
params.description: Previous post status.

params.name: post
params.description: Post data.

name: wp_trash_comment
description: Moves a comment to the Trash If Trash is disabled, comment is permanently deleted.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Comment ID or WP_Comment object.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_trash_post
description: Moves a post or page to the Trash If Trash is disabled, the post or page is permanently deleted.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. Post ID. Default is the ID of the global `$post` if `EMPTY_TRASH_DAYS` equals true.

returnDescription: Post data on success, false or null on failure.

name: wp_trash_post_comments
description: Moves comments for a post to the Trash.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Defaults to global $post.

returnDescription: False on failure.

name: wp_trigger_error
description: Generates a user-level error/warning/notice/deprecation message. Generates the message when `WP_DEBUG` is true.

params.name: function_name
params.description: The function that triggered the error.

params.name: message
params.description: The message explaining the error. The message can contain allowed HTML 'a' (with href), 'code', 'br', 'em', and 'strong' tags and http or https protocols. If it contains other HTML tags or protocols, the message should be escaped before passing to this function to avoid being stripped {@see wp_kses()}.

params.name: error_level
params.description: Optional. The designated error type for this error. Only works with E_USER family of constants. Default E_USER_NOTICE.

name: wp_trim_excerpt
description: Generates an excerpt from the content, if needed. Returns a maximum of 55 words with an ellipsis appended if necessary. The 55-word limit can be modified by plugins/themes using the {@see 'excerpt_length'} filter The ' […]' string can be modified by plugins/themes using the {@see 'excerpt_more'} filter

params.name: text
params.description: Optional. The excerpt. If set to empty, an excerpt is generated.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. WP_Post instance or Post ID/object. Default null.

returnDescription: The excerpt.

name: wp_trim_words
description: Trims text to a certain number of words. This function is localized. For languages that count 'words' by the individual character (such as East Asian languages), the $num_words argument will apply to the number of individual characters.

params.name: text
params.description: Text to trim.

params.name: num_words
params.description: Number of words. Default 55.

params.name: more
params.description: Optional. What to append if $text needs to be trimmed. Default '…'.

returnDescription: Trimmed text.

name: wp_trusted_keys
description: Retrieves the list of signing keys trusted by WordPress.
returnDescription: Array of base64-encoded signing keys.

name: wp_typography_get_css_variable_inline_style
description: Generates an inline style for a typography feature e.g. text decoration, text transform, and font style.

params.name: attributes
params.description: Block's attributes.

params.name: feature
params.description: Key for the feature within the typography styles.

params.name: css_property
params.description: Slug for the CSS property the inline style sets.

returnDescription: CSS inline style.

name: wp_typography_get_preset_inline_style_value
description: Generates an inline style value for a typography feature e.g. text decoration, text transform, and font style. Note: This function is for backwards compatibility. * It is necessary to parse older blocks whose typography styles contain presets. * It mostly replaces the deprecated `wp_typography_get_css_variable_inline_style()`, but skips compiling a CSS declaration as the style engine takes over this role.

params.name: style_value
params.description: A raw style value for a single typography feature from a block's style attribute.

params.name: css_property
params.description: Slug for the CSS property the inline style sets.

returnDescription: A CSS inline style value.

name: wp_underscore_audio_template
description: Outputs the markup for an audio tag to be used in an Underscore template when data.model is passed.

name: wp_underscore_playlist_templates
description: Outputs the templates used by playlists.

name: wp_underscore_video_template
description: Outputs the markup for a video tag to be used in an Underscore template when data.model is passed.

name: wp_uninitialize_site
description: Runs the uninitialization routine for a given site. This process includes dropping the site's database tables and deleting its uploads directory.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Site ID or object.

returnDescription: True on success, or error object on failure.

name: wp_unique_filename
description: Gets a filename that is sanitized and unique for the given directory. If the filename is not unique, then a number will be added to the filename before the extension, and will continue adding numbers until the filename is unique. The callback function allows the caller to use their own method to create unique file names. If defined, the callback should take three arguments: - directory, base filename, and extension - and return a unique filename.

params.name: dir
params.description: Directory.

params.name: filename
params.description: File name.

params.name: unique_filename_callback
params.description: Callback. Default null.

returnDescription: New filename, if given wasn't unique.

name: wp_unique_id
description: Gets unique ID. This is a PHP implementation of Underscore's uniqueId method. A static variable contains an integer that is incremented with each call. This number is returned with the optional prefix. As such the returned value is not universally unique, but it is unique across the life of the PHP process.

params.name: prefix
params.description: Prefix for the returned ID.

returnDescription: Unique ID.

name: wp_unique_id_from_values
description: Generates a unique ID based on the structure and values of a given array. This function serializes the array into a JSON string and generates a hash that serves as a unique identifier. Optionally, a prefix can be added to the generated ID for context or categorization.

params.name: data
params.description: The input array to generate an ID from.

params.name: prefix
params.description: Optional. A prefix to prepend to the generated ID. Default empty string.

returnDescription: The generated unique ID for the array.

name: wp_unique_post_slug
description: Computes a unique slug for the post, when given the desired slug and some post details.

params.name: slug
params.description: The desired slug (post_name).

params.name: post_id
params.description: Post ID.

params.name: post_status
params.description: No uniqueness checks are made if the post is still draft or pending.

params.name: post_type
params.description: Post type.

params.name: post_parent
params.description: Post parent ID.

returnDescription: Unique slug for the post, based on $post_name (with a -1, -2, etc. suffix)

name: wp_unique_prefixed_id
description: Generates an incremental ID that is independent per each different prefix. It is similar to `wp_unique_id`, but each prefix has its own internal ID counter to make each prefix independent from each other. The ID starts at 1 and increments on each call. The returned value is not universally unique, but it is unique across the life of the PHP process and it's stable per prefix.

params.name: prefix
params.description: Optional. Prefix for the returned ID. Default empty string.

returnDescription: Incremental ID per prefix.

name: wp_unique_term_slug
description: Makes term slug unique, if it isn't already. The `$slug` has to be unique global to every taxonomy, meaning that one taxonomy term can't have a matching slug with another taxonomy term. Each slug has to be globally unique for every taxonomy. The way this works is that if the taxonomy that the term belongs to is hierarchical and has a parent, it will append that parent to the $slug. If that still doesn't return a unique slug, then it tries to append a number until it finds a number that is truly unique. The only purpose for `$term` is for appending a parent, if one exists.

params.name: slug
params.description: The string that will be tried for a unique slug.

params.name: term
params.description: The term object that the `$slug` will belong to.

returnDescription: Will return a true unique slug.

name: wp_unregister_ability
description: Unregisters an ability from the Abilities API. Removes a previously registered ability from the global registry. Use this to disable abilities provided by other plugins or when an ability is no longer needed. Can be called at any time after the ability has been registered. Example: if ( wp_has_ability( 'other-plugin/some-ability' ) ) { wp_unregister_ability( 'other-plugin/some-ability' ); }

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the ability to unregister, including namespace prefix (e.g., 'my-plugin/my-ability').

returnDescription: The unregistered ability instance on success, `null` on failure.

name: wp_unregister_ability_category
description: Unregisters an ability category. Removes a previously registered ability category from the global registry. Use this to disable ability categories that are no longer needed. Can be called at any time after the ability category has been registered. Example: if ( wp_has_ability_category( 'deprecated-category' ) ) { wp_unregister_ability_category( 'deprecated-category' ); }

params.name: slug
params.description: The slug of the ability category to unregister.

returnDescription: The unregistered ability category instance on success, `null` on failure.

name: wp_unregister_font_collection
description: Unregisters a font collection from the Font Library.

params.name: slug
params.description: Font collection slug.

returnDescription: True if the font collection was unregistered successfully, else false.

name: wp_unregister_GLOBALS
description: Turn register globals off.

name: wp_unregister_sidebar_widget
description: Remove widget from sidebar.

params.name: id
params.description: Widget ID.

name: wp_unregister_widget_control
description: Removes control callback for widget.

params.name: id
params.description: Widget ID.

name: wp_unschedule_event
description: Unschedules a previously scheduled event. The `$timestamp` and `$hook` parameters are required so that the event can be identified.

params.name: timestamp
params.description: Unix timestamp (UTC) of the event.

params.name: hook
params.description: Action hook of the event.

params.name: args
params.description: Optional. Array containing each separate argument to pass to the hook's callback function. Although not passed to a callback, these arguments are used to uniquely identify the event, so they should be the same as those used when originally scheduling the event. Default empty array.

returnDescription: True if event successfully unscheduled. False or WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_unschedule_hook
description: Unschedules all events attached to the hook. Can be useful for plugins when deactivating to clean up the cron queue. Warning: This function may return boolean false, but may also return a non-boolean value which evaluates to false. For information about casting to booleans see the {@link https://www.php.net/manual/en/language.types.boolean.php PHP documentation}. Use the `===` operator for testing the return value of this function.

params.name: hook
params.description: Action hook, the execution of which will be unscheduled.

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a WP_Error on failure. Default false.

returnDescription: On success an integer indicating number of events unscheduled (0 indicates no events were registered on the hook), false or WP_Error if unscheduling fails.

name: wp_unslash
description: Removes slashes from a string or recursively removes slashes from strings within an array. This should be used to remove slashes from data passed to core API that expects data to be unslashed.

params.name: value
params.description: String or array of data to unslash.

returnDescription: Unslashed `$value`, in the same type as supplied.

name: wp_unspam_comment
description: Removes a comment from the Spam.

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Comment ID or WP_Comment object.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_untrash_comment
description: Removes a comment from the Trash

params.name: comment_id
params.description: Comment ID or WP_Comment object.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_untrash_post
description: Restores a post from the Trash.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Optional. Post ID. Default is the ID of the global `$post`.

returnDescription: Post data on success, false or null on failure.

name: wp_untrash_post_comments
description: Restores comments for a post from the Trash.

params.name: post
params.description: Optional. Post ID or post object. Defaults to global $post.

name: wp_untrash_post_set_previous_status
description: Filters callback which sets the status of an untrashed post to its previous status. This can be used as a callback on the `wp_untrash_post_status` filter.

params.name: new_status
params.description: The new status of the post being restored.

params.name: post_id
params.description: The ID of the post being restored.

params.name: previous_status
params.description: The status of the post at the point where it was trashed.

returnDescription: The new status of the post.

name: wp_update_attachment_metadata
description: Updates metadata for an attachment.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: Attachment post ID.

params.name: data
params.description: Attachment meta data.

returnDescription: Whether the metadata was successfully updated. True on success, the Meta ID if the key didn't exist. False if $post is invalid, on failure, or if $data is the same as the existing metadata.

name: wp_update_blog_public_option_on_site_update
description: Updates the `blog_public` option for a given site ID.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Site ID.

params.name: is_public
params.description: Whether the site is public. A numeric string, for compatibility reasons. Accepts '1' or '0'.

name: wp_update_category
description: Aliases wp_insert_category() with minimal args. If you want to update only some fields of an existing category, call this function with only the new values set inside $catarr.

params.name: catarr
params.description: The 'cat_ID' value is required. All other keys are optional.

returnDescription: The ID number of the new or updated Category on success. Zero or FALSE on failure.

name: wp_update_comment
description: Updates an existing comment in the database. Filters the comment and makes sure certain fields are valid before updating.

params.name: commentarr
params.description: Contains information on the comment.

params.name: wp_error
params.description: Optional. Whether to return a WP_Error on failure. Default false.

returnDescription: The value 1 if the comment was updated, 0 if not updated. False or a WP_Error object on failure.

name: wp_update_comment_count
description: Updates the comment count for post(s). When $do_deferred is false (is by default) and the comments have been set to be deferred, the post_id will be added to a queue, which will be updated at a later date and only updated once per post ID. If the comments have not be set up to be deferred, then the post will be updated. When $do_deferred is set to true, then all previous deferred post IDs will be updated along with the current $post_id.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Post ID.

params.name: do_deferred
params.description: Optional. Whether to process previously deferred post comment counts. Default false.

returnDescription: True on success, false on failure or if post with ID does not exist.

name: wp_update_comment_count_now
description: Updates the comment count for the post.

params.name: post_id
params.description: Post ID

returnDescription: True on success, false if the post does not exist.

name: wp_update_core
description: This was once used to kick-off the Core Updater. Deprecated in favor of instantiating a Core_Upgrader instance directly, and calling the 'upgrade' method.

name: wp_update_custom_css_post
description: Updates the `custom_css` post for a given theme. Inserts a `custom_css` post when one doesn't yet exist.

params.name: css
params.description: CSS, stored in `post_content`.

params.name: args
params.description: { Args. @type string $preprocessed Optional. Pre-processed CSS, stored in `post_content_filtered`. Normally empty string. @type string $stylesheet Optional. Stylesheet (child theme) to update. Defaults to active theme/stylesheet. }

returnDescription: Post on success, error on failure.

name: wp_update_https_detection_errors
description: Runs a remote HTTPS request to detect whether HTTPS supported, and stores potential errors. This internal function is called by a regular Cron hook to ensure HTTPS support is detected and maintained.

name: wp_update_https_migration_required
description: Updates the 'https_migration_required' option if needed when the given URL has been updated from HTTP to HTTPS. If this is a fresh site, a migration will not be required, so the option will be set as `false`. This is hooked into the {@see 'update_option_home'} action.

params.name: old_url
params.description: Previous value of the URL option.

params.name: new_url
params.description: New value of the URL option.

name: wp_update_image_subsizes
description: If any of the currently registered image sub-sizes are missing, create them and update the image meta data.

params.name: attachment_id
params.description: The image attachment post ID.

returnDescription: The updated image meta data array or WP_Error object if both the image meta and the attached file are missing.

name: wp_update_link
description: Updates a link in the database.

params.name: linkdata
params.description: Link data to update. See wp_insert_link() for accepted arguments.

returnDescription: Value 0 or WP_Error on failure. The updated link ID on success.

name: wp_update_nav_menu_item
description: Saves the properties of a menu item or create a new one. The menu-item-title, menu-item-description and menu-item-attr-title are expected to be pre-slashed since they are passed directly to APIs that expect slashed data.

params.name: menu_id
params.description: The ID of the menu. If 0, makes the menu item a draft orphan.

params.name: menu_item_db_id
params.description: The ID of the menu item. If 0, creates a new menu item.

params.name: menu_item_data
params.description: The menu item's data.

returnDescription: The menu item's database ID or WP_Error object on failure.

name: wp_update_nav_menu_object
description: Saves the properties of a menu or create a new menu with those properties. Note that `$menu_data` is expected to be pre-slashed.

params.name: menu_id
params.description: The ID of the menu or "0" to create a new menu.

params.name: menu_data
params.description: The array of menu data.

returnDescription: Menu ID on success, WP_Error object on failure.

name: wp_update_network_counts
description: Updates the network-wide counts for the current network.

params.name: network_id
params.description: ID of the network. Default is the current network.

name: wp_update_network_site_counts
description: Updates the network-wide site count.

params.name: network_id
params.description: ID of the network. Default is the current network.

name: wp_update_network_user_counts
description: Updates the network-wide user count.

params.name: network_id
params.description: ID of the network. Default is the current network.

name: wp_update_php_annotation
description: Prints the default annotation for the web host altering the "Update PHP" page URL. This function is to be used after {@see wp_get_update_php_url()} to display a consistent annotation if the web host has altered the default "Update PHP" page URL.

params.name: before
params.description: Markup to output before the annotation. Default ` `.

params.name: after
params.description: Markup to output after the annotation. Default ` `.

params.name: display
params.description: Whether to echo or return the markup. Default `true` for echo.

name: wp_update_plugin
description: This was once used to kick-off the Plugin Updater. Deprecated in favor of instantiating a Plugin_Upgrader instance directly, and calling the 'upgrade' method. Unused since 2.8.0.

name: wp_update_plugins
description: Checks for available updates to plugins based on the latest versions hosted on WordPress.org. Despite its name this function does not actually perform any updates, it only checks for available updates. A list of all plugins installed is sent to api.wordpress.org, along with the site locale. Checks against the WordPress server at api.wordpress.org. Will only check if WordPress isn't installing.

params.name: extra_stats
params.description: Extra statistics to report to the WordPress.org API.

name: wp_update_post
description: Updates a post with new post data. The date does not have to be set for drafts. You can set the date and it will not be overridden.

params.name: postarr
params.description: Optional. Post data. Arrays are expected to be escaped, objects are not. See wp_insert_post() for accepted arguments. Default array.

returnDescription: The post ID on success. The value 0 or WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_update_site
description: Updates a site in the database.

params.name: site_id
params.description: ID of the site that should be updated.

params.name: data
params.description: Site data to update. See {@see wp_insert_site()} for the list of supported keys.

returnDescription: The updated site's ID on success, or error object on failure.

name: wp_update_term
description: Updates term based on arguments provided. The `$args` will indiscriminately override all values with the same field name. Care must be taken to not override important information need to update or update will fail (or perhaps create a new term, neither would be acceptable). Defaults will set 'alias_of', 'description', 'parent', and 'slug' if not defined in `$args` already. 'alias_of' will create a term group, if it doesn't already exist, and update it for the `$term`. If the 'slug' argument in `$args` is missing, then the 'name' will be used. If you set 'slug' and it isn't unique, then a WP_Error is returned. If you don't pass any slug, then a unique one will be created.

params.name: term_id
params.description: The ID of the term.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: The taxonomy of the term.

params.name: args
params.description: { Optional. Array of arguments for updating a term. @type string $alias_of Slug of the term to make this term an alias of. Default empty string. Accepts a term slug. @type string $description The term description. Default empty string. @type int $parent The id of the parent term. Default 0. @type string $slug The term slug to use. Default empty string. }

returnDescription: An array containing the `term_id` and `term_taxonomy_id`, WP_Error otherwise.

name: wp_update_term_count
description: Updates the amount of terms in taxonomy. If there is a taxonomy callback applied, then it will be called for updating the count. The default action is to count what the amount of terms have the relationship of term ID. Once that is done, then update the database.

params.name: terms
params.description: The term_taxonomy_id of the terms.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: The context of the term.

params.name: do_deferred
params.description: Whether to flush the deferred term counts too. Default false.

returnDescription: If no terms will return false, and if successful will return true.

name: wp_update_term_count_now
description: Performs term count update immediately.

params.name: terms
params.description: The term_taxonomy_id of terms to update.

params.name: taxonomy
params.description: The context of the term.

returnDescription: Always true when complete.

name: wp_update_theme
description: This was once used to kick-off the Theme Updater. Deprecated in favor of instantiating a Theme_Upgrader instance directly, and calling the 'upgrade' method. Unused since 2.8.0.

name: wp_update_themes
description: Checks for available updates to themes based on the latest versions hosted on WordPress.org. Despite its name this function does not actually perform any updates, it only checks for available updates. A list of all themes installed is sent to api.wordpress.org, along with the site locale. Checks against the WordPress server at api.wordpress.org. Will only check if WordPress isn't installing.

params.name: extra_stats
params.description: Extra statistics to report to the WordPress.org API.

name: wp_update_urls_to_https
description: Update the 'home' and 'siteurl' option to use the HTTPS variant of their URL. If this update does not result in WordPress recognizing that the site is now using HTTPS (e.g. due to constants overriding the URLs used), the changes will be reverted. In such a case the function will return false.
returnDescription: True on success, false on failure.

name: wp_update_user
description: Updates a user in the database. It is possible to update a user's password by specifying the 'user_pass' value in the $userdata parameter array. If current user's password is being updated, then the cookies will be cleared.

params.name: userdata
params.description: An array of user data or a user object of type stdClass or WP_User.

returnDescription: The updated user's ID or a WP_Error object if the user could not be updated.

name: wp_update_user_counts
description: Updates the total count of users on the site.

params.name: network_id
params.description: ID of the network. Defaults to the current network.

returnDescription: Whether the update was successful.

name: wp_upgrade
description: Runs WordPress Upgrade functions. Upgrades the database if needed during a site update.

name: wp_upload_bits
description: Creates a file in the upload folder with given content. If there is an error, then the key 'error' will exist with the error message. If success, then the key 'file' will have the unique file path, the 'url' key will have the link to the new file. and the 'error' key will be set to false. This function will not move an uploaded file to the upload folder. It will create a new file with the content in $bits parameter. If you move the upload file, read the content of the uploaded file, and then you can give the filename and content to this function, which will add it to the upload folder. The permissions will be set on the new file automatically by this function.

params.name: name
params.description: Filename.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Never used. Set to null.

params.name: bits
params.description: File content

params.name: time
params.description: Optional. Time formatted in 'yyyy/mm'. Default null.

returnDescription: { Information about the newly-uploaded file. @type string $file Filename of the newly-uploaded file. @type string $url URL of the uploaded file. @type string $type File type. @type string|false $error Error message, if there has been an error. }

name: wp_upload_dir
description: Returns an array containing the current upload directory's path and URL. Checks the 'upload_path' option, which should be from the web root folder, and if it isn't empty it will be used. If it is empty, then the path will be 'WP_CONTENT_DIR/uploads'. If the 'UPLOADS' constant is defined, then it will override the 'upload_path' option and 'WP_CONTENT_DIR/uploads' path. The upload URL path is set either by the 'upload_url_path' option or by using the 'WP_CONTENT_URL' constant and appending '/uploads' to the path. If the 'uploads_use_yearmonth_folders' is set to true (checkbox if checked in the administration settings panel), then the time will be used. The format will be year first and then month. If the path couldn't be created, then an error will be returned with the key 'error' containing the error message. The error suggests that the parent directory is not writable by the server.

params.name: time
params.description: Optional. Time formatted in 'yyyy/mm'. Default null.

params.name: create_dir
params.description: Optional. Whether to check and create the uploads directory. Default true for backward compatibility.

params.name: refresh_cache
params.description: Optional. Whether to refresh the cache. Default false.

returnDescription: { Array of information about the upload directory. @type string $path Base directory and subdirectory or full path to upload directory. @type string $url Base URL and subdirectory or absolute URL to upload directory. @type string $subdir Subdirectory if uploads use year/month folders option is on. @type string $basedir Path without subdir. @type string $baseurl URL path without subdir. @type string|false $error False or error message. }

name: wp_use_widgets_block_editor
description: Determines whether or not to use the block editor to manage widgets. Defaults to true unless a theme has removed support for widgets-block-editor or a plugin has filtered the return value of this function.
returnDescription: Whether to use the block editor to manage widgets.

name: wp_user_personal_data_exporter
description: Finds and exports personal data associated with an email address from the user and user_meta table.

params.name: email_address
params.description: The user's email address.

returnDescription: { An array of personal data. @type array[] $data An array of personal data arrays. @type bool $done Whether the exporter is finished. }

name: wp_user_request_action_description
description: Gets action description from the name and return a string.

params.name: action_name
params.description: Action name of the request.

returnDescription: Human readable action name.

name: wp_user_settings
description: Saves and restores user interface settings stored in a cookie. Checks if the current user-settings cookie is updated and stores it. When no cookie exists (different browser used), adds the last saved cookie restoring the settings.

name: wp_using_ext_object_cache
description: Toggles `$_wp_using_ext_object_cache` on and off without directly touching global.

params.name: using
params.description: Whether external object cache is being used.

returnDescription: The current 'using' setting.

name: wp_using_themes
description: Determines whether the current request should use themes.
returnDescription: True if themes should be used, false otherwise.

name: wp_validate_application_password
description: Validates the application password credentials passed via Basic Authentication.

params.name: input_user
params.description: User ID if one has been determined, false otherwise.

returnDescription: The authenticated user ID if successful, false otherwise.

name: wp_validate_auth_cookie
description: Validates authentication cookie. The checks include making sure that the authentication cookie is set and pulling in the contents (if $cookie is not used). Makes sure the cookie is not expired. Verifies the hash in cookie is what is should be and compares the two.

params.name: cookie
params.description: Optional. If used, will validate contents instead of cookie's.

params.name: scheme
params.description: Optional. The cookie scheme to use: 'auth', 'secure_auth', or 'logged_in'. Note: This does *not* default to 'auth' like other cookie functions.

returnDescription: User ID if valid cookie, false if invalid.

name: wp_validate_boolean
description: Filters/validates a variable as a boolean. Alternative to `filter_var( $value, FILTER_VALIDATE_BOOLEAN )`.

params.name: value
params.description: Boolean value to validate.

returnDescription: Whether the value is validated.

name: wp_validate_logged_in_cookie
description: Validates the logged-in cookie. Checks the logged-in cookie if the previous auth cookie could not be validated and parsed. This is a callback for the {@see 'determine_current_user'} filter, rather than API.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The user ID (or false) as received from the `determine_current_user` filter.

returnDescription: User ID if validated, false otherwise. If a user ID from an earlier filter callback is received, that value is returned.

name: wp_validate_redirect
description: Validates a URL for use in a redirect. Checks whether the $location is using an allowed host, if it has an absolute path. A plugin can therefore set or remove allowed host(s) to or from the list. If the host is not allowed, then the redirect is to $fallback_url supplied.

params.name: location
params.description: The redirect to validate.

params.name: fallback_url
params.description: The value to return if $location is not allowed.

returnDescription: Redirect-sanitized URL.

name: wp_validate_site_data
description: Validates data for a site prior to inserting or updating in the database.

params.name: errors
params.description: Error object, passed by reference. Will contain validation errors if any occurred.

params.name: data
params.description: Associative array of complete site data. See {@see wp_insert_site()} for the included data.

params.name: old_site
params.description: The old site object if the data belongs to a site being updated, or null if it is a new site being inserted.

name: wp_validate_user_request_key
description: Validates a user request by comparing the key with the request's key.

params.name: request_id
params.description: ID of the request being confirmed.

params.name: key
params.description: Provided key to validate.

returnDescription: True on success, WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_verify_fast_hash
description: Checks whether a plaintext message matches the hashed value. Used to verify values hashed via wp_fast_hash(). The function uses Sodium to hash the message and compare it to the hashed value. If the hash is not a generic hash, the hash is treated as a phpass portable hash in order to provide backward compatibility for passwords and security keys which were hashed using phpass prior to WordPress 6.8.0.

params.name: message
params.description: The plaintext message.

params.name: hash
params.description: Hash of the message to check against.

returnDescription: Whether the message matches the hashed message.

name: wp_verify_nonce
description: Verifies that a correct security nonce was used with time limit. A nonce is valid for between 12 and 24 hours (by default).

params.name: nonce
params.description: Nonce value that was used for verification, usually via a form field.

params.name: action
params.description: Should give context to what is taking place and be the same when nonce was created.

returnDescription: 1 if the nonce is valid and generated between 0-12 hours ago, 2 if the nonce is valid and generated between 12-24 hours ago. False if the nonce is invalid.

name: wp_version_check
description: Checks WordPress version against the newest version. The WordPress version, PHP version, and locale is sent to api.wordpress.org. Checks against the WordPress server at api.wordpress.org. Will only check if WordPress isn't installing.

params.name: extra_stats
params.description: Extra statistics to report to the WordPress.org API.

name: wp_video_shortcode
description: Builds the Video shortcode output. This implements the functionality of the Video Shortcode for displaying WordPress mp4s in a post.

params.name: attr
params.description: { Attributes of the shortcode. @type string $src URL to the source of the video file. Default empty. @type int $height Height of the video embed in pixels. Default 360. @type int $width Width of the video embed in pixels. Default $content_width or 640. @type string $poster The 'poster' attribute for the ` ` element. Default empty. @type string $loop The 'loop' attribute for the ` ` element. Default empty. @type string $autoplay The 'autoplay' attribute for the ` ` element. Default empty. @type string $muted The 'muted' attribute for the ` ` element. Default false. @type string $preload The 'preload' attribute for the ` ` element. Default 'metadata'. @type string $class The 'class' attribute for the ` ` element. Default 'wp-video-shortcode'. }

params.name: content
params.description: Shortcode content.

returnDescription: HTML content to display video.

name: wp_welcome_panel
description: Displays a welcome panel to introduce users to WordPress.

name: wp_widget_control
description: Meta widget used to display the control form for a widget. Called from dynamic_sidebar().

name: wp_widget_description
description: Retrieves description for widget. When registering widgets, the options can also include 'description' that describes the widget for display on the widget administration panel or in the theme.

params.name: id
params.description: Widget ID.

returnDescription: Widget description, if available.

name: wp_widget_rss_form
description: Displays RSS widget options form. The options for what fields are displayed for the RSS form are all booleans and are as follows: 'url', 'title', 'items', 'show_summary', 'show_author', 'show_date'.

params.name: args
params.description: Values for input fields.

params.name: inputs
params.description: Override default display options.

name: wp_widget_rss_output
description: Displays the RSS entries in a list.

params.name: rss
params.description: RSS url.

params.name: args
params.description: Widget arguments.

name: wp_widget_rss_process
description: Processes RSS feed widget data and optionally retrieve feed items. The feed widget can not have more than 20 items or it will reset back to the default, which is 10. The resulting array has the feed title, feed url, feed link (from channel), feed items, error (if any), and whether to show summary, author, and date. All respectively in the order of the array elements.

params.name: widget_rss
params.description: RSS widget feed data. Expects unescaped data.

params.name: check_feed
params.description: Optional. Whether to check feed for errors. Default true.

name: wp_widgets_access_body_class
description: WordPress function wp_widgets_access_body_class

name: wp_widgets_add_menu
description: Appends the Widgets menu to the themes main menu.

name: wp_widgets_init
description: Registers all of the default WordPress widgets on startup. Calls {@see 'widgets_init'} action after all of the WordPress widgets have been registered.

name: wp_write_post
description: Creates a new post from the "Write Post" form using `$_POST` information.
returnDescription: Post ID on success, WP_Error on failure.

name: wp_zip_file_is_valid
description: Determines whether the given file is a valid ZIP file. This function does not test to ensure that a file exists. Non-existent files are not valid ZIPs, so those will also return false.

params.name: file
params.description: Full path to the ZIP file.

returnDescription: Whether the file is a valid ZIP file.

name: wpautop
description: Replaces double line breaks with paragraph elements. A group of regex replaces used to identify text formatted with newlines and replace double line breaks with HTML paragraph tags. The remaining line breaks after conversion become ` ` tags, unless `$br` is set to '0' or 'false'.

params.name: text
params.description: The text which has to be formatted.

params.name: br
params.description: Optional. If set, this will convert all remaining line breaks after paragraphing. Line breaks within ` `, ` `, and ` ` tags are not affected. Default true.

returnDescription: Text which has been converted into correct paragraph tags.

name: wpmu_activate_signup
description: Activates a signup. Hook to {@see 'wpmu_activate_user'} or {@see 'wpmu_activate_blog'} for events that should happen only when users or sites are self-created (since those actions are not called when users and sites are created by a Super Admin).

params.name: key
params.description: The activation key provided to the user.

returnDescription: An array containing information about the activated user and/or blog.

name: wpmu_activate_stylesheet
description: Loads styles specific to this page.

name: wpmu_admin_do_redirect
description: Redirect a user based on $_GET or $_POST arguments. The function looks for redirect arguments in the following order: 1) $_GET['ref'] 2) $_POST['ref'] 3) $_SERVER['HTTP_REFERER'] 4) $_GET['redirect'] 5) $_POST['redirect'] 6) $url

params.name: url
params.description: Optional. Redirect URL. Default empty.

name: wpmu_admin_redirect_add_updated_param
description: Adds an 'updated=true' argument to a URL.

params.name: url
params.description: Optional. Redirect URL. Default empty.

name: wpmu_checkAvailableSpace
description: Determines if the available space defined by the admin has been exceeded by the user.

name: wpmu_create_blog
description: Creates a site. This function runs when a user self-registers a new site as well as when a Super Admin creates a new site. Hook to {@see 'wpmu_new_blog'} for events that should affect all new sites. On subdirectory installations, $domain is the same as the main site's domain, and the path is the subdirectory name (eg 'example.com' and '/blog1/'). On subdomain installations, $domain is the new subdomain + root domain (eg 'blog1.example.com'), and $path is '/'.

params.name: domain
params.description: The new site's domain.

params.name: path
params.description: The new site's path.

params.name: title
params.description: The new site's title.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The user ID of the new site's admin.

params.name: options
params.description: Optional. Array of key=>value pairs used to set initial site options. If valid status keys are included ('public', 'archived', 'mature', 'spam', 'deleted', or 'lang_id') the given site status(es) will be updated. Otherwise, keys and values will be used to set options for the new site. Default empty array.

returnDescription: Returns WP_Error object on failure, the new site ID on success.

name: wpmu_create_user
description: Creates a user. This function runs when a user self-registers as well as when a Super Admin creates a new user. Hook to {@see 'wpmu_new_user'} for events that should affect all new users, but only on Multisite (otherwise use {@see 'user_register'}).

params.name: user_name
params.description: The new user's login name.

params.name: password
params.description: The new user's password.

params.name: email
params.description: The new user's email address.

returnDescription: Returns false on failure, or int $user_id on success.

name: wpmu_current_site
description: This deprecated function managed much of the site and network loading in multisite. The current bootstrap code is now responsible for parsing the site and network load as well as setting the global $current_site object.

name: wpmu_delete_blog
description: Deletes a site.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Site ID.

params.name: drop
params.description: True if site's database tables should be dropped. Default false.

name: wpmu_delete_user
description: Deletes a user and all of their posts from the network. This function: - Deletes all posts (of all post types) authored by the user on all sites on the network - Deletes all links owned by the user on all sites on the network - Removes the user from all sites on the network - Deletes the user from the database

params.name: id
params.description: The user ID.

returnDescription: True if the user was deleted, false otherwise.

name: wpmu_get_blog_allowedthemes
description: Deprecated functionality for getting themes allowed on a specific site.

name: wpmu_log_new_registrations
description: Logs the user email, IP, and registration date of a new site.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: The new site's object or ID.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID, or array of arguments including 'user_id'.

name: wpmu_menu
description: Outputs the WPMU menu.

name: wpmu_new_site_admin_notification
description: Notifies the Multisite network administrator that a new site was created. Filter {@see 'send_new_site_email'} to disable or bypass. Filter {@see 'new_site_email'} to filter the contents.

params.name: site_id
params.description: Site ID of the new site.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID of the administrator of the new site.

returnDescription: Whether the email notification was sent.

name: wpmu_signup_blog
description: Records site signup information for future activation.

params.name: domain
params.description: The requested domain.

params.name: path
params.description: The requested path.

params.name: title
params.description: The requested site title.

params.name: user
params.description: The user's requested login name.

params.name: user_email
params.description: The user's email address.

params.name: meta
params.description: Optional. Signup meta data. By default, contains the requested privacy setting and lang_id.

name: wpmu_signup_blog_notification
description: Sends a confirmation request email to a user when they sign up for a new site. The new site will not become active until the confirmation link is clicked. This is the notification function used when site registration is enabled. Filter {@see 'wpmu_signup_blog_notification'} to bypass this function or replace it with your own notification behavior. Filter {@see 'wpmu_signup_blog_notification_email'} and {@see 'wpmu_signup_blog_notification_subject'} to change the content and subject line of the email sent to newly registered users.

params.name: domain
params.description: The new blog domain.

params.name: path
