params.description: The new blog path.

params.name: title
params.description: The site title.

params.name: user_login
params.description: The user's login name.

params.name: user_email
params.description: The user's email address.

params.name: key
params.description: The activation key created in wpmu_signup_blog().

params.name: meta
params.description: Optional. Signup meta data. By default, contains the requested privacy setting and lang_id.

name: wpmu_signup_stylesheet
description: Prints styles for front-end Multisite Sign-up pages.

name: wpmu_signup_user
description: Records user signup information for future activation. This function is used when user registration is open but new site registration is not.

params.name: user
params.description: The user's requested login name.

params.name: user_email
params.description: The user's email address.

params.name: meta
params.description: Optional. Signup meta data. Default empty array.

name: wpmu_signup_user_notification
description: Sends a confirmation request email to a user when they sign up for a new user account (without signing up for a site at the same time). The user account will not become active until the confirmation link is clicked. This is the notification function used when no new site has been requested. Filter {@see 'wpmu_signup_user_notification'} to bypass this function or replace it with your own notification behavior. Filter {@see 'wpmu_signup_user_notification_email'} and {@see 'wpmu_signup_user_notification_subject'} to change the content and subject line of the email sent to newly registered users.

params.name: user_login
params.description: The user's login name.

params.name: user_email
params.description: The user's email address.

params.name: key
params.description: The activation key created in wpmu_signup_user()

params.name: meta
params.description: Optional. Signup meta data. Default empty array.

name: wpmu_update_blogs_date
description: Updates the last_updated field for the current site.

name: wpmu_validate_blog_signup
description: Processes new site registrations. Checks the data provided by the user during blog signup. Verifies the validity and uniqueness of blog paths and domains. This function prevents the current user from registering a new site with a blogname equivalent to another user's login name. Passing the $user parameter to the function, where $user is the other user, is effectively an override of this limitation. Filter {@see 'wpmu_validate_blog_signup'} if you want to modify the way that WordPress validates new site signups.

params.name: blogname
params.description: The site name provided by the user. Must be unique.

params.name: blog_title
params.description: The site title provided by the user.

params.name: user
params.description: Optional. The user object to check against the new site name. Default empty string.

returnDescription: { Array of domain, path, site name, site title, user and error messages. @type string $domain Domain for the site. @type string $path Path for the site. Used in subdirectory installations. @type string $blogname The unique site name (slug). @type string $blog_title Blog title. @type string|WP_User $user By default, an empty string. A user object if provided. @type WP_Error $errors WP_Error containing any errors found. }

name: wpmu_validate_user_signup
description: Sanitizes and validates data required for a user sign-up. Verifies the validity and uniqueness of user names and user email addresses, and checks email addresses against allowed and disallowed domains provided by administrators. The {@see 'wpmu_validate_user_signup'} hook provides an easy way to modify the sign-up process. The value $result, which is passed to the hook, contains both the user-provided info and the error messages created by the function. {@see 'wpmu_validate_user_signup'} allows you to process the data in any way you'd like, and unset the relevant errors if necessary.

params.name: user_name
params.description: The login name provided by the user.

params.name: user_email
params.description: The email provided by the user.

returnDescription: { The array of user name, email, and the error messages. @type string $user_name Sanitized and unique username. @type string $orig_username Original username. @type string $user_email User email address. @type WP_Error $errors WP_Error object containing any errors found. }

name: wpmu_welcome_notification
description: Notifies the site administrator that their site activation was successful. Filter {@see 'wpmu_welcome_notification'} to disable or bypass. Filter {@see 'update_welcome_email'} and {@see 'update_welcome_subject'} to modify the content and subject line of the notification email.

params.name: blog_id
params.description: Site ID.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

params.name: password
params.description: User password, or "N/A" if the user account is not new.

params.name: title
params.description: Site title.

params.name: meta
params.description: Optional. Signup meta data. By default, contains the requested privacy setting and lang_id.

returnDescription: Whether the email notification was sent.

name: wpmu_welcome_user_notification
description: Notifies a user that their account activation has been successful. Filter {@see 'wpmu_welcome_user_notification'} to disable or bypass. Filter {@see 'update_welcome_user_email'} and {@see 'update_welcome_user_subject'} to modify the content and subject line of the notification email.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID.

params.name: password
params.description: User password.

params.name: meta
params.description: Optional. Signup meta data. Default empty array.

name: wptexturize
description: Replaces common plain text characters with formatted entities. Returns given text with transformations of quotes into smart quotes, apostrophes, dashes, ellipses, the trademark symbol, and the multiplication symbol. As an example, 'cause today's effort makes it worth tomorrow's "holiday" ... Becomes: ’cause today’s effort makes it worth tomorrow’s “holiday” … Code within certain HTML blocks are skipped. Do not use this function before the {@see 'init'} action hook; everything will break.

params.name: text
params.description: The text to be formatted.

params.name: reset
params.description: Set to true for unit testing. Translated patterns will reset.

returnDescription: The string replaced with HTML entities.

name: wptexturize_primes
description: Implements a logic tree to determine whether or not "7'." represents seven feet, then converts the special char into either a prime char or a closing quote char.

params.name: haystack
params.description: The plain text to be searched.

params.name: needle
params.description: The character to search for such as ' or ".

params.name: prime
params.description: The prime char to use for replacement.

params.name: open_quote
params.description: The opening quote char. Opening quote replacement must be accomplished already.

params.name: close_quote
params.description: The closing quote char to use for replacement.

returnDescription: The $haystack value after primes and quotes replacements.

name: wpview_media_sandbox_styles
description: Returns the URLs for CSS files used in an iframe-sandbox'd TinyMCE media view.
returnDescription: The relevant CSS file URLs.

name: write_post
description: Calls wp_write_post() and handles the errors.
returnDescription: Post ID on success, void on failure.

name: xfn_check
description: Displays 'checked' checkboxes attribute for XFN microformat options.

params.name: xfn_relationship
params.description: XFN relationship category. Possible values are: 'friendship', 'physical', 'professional', 'geographical', 'family', 'romantic', 'identity'.

params.name: xfn_value
params.description: Optional. The XFN value to mark as checked if it matches the current link's relationship. Default empty string.

params.name: deprecated
params.description: Deprecated. Not used.

name: xmlrpc_getpostcategory
description: Retrieves the post category or categories from XML-RPC XML. If the `category` element is not found in the XML, the default post category from the `$post_default_category` global will be used instead. The return type will then be a string. If the `category` element is found, the return type will be an array.

params.name: content
params.description: XML-RPC XML Request content.

returnDescription: An array of category names or default category name.

name: xmlrpc_getposttitle
description: Retrieves post title from XML-RPC XML. If the `title` element is not found in the XML, the default post title from the `$post_default_title` global will be used instead.

params.name: content
params.description: XML-RPC XML Request content.

returnDescription: Post title.

name: xmlrpc_pingback_error
description: Default filter attached to xmlrpc_pingback_error. Returns a generic pingback error code unless the error code is 48, which reports that the pingback is already registered.

name: xmlrpc_removepostdata
description: XML-RPC XML content without title and category elements.

params.name: content
params.description: XML-RPC XML Request content.

returnDescription: XML-RPC XML Request content without title and category elements.

name: zeroise
description: Add leading zeros when necessary. If you set the threshold to '4' and the number is '10', then you will get back '0010'. If you set the threshold to '4' and the number is '5000', then you will get back '5000'. Uses sprintf to append the amount of zeros based on the $threshold parameter and the size of the number. If the number is large enough, then no zeros will be appended.

params.name: number
params.description: Number to append zeros to if not greater than threshold.

params.name: threshold
params.description: Digit places number needs to be to not have zeros added.

returnDescription: Adds leading zeros to number if needed.
